title: Truly a magical place
text: We stayed at Khama Rhino Sanctuary for one night on our way to Maun and I absolutely loved this place. I really appreciate everything they do and the incredible care they provide for the wildlife. The animals are free to roam for miles and miles and there are so many different species being protected here. The camping spots were huge and offered plenty of space for everyone. The toilets were clean and there was hot water for a quick shower. We went on a jeep drive to watch the sunset and were lucky enough to see so many animals along the way.

title: Great Experience
text: A once in a lifetime experience. A must see destination. We were fortunate enough to spot two white rhinos. Affordable game sighting park.Great guides

title: Beautiful rhinos and many other animals!
text: I joined a group trip to Africa because this was on the itinerary. We saw 11 white rhinos! The guide was informative and it was a great experience seeing so many animals and knowing the rhinos are protected.

title: Good Stopover
text: Nice Campsite for a stop off on the way to Maun. They have a small convenience store on site. The sites were far apart and you could self drive to see the animals

title: Rhino viewing
text: The chance to observe rhino in the wild. Great camping sites and the bungalows are quite good too. We have been here on several occasions and will be back on our next trip.

title: Great location for getting close to lots of animals - just make sure you rug up at night
text: Camped here as part of a group tour. Was great to go for a 4x4 tour in the afternoon and get close up to rhinos, giraffes, zebras, wildebeests and various antelopes. Unfortunately no big cats though. Facilities were fine, but it was absolutely freezing overnight (-5 degrees) so make sure you have a good sleeping bag and warm clothes if you’re camping in winter.

title: Great landscape and wildlife viewing
text: Khama Rhino Sanctuary was the first self-drive safari on our 2-week trip to Botswana. We enjoyed spotting the animals - rhinos, giraffes, antelopes, warthogs and birds. The only downside was that we had hoped we could hire a guide on the spot to accompany us in our car, but were told there was nobody there who could do so in spite of ten or so staff members sitting by the entrance two hours before closing time. ;) We did love the experience though, the landscape and the wildlife viewing were great.

title: Shocking Accommodation at Khama Rhino Sanctuary
text: We stayed in a family chalet. The chalets are horrible. Very dirty, drafty, and no cutlery or towels. Linen is provided, but due to the cold we slept in our own sleeping bags. In addition, for the 3 of us, we had to pay Pula400 as a conservation fee when entering the rhino sanctuary. In my view, it’s not worth it and should be avoided at all costs.

title: Khama camp facilities
text:
The camp sites have no electrical points or dish washing facilities. Ablution very far from the camp site number 2. Ablution was clean, however vary small and crammed.
Location is good, reserve is good, but it needs better facilities.

title: A beautiful Sanctuary that does a great job with our endangered Rhinos.
text: Friendly and efficient staff at the reception. They have a restaurant at the reception. We camped in D1 and D2 . Campsite 9 is close to ablution block. The ablation was clean and had hot water. No electric points at campsite.

title: In need of maintenance and a decent cook!
text:
3rd time staying at Rhino, as it’s ideally located between the border and the Delta.
The chalets are falling apart and in need of a refurbishment.
The ‘restaurant’ never has the full availability of the menu and cooking is slow and not that good.

Still a good opportunity to see game and the park is well tendered.

title: Pretty Refreshing Spot
text:
After so many years of being a regular Khama Rhino visitor, thought I'd finally share my over the years experience.

Normally I do self drives with friends usually on the way to my hometown (Palapye). The staff is always sweet (bring your I.D). Locating the animals is always so easy though at times the Rhinos and Giraffes are difficult to find 🤦. But overall the drive around the sanctuary seeing all the animals is super refreshing.

I've spent a night in one of their A-line chalets before. The rooms were clean, I wasn't really impressed with the cleanliness of the floor of the outdoor shower, the toilet was sorta okay.

As for the toilets in the sanctuary itself for public use during drives, they are not really well monitored but they are okay-ish.

title: Good animal viewing
text: Enjoyed the self game drive, although the morning was hot we easily spotted animals seeking shelter from the sun in some shady tree areas. We were fortunate to spot an abundance of Rhino as well as Zebra, Giraffe, Impala, Waterbuck and various BirdLife. You need an AWD or 4X4 to self drive the sandy roads or book a drive with a guide at reception area.  Ring your ID as they will need it to make copies when registering you. Only thing we spotted that needed attention was the Toilets! Urinals we’re flooded and so was the wash basin. Hopefully that gets sorted out quick.

title: Rhino eyes
text:
the Sanctuary is not one of the top attractions in Botswana,  All is about the Okavango....yet it is worth going to. There is a little gift shops where good stuff costs 1/2 than in Gaborone. And the guides are knowledgeable and clearly familiar with the habits of the animals.
The rhinos are superb and...a bit scary...what if they don't like us? They are big!!!!But they look at you through those small eyes...judge you are not a threat and go back to lie down under the shade of a tree. One can take loads of photos of these big, white creatures. Then there are smaller ones, antelopes, with their swirling horns, and the tall necked giraffes, who look a bit scared...even though they should be accustomed to the inquisitive eyes of people. There is plenty of time to look at the wildlife. What one does not see here is elephant and hippos...but elephants may cross the road at any time in Botswana and hippos are in the Okavango delta. Rhinos are a bit different, with that big heavy head...I wondered about what they think.
WE spent much time simply staring at a rhino family. And as much as I love penguins, I realized that there is something unique in this part of Africa. There are mysterious animals and there is the connection to the ancestral world whence humans came out from

title: Great experience
text:
Big reserve with loads of animals, mainly rhinos, impalas, zebras, birds, gnus. 
Driver was informative and made sure we so as much as we can.

title: Brilliant sightings for rhino
text: Spacious campsites with big trees. Good ablution facilities with hot water. Lovely bird hide and rhino's on the pans. Restaurant is a bit of a let down.

title: Great rhino sightings
text:
After a mix-up in our 3-night reservation for a 2-bedroomed cottage (caused by our Botswana travel agent not by the Park officials) we were accommodated in a chalet normally used by teachers at a facility for wildlife education. This accommodation was very basic but we were most grateful as the park was overflowing with visitors.

Absolutely wonderful sightings of various animals and birds, including 26 white and 1 black rhino on our last afternoon! We were surprised at the size of the park, and it was reassuring to note the presence of many armed army personnel within and on the borders of the reserve protecting its assets.

Definitely a place to be revisited!

title: Wonderful park but accommodation is badly managed
text:
The wildlife in the park was great.

Our family stayed for one night in a chalet. The chalet was old and badly maintained and we had small insects falling onto our bed from the grass roof during the night.

The staff allowed a group of campers to have a loud party and play music in the camp site until late in the evening. We were unable to  listen to any of the night sounds in the bush.

Based on our experience we definitely won’t visit the park again, which is a real pity because it is the only sanctuary for rhinos in Botswana.

title: Schön für eine Nacht mit erstem Game-Drive
text:
Schöner Park, als Selbstfahrer mit 4x4 gut zu erkunden, teils aber etwas Tiefsandig. Mit einem Duster kommt man z.B. nicht weit.
Die Camping-Plätze waren gut gelegen und schön ausgestattet, der Weg zu den 2 Ablutions war jeweils nicht weit. Diese waren aber etwas in die Jahre gekommen und nicht sonderlich gepflegt.

title: Great stay.
text: Camped one night - July 2021. Friendly reception, clean campsite with awesome trees. Birdlife was amazing with nice sightings of variety of animals. Ablutions clean with hot showers. (Shower heads are low and tall people need to bend their knees to get under the shower - a minor hick-up though). Will definitely stay again.

title: Not Easy To Find, But Well Worth The Effort.
text:
If you're interested in the history of Botswana & Bechuanaland, then this quaint little Museum is well worth a visit. We visited while passing through Serowe on our way to the Khama Rhino Sanctuary. It's not very well signposted, but if you take the time to find it, you're in for a fascinating hour or so with a local Guide showing you around, explaining the fascinating history, and, if you're lucky, maybe getting a live rendition of a traditional song thrown in for good measure.
We absolutely loved our time here, and would recommend it to anyone with an interest in the Country's history, or the story of Sir Seretse Khama & Ruth (as depicted in the film "A United Kingdom").
And, just like over the rest of Botswana, the People were wonderful and so welcoming.

title: Savuti campsite in Chobe has a wealth of wildlife in March
text: Savuti camping for 3 nights was the BEST.  We had a pride of lions very happily rest at the edge of our camp on the 2nd last night, and they remained for us to watch for over 2 hours in the morning.  In the afternoon, the same pride were responsible for a "kill" which we were entranced by.  Our guide was first on the scene, and joined by another 6 jeeps once the word got out.  We couldn't fault anything and have memorable video footage.  The better phone/camera you have the happier your memories will be.  Early Kingfisher Safaris made our very long journey from Australia the best trip of a lifetime!

title: Don't get lost!
text: Savuti is almost guaranteed to deliver, when it comes to game drives. In just a few hours we saw hundreds of elephants, thousands of zebra and springbok plus giraffes, buffalo, wildebeest, kudu, etc. as well as a varied birdlife, including vultures feasting on a meal of leftover elephant. That said - considering the exorbitant entrance fees to the park, there should be a little left over to update the signs in the park. Many have, I suspect, been used by elephants etc. to rub itchy spots, the directions on the signs are worn off and impossible to read, or the signs have been tipped over. It is very easy to loose your way and ending up in the wrong place. As for the tar roads leading to the north of Botswana - they are a disgrace. The road from Sehitwa via Shakawe we named Pothole Avenue - the tar is in some places nonexistent, which is almost to be preferred to giant potholes that are almost impossible to avoid. The same goes for the Maun-Nata-Kasane road. The government of Botswana should be ashamed of not maintaining the infrastructure - but maybe the politicians are more interested in short term mining adventures with foreign investors skimming the profits and ruining the roads with heavy trucks. Botswana could learn a lot from Namibia - if you want to experience Savuti, go via Caprivi if possible. Both Maun and Kasane have a lot of lovely, expensive lodges, campsites and also a growing amount of private guesthouses. The towns is growing, but apparently without much thought to the main source of income: Tourism. In the evening, the old centre of Kasane is practically dead, no evening life, except at the lodges and larger guesthouses. A bit of city planning would go a long way. But city planners seem to be interested solely in developing industrial areas and boring mainstream malls on the outskirts of Kasane. The same goes for Maun. No places to stroll in the evening, you need to take your car to a specific place. We lived in Botswana 30 years ago, back then Kasane and Maun were small, but lively and pleasant to visit!

title: GREAT GAME VIEWING
text: Spent two nights here at a campsite within the park. The area has many features: the Mababe Depression, the Savuti Channel, the Linyati Channel [both channels dry at the moment], various vleis or pans [bore holes for the wildlife to drink], the Savuti Marsh and several rocky hills or kopjes. The game is good but you still have to work at it. Saw lions twice [a lazy male on the first day plus two of the three in Scotty’s pride walking around the outskirts of camp at morning java] but no leopards [just recent tracks]. A long slog through heavy sandy was well rewarded with a group of somewhat skittish wild dogs in the mid-morning sun.

title: Zona arida ma piena di animali
text: La zona di Savuti è situata nel Chobe National Park. L'ambiente qui è più aspro ma la concentrazione di wildlife, a seconda delle stagioni, può essere molto alta: giraffe, elefanti, zebre, impala, antilope roana, antilope nera, gnu, kudu, bufali, facoceri e … i relativi predatori! cioè leoni, iene, sciacalli, ghepardi e leopardi. Dal punto di vista geografico Savuti è un mistero, primo tra tutti l’enigma del Savuti Channel che nel corso degli ultimi 100 anni ha smesso e ripreso a fluire più volte e sempre senza motivo apparente; l’ultimo periodo di “secca” iniziato nel 1982 è terminato nel 2008!. Chobe è per estensione (10.566 kmq) il secondo parco del Paese ed ha un’altissima concentrazione di fauna selvatica. Il parco è diviso in 4 ecosistemi differenti: Serondela nell’estremo nord-est con le sue verdi pianure; Savuti a occidente; Linyanti nel nord-ovest e il caldo e arido hinterland al centro. Gli abitanti originari di questa zona, come del resto di questa parte del continente, erano i San, conosciuti in Botswana come i Basarwa. Erano cacciatori-raccoglitori che si muovevano da una zona all’altra alla ricerca di cibo e acqua. Essi furono poi raggiunti da gruppi di Basubiya e più tardi nel 1911 da un gruppo di Batawana. Il gruppo etnico più numeroso del Paese è costituito invece dagli Tswana, da sempre dediti alla pastorizia; la loro lingua è di origine bantù e si basa sull'uso di prefissi (bo = luogo, Botswana = paese degli Tswana). La prima idea di creare un parco nazionale per proteggere la fauna dall’estinzione e attirare visitatori risale al 1931 ma diventò realtà solo nel 1967 ed effettivamente Chobe divenne il primo parco nazionale del Paese. L’area fu estesa nel 1980 e anche nel 1987.

title: 4x4 trip in Savuti
text: Fantastic trip to Savuti Game Reserve wildlife reserve located in Botswana. It is known for its large elephant population, as well as other species such as lions, leopards, and hippos. The reserve is also home to a variety of bird species. The landscape of the reserve is diverse, including savannah grasslands, wetlands, and rocky outcroppings. Visitors can go on safari drives and walks to see the wildlife, and there are also luxury camps and lodges available for accommodation. Overall, Savuti Game Reserve offers visitors the opportunity to experience the beauty of the African wilderness and see a wide variety of wildlife in their natural habitat.

title: Fantastic
text: Staff, location and cabin absolutely brilliant. Safaris and guide,Tua, just exceptional. Laundry done in a day, wine and food delicious. A must visit.

title: A tree climbing Impala?
text: One of the first things we saw here was an Impala up a tree. Of course it didn't climb the tree but was left there by a leopard. So we were checked in, relaxed and had our first game drive back to the same tree. Sure enough we saw our first ever leopard here. Then the second. Then the third. It was a mother and two sub adults. What could be better? So imagine our astonishment when an adult male came out of the bushes and joined them! A complete family group of  four. We spoke to Matto Barfuss who was also there. He spends six months of every year in the bush and hasn't seen that for ten years. What a privilege. Of course we saw much more in our three days. Wild dogs were an obvious highlight. And three young male lions relaxing together, one of which walked past the jeep so close I could have touched him. Food and service here was first class, as were the guides. Ask the guide about the Savute Channel, its an interesting fact.

title: Animals Galore
text: We had special moments every day Lions, Cheetahs, Elephant, herds of wildebeest & antelope, 100's of birds, giraffe, & best of all Painted Wolves (Wild Dogs !!!)

title: excellent national park
text: Beautiful views and wildlife. Saw many, many birds along with giraffe, buffalo, elephant, lions, impala, jackels, zebra and many more.

title: 6 memorable days in beautiful Moremi: an experience of a lifetime.
text:
We spent 6 days in Moremi and were blown away by the experience: viewing animals closer than ever, despite having previously spent a lot of time self-driving in the bush. Felt very pampered as we had a cook (excellent food), camp attendant (hot showers organised) and a guide who knows the area intimately. He showed us  a wild dog den, and we had close views of cheetah, leopard, lion - all before  vehicles from nearby lodges arrived. A very intimate experience, especially as there were only 4 of us. 
No having to risk a trip to toilet facilties at night either. - each tent had a separate cubicle for all ablutions. We had lived in Botswana for many years previously, but this trip ranks as our best safari experience ever.

placeInfo.name: Central Kalahari Wild Tours
placeInfo.address: central khalahari Wild Tours Tsanekona Plot 4827, Maun, Gaborone Botswana

title: Wild Botswana - A Bespoke Tour
text: We had a wonderful bespoke tour of Botswana's wildest sites as well as Gabarone and Victoria Falls. The wild camping was as luxurious as I could have hoped for, with everything done for us, and the wildlife was amazing. We were driven by a very experienced and knowledgeable guide, who was backed up by a team of chefs and drivers to arrange all our transport, camping and food needs. We even had warm showers in the bush (solar heated of course).

placeInfo.name: Central Kalahari Wild Tours
placeInfo.address: central khalahari Wild Tours Tsanekona Plot 4827, Maun, Gaborone Botswana

title: Safari of a Lifetime
text: My family of six adults had a fabulous two week safari last August/September with Dulang and his amazing team.  Dulang delivered a bespoke trip which included me visiting some old friends in addition to experiences in the Khama Rhino Sanctuary near Serowe, the Makgadikgadi Pans, a day trip to Victoria Falls in Zimbabwe, a boat trip on the Chobe river and then a cross Kalahari road trip from Kasane to Maun via Chobe, Savute, Khwai and the Okavango.  All transport and accommodation was included and most of our food; we were looked after royally, with all campsite management and cooking done for us to the very highest standard.  The bucket showers after a couple of days' wild camping were a real joy!  Having our own vehicles, drivers and staff on a tailor made trip felt like such a luxury, and yet this cost us less than half of  what a similar package tour with one of the big international companies would have cost; the total including return flights from the UK and from Maun to Gaborone for six adults came in at ~£20,000.  I did book my own flights, but Dulang managed all the rest. Yes, it's hot and dusty and bumpy.  Don't expect five star hotels, but if you are prepared to rough it a little and want to experience some of the best of the last African wilderness areas without being in a convoy of international tourists, then this is the way to do it.  I have lived in Botswana and had high expectations of the wildlife we might see.  The trip surpassed them all.  We saw multiple prides of lions, several leopards and a couple of cheetah, packs of wild dogs, packs of hyenas, rhino, buffallo, hippos and crocs, all the antelopes and birds, and of course the many many elephants and giraffes for which Botswana is so famous.  This trip was my 60th birthday gift to myself; my husband, our kids and their partners all experienced the safari of a lifetime.  Thanks to Dulang and the family and to Thuso and all the bush team.

title: A unique and unforgettable landscape
text:
Visiting the Okavango Delta was unlike any other safari experience. The mix of water channels, islands, and open floodplains creates a constantly changing landscape.

Each outing felt different depending on the light and wildlife activity. The peaceful atmosphere, especially in the early mornings, made the experience feel immersive and unhurried.

It’s a remarkable destination for experiencing Botswana’s wilderness.

title: An unforgettable lifetime African Safari
text:
We are truly delighted to welcome you and to share our best experience with African Unique Wilderness Safaris on Botswana soil, the Okavango Delta.
Based in Maun, at the gateway to this remarkable wilderness, the team together with their director Joel (aka  Joel Of Africa) is committed to offering authentic, personalised safari experiences that connect guests with the beauty, wildlife, and culture of Botswana. From the moment you arrive, they ensure you feel safe, comfortable, and inspired by the unique landscapes and wildlife that make the Okavango Delta so special.
We truly recommend African Unique Wilderness for the unforgettable memories in the heart of Africa.
Warm regards,

title: What an experience!
text:
We did a 3 day glamping safari and it was totally amazing.
We saw a leopard with a fresh antelope kill, lions 10m away, experienced the hippo highway up close, elephants crossing the river in front of us, giraffes and more.

title: Booking Mokoro trip to Okavango on cheep.
text:
We bypassed travel agencies and booked mokoro 1 day trip directly with Okavango Kopano Mokoro Community Trust (OKMCT) office. This avoids travel agencies and their hefty fees. OKMCT office is on Sir Seretse Khama Rd about 5 km outside of Maun (google will help you to find your way there). We went there a day before our mokoro trip.

Fees per day are as follows:
P105 per vehicle for concession entrance fee.
P315 for hiring Head Poler (paid directly to the poler at landing)
P105 for renting mokoro (2 people fits in)
P105 camping fee (if you stay overnight)

If you have 4x4 vehicle then you pay above fees, collect voucher/ permit at OKMCT office and just drive to the landing to start your mokoro trips. It takes about 90 minutes drive to get from Maun to mokoro  landing. Most of the people start driving from Maun at 7:30AM and get on water shortly after 9AM.

We had to hire a driver with 4x4 vehicle for 1 day to drive us from our Maun hotel to mokoro landing. OKMCT person helped with this as a side private arrangement.  This cost us  additional BWP1,700. We also bought lunch for ourselves and poler. 

So, all together we spent BWP2,330 for 2 people 1 day mokoro trip. It would have been only BWP630 if we had our own 4x4 vehicle.

title: Botswana's Okavango Delta is brilliant in March
text: March seems a very good time to visit the Delta.  Very green.  Drying out from rains of January and February. Temperatures delightful day and night and perfect for a camping safari, with clear skies brimming with stars by night, trees with flocks of all sorts of gorgeously feather birds, wildflowers, and of course, hippos, elephants, crocs, leopards, lions, zebras, impalas, meerkats, hyena, ostrichs, monkeys, wild dogs. We hadn't realised the dugouts had been superseded with fibreglass kayaks a decade ago, but from a conservation point of view this makes sense.

title: MOBILE CAMPING IN THE OKAVANGO DELTA AND CHOBE NATIONAL PARK ARE THE BEST IN BOTSWANA.
text: AMAZING, PLACE YOU DON'T WANNA MISS WHEN YOU ARE A TRUE NATURE LOVER, I STRONGLY RECCOMEND TRAVELERS TO DO CAMPING IN THE OKAVANGO DELTA, BECAUSE IT IS QUITE AFFODABLE FOR A LIFETIME.

title: Worth seeing and impressive nature paradise
text: During our tour of Namibia and Botswana we spent a total of 5 days in two different accommodations in the Okavango Delta and were able to enjoy this wonderful region to the fullest during this time. The most impressive were the impressions you could enjoy from the water, as there were so many different bird species. We took part in several boat tours where the guides showed us the most diverse animal species.

title: Wonderful place to visit
text: Go in the wet seasons when you can go in a mokoro canoe down the canals the elephants create by walking through the tall grasses. Amazing place to be.

title: Amazing
text: This is the most visited place in botswana.we stayed at kwara camp.very friendly staffs.stayed here for three nights.whats an amazing experience.

title: A great place to camp and experience some African outdoors
text: Camped here for a couple nights on a group tour. Activities included poler transport to and from the campsite, a couple walks around the delta, a poler cruise along the waterways at dusk, a swim in the waterways, and songs around the camp fire at night. A great experience, even including the time we nearly got charged by an elephant that was startled by our approach (but our poler handled it fine). One of the highlights of our Africa tour.

title: The real Africa
text:
The Okavango originates in Angola, for a short stretch it marks the borders between Angola itself and Namibia, then flows into Botswana, within the Moremi.

It has a unique characteristic: it does not flow into any sea and does not flow into any river: it simply disperses in a large swamp called a delta.

We stayed inside the Moremi nature reserve for eight days, in two different tented camps, from which our safaris in search of animals started daily.

We were particularly lucky, having found lions, a large quantity of elephants, hippos, zebras and gazelles, the very rare leopards; many different varieties of birds.

The tented camps, rather than being tiring, allowed us to live in complete contact with nature, in an "out of this world" situation: electricity only that strictly necessary to light dinner, limited water for washing (no problem for that to be drinking), Spartan tents, no telephone contact or Internet (finally!!): an experience to be repeated and recommended.

Nature, during the period of our stay, was still asleep, waiting for the heavy rains that arrive between the end of November and the beginning of December.

In many trips we have taken, none have given us the emotions of the Okavango: spectacular!!!

title: Beautiful Okavango
text: Okavango delta is a very beautiful place I visited the area on a mokoro safari which was a great way to explore the area although it was a very hot day when I explored

title: Overflight with small plane was the sensation
text: We took off from Maun in a small plane for a sightseeing flight over the delta. We finally saw green Africa despite the rainy season and large herds of all kinds of animals. Fantastic.

title: The outstanding Okavango Delta
text: Our first ever Safari Trip. Stayed 3 nights at Kwara Camp. Excellent facilities and welcoming staff. The game drives were superb and we saw incredible wildlife in the most beautiful and unspoilt landscape. Highly recommended

title: SIMPLE SPLENDOUR
text: The 1000th World Heritage Site, the area covers more than two million hectares of wetlands and seasonally flooded grasslands. The Moremi Game Reserve is part of the delta. It is an excellent place to take a mokoro ride, the edges of the boats but inches above the waterline, skillfully guided by boatmen. The game viewing is very good.

title: Marvellous camping Safari in Botswana.
text: The 9-day 8-night Okavango Expeditions camping Safari, Kasane to Maun, with 2 nights in 4 different  campsites, starting in Chobe, was a marvellous experience.  Tour guides/drivers Paul & Ossi had superb fauna/flora, knowledge, binocular-grade eyesight &, seemingly a 6th sense on where to find elusive animals.  The food was amazing, especially considering the prep. conditions and the team setting up and taking down the en-suite (bush style!)  tents, with beds, were streamlined and speedy - an all-round excellent experience.  Sitting under the star-studded sky, with lions grunting not far off, plus other animal communications during the night, just added to the experience.

title: incredible
text: Wenn Afrika-Safari, dann nur Botswana. Ist zwar schweinemäßig teuer, aber das absolut Beste von allem. Da kommt Kenia nicht mit, auch nicht Südafrika und auch nicht Krüger Park. Hier sind die Tiere und hier ist das ursprüngliche Afrika

title: Prachtig
text: Dit vergeet je nooit meer! Wonderschone ervaring van de natuur met veel wildlife, wandelingen, zonsopgang en -ondergang,..

title: Seeing African wildlife in style!
text: We followed a pack of wild dogs on a kill and saw many other animals. We stayed at Duke's Camp which was amazing!

title: Qualcosa di unico....
text:
Il Delta è un ecosistema unico situato al centro di una delle maggiori distese di sabbia del mondo, il Bacino del Kalahari, proprio come un’oasi in un territorio inospitale. La vegetazione è lussureggiante e l’avifauna è tra le più ricche dell’Africa. Il fiume Okavango, non raggiunge mai il mare, ma le sue possenti acque si riversano sulle sabbie del Kalahari. Qui la grande sete del deserto viene localmente placata in una selva blu-verde di acqua dolce, con canneti color smeraldo e alberi imponenti.
Il fiume Okavango ha la propria sorgente nell'Altopiano del Benguela in Angola e prima di penetrare in Botswana attraversa circa 1.300 km, poi si allarga e si disperde formando tre corsi principali ed un infinito numero di ramificazioni: canali, pozze e lagune di acqua incredibilmente limpida e pura che coprono una superficie di circa 16.000 kmq. I paesaggi dell'Okavango, le oltre 600 specie di animali e uccelli che vi abitano, la vita acquatica, tutto rende questa parte del continente africano, totalmente sconosciuta ai più, una delle mete più straordinarie. In questo connubio di terra e acqua la natura trionfa: pellicani, egrette, cicogne, aquile pescatrici, pesci tigre, coccodrilli, ippopotami, elefanti e anche, con un po’ di fortuna, predatori.

title: Amazing tour operator
text: Nyala adventure safaris Mobile camping we 5 of 5 . We had Experience and the food was very amazing. We sow lots of animals all game drives.

placeInfo.name: Nyala Adventure Safaris
placeInfo.address: Boseja Street, Maun, Ngamiland East Botswana

title: Budget safari, GREAT experience
text:
We did a 5 nights - 5 days safari with the company. It was such a great experience, couldn't have been better.  The experience of the safari was just outstanding, we saw so many animals. 
All the camp gear is confortable yet adventurous, the food is really good too. Mucheka is a really helpful and kind person. And you will only be aware of the truth of this sentence once you meet him. People in Botwana are usually easygoing, helpful and sincerely kind, however Mucheka just breaks the records. He is always trying to make the best experience for you. He always gave us advice on which were the best options, even not concerning safari. if you have any questions just speak to him. Do take his calls, most of the booking we made it via WhatsApp calls and it's the best way to do so. We advise an early booking. It is normal to pay all the amount in advance, since accommodation and everything needed needs to be booked.
Nyala is probably the best tour operator in all Botswana regarding affordability, quality and satisfaction.

placeInfo.name: Nyala Adventure Safaris
placeInfo.address: Boseja Street, Maun, Ngamiland East Botswana

title: Disappointing and a bit sketchy
text:
Nyala Mobile Safaris is a relatively new business. Our family of 3 adults booked a 5-day budget mobile safari based on a good price for the comfort level and a handful of excellent reviews. Our overall experience was disappointing, including poor communication throughout and some sketchy aspects. We recommend using a more proven operator; we certainly would in the future. For details read on.

The good: The setup of the camps was as comfortable as advertised, with spacious tents, small cots, a portable bathroom and shower, and other amenities. The food was certainly adequate under the circumstances. The vehicle had some issues but none untoward given the exceptionally bad roads it navigates day in and day out. As advertised, there was plenty of free alcohol, which was useful in taking the edge off the steady stream of difficulties.

The disappointing: Our crew consisted of company-owner Mucheka who was in charge of camping, meals, and other logistics; a certified guide who was in charge of the safari aspects; and a "helper boy." Unfortunately the guide was surprisingly incompetent. Over the entire 5-day safari he found literally zero animals we hadn't spotted on our own, and on multiple occasions he got lost when we were on a game drive without Macheka. It's impossible to know how much more wildlife we would have seen if our guide had participated in the spotting (and how much less time spent driving aimlessly if he knew the way), but wildlife spotting has certainly been the norm for guides during the handful of other safaris we've been on.

The sketchy: We paid for a private safari months in advance and confirmed it with Mucheka several times, yet when we arrived at the airport we were told that "two friends" -- a last-minute booking -- would be joining us for the first half of our trip and were waiting in the safari vehicle. We put our foot down and they did find a second vehicle, driver, and dining table, but for the first 2+ days Macheka was juggling two parallel safaris, presumably to the detriment of both. At the other end, when our post-safari transfer, included in the price, dropped us off, we were told Macheka hadn't paid for it and we needed to fork over $105. The money was recovered in the end but not without effort. Overall, communication with Macheka was challenging and a bit slippery from start to finish; it should have been a warning sign.

Again, we would not use Nyala Mobile Safaris again ourselves and unfortunately cannot recommend the company to others.

placeInfo.name: Nyala Adventure Safaris
placeInfo.address: Boseja Street, Maun, Ngamiland East Botswana

title: Great spot close to Gaborone.
text:
Great spot so close to Gabs. 
I didn’t have time to explore the reserve this time, but the chalets were in a beautiful setting. They were very well kept and had everything you might need. 
The restaurant was really well run, friendly and attentive staff, and the food was delicious. It was a lovely setting for dinner. 
All the staff on the reserve were extremely friendly and helpful. 
I definitely recommend a visit to anyone in the area.

title: Prompt service, best steak!
text:
This Nature Reserve is located close to the Kgale Hill along the A1 and I went here for lunch. The atmosphere is calming and connects one to nature. 

I only visited the restaurant and was impressed by how close you get to the animals, while dining, in a safe and protected manner - a herd of Impalas and a couple of warthogs were what I saw on the day. I ordered their special on the day which was a steak, pork chop, wors, chakalaka, pap and sandwich. The presentation was splendid - food was served very quickly and I really enjoyed the steak. 

It's also child-friendly with a kid's playground. I'd recommend this for an interesting twist to traditional dining and lunch while in Gaborone.

title: Ripped off
text: The game drive was advertised for a minimum of two people, we arrived with three people and were advised that the minimum was now 4 people. I paid for 4 people and at the last minute after the game drive had started with the three of us two more joined. The reserve refused to refund me the additional person. Exceptionally poor and rude reception staff. Emails have gone ignored. I do not recommend this at all.

title: A must visit for those visiting Gaborone
text: We visited august 2023 but there was no option to choose this. This nature reserve has lots to do. We went to a separate enclosure for vultures and had an up close and personal talk about snakes where we were allowed to hold them. We then went on a safari trip where we saw giraffes, rhinos and antelope. The tour guide was knowledgeable and friendly. We had lunch in the restaurant on site, really good prices and lots to offer. The restaurant is next to an area where deer walk around, so nice to see! The park offers a twilight tour also to see more animals but we didn’t do it this time. This park is a must visit for those visiting Gaborone.

title: Not much to do honestly
text: Not sure if it's just the day we went on, but we hardly saw any animals genuinely. There is very low activity there, and I don't understand why it's considered the top attraction in Gabz. The staff are also very moody and disgruntled  for some reason, didn't seem eager or even willing to provide explanations of the wildlife and scenery, etc. They were also quite rude which compromised the experience. However, the game drive made up for it, even without seeing any animals, it was still a very decent experience.

title: Wonderful Experience
text: Amazing experience,lots of rhinos,giraffes and zebras.our guide was super friendly and funny.This is a must.wonderful service.

title: Great experience. Plenty of animals
text:
Amazing. Rhinos, antilopes, springboks, hyppos, giraffes and many more.

Two hours well spent within a 30m drive from Gaborone.

And nice lunch drink at the end.

title: This is a must when in Botswana
text: Amazing, amazing experience. The person who took us on a game drive searched for the rhinos. We saw so many animals! The staff is so friendly and helpful, you get a panic button in case something happens. The spa has great massages and the restaurant is a must!

title: Mokolodi game drive
text: Mokolodi Nature Reserve is home to various animals, including rhinos, giraffes, impalas, kudus, zebras, and more. The game drives provide excellent opportunities to see wildlife up close, making it a highlight for many visitors. Our guide, Benjamin, was particularly informative, sharing extra information about the animals we encountered, which enhanced the overall experience. This informative and engaging approach by guides like Benjamin adds significant value to the game drives​

title: Wonderful serene getaway from Gaborone.
text: We were delighted to hear about the reserve so close to Gaborone. We had some free time so we reserved a chalet for three nights. Although we did not receive a very warm welcome at reception we headed out to find our chalet. After getting a little lost we found a delightful chalet on the watering hole. Super clean with a well stocked kitchenette. The nice patio overlooking the watering hole was awesome with a fire pit and wicker chairs. We went out for game drives every day. We saw quite a few of the animals on the reserve. Giraffe, zebras, kudo, wildabeast, rinos, crocodile and hippos, fish eagles and baboons to name a few. It’s a different type of game drive, you really have to look but somehow we enjoyed it very much. The best part were the evening animal visits to the watering hole between 5pm and 6.  We wish our initial arrival had been a little more welcoming and maybe a little more info given. We managed and loved it overall. We returned the following we for an additional three nights. It’s a beautiful place.

title: Not worth it
text:
I have been on other game rides – – Kruger in South Africa and the Masai Mara in Kenya among others. So those are game rides.; With that background, there is nothing to recommend this tow hour jaunt a half hour from Gaborone in Botswana. You drive through trees on a bumpy, rutted road in an old open  air truck with no suspension, (anyone with back problems, like me, beware, it is painful), and see animals peeking out through the trees. They are abundant, but there are no wide open spaces with African vistas; just trees, impala, and giraffes, in that order.

If you've never done a game ride, and this is your only opportunity, then well, why not. But otherwise find something else to do. 

Having said that, they do have a very nice restaurant on site which offers an extensive lunch menu outdoors.. So if you decide to do this, make sure you stay for lunch.

title: Awesome rhino tracking experience!
text: I visited on a Saturday with five other American exchange students, and we did the Rhino Tracking. After just 30 minutes, our guides spotted rhino tracks and we got out of the truck to track down the rhinos on foot. There were four of them, not far from the road, and we watched/followed them for at least 45 minutes. We had a fantastic experience watching the rhinos, and we felt lucky to have encountered them so early in the day. We then had several hours left of the tour to ride around looking for other wildlife. We saw giraffes, impalas, a kudu, warthog, hippos, and lots of birds. Then there was a very well done picnic by a lake. The whole thing lasted four hours and was easily worth the P800 per person we paid. We will be back for giraffe tracking!

title: Excellent economic option close to Gaborone
text:
It has been an excellent option in terms of proximity and economy since it is relatively close to Gaborone.
Africa is characterized by the remoteness of its natural reserves and the onerousness of its access fee and Mokolodi is the exception.
There are various hours of scheduled tours during the day, the guide has been very friendly and we have seen: Giraffes, zebras, impalas, wild boars, ostriches, etc.
We haven't had any luck seeing the hippos in the lake, nor the cheetah...

To keep in mind: Be careful! With ostriches they can be aggressive!

The food in the restaurant is highly recommended!

Enjoy Mokolodi Nature Reserve because: LIFE...is a JOURNEY!

title: POSITIVE and NEGATIVE details about Mokolodi Nature Reserve.
text:
THE BEST PARTS: beautiful land, many standard and active animals, basic and tidy chalets (we stayed in the small chalet (“Hartebeest”, buy the watering hole), and was the coolest part of our experience. With no one else around for miles but the animals. We were told the only predators in the reserve are leopards and hyenas, and although we never saw any, there was still enough excitement happening within the camp site to keep us happy. The kitchen had hot plates, silverware, utensils, a fridge, and toaster oven. The water in the tap is both hot and cold as needed. There is a nice breeze through the chalet all day and evening to keep you comfortable. 

LESSER PARTS: we tried to book a chalet for 2 nights, 1 week in advance, but we couldn’t pay over the phone b/c their card reader was giving them errors, (which was fine with us). Instead, we were told to pay on arrival, which we did, they charged us for only 1 night, so I wanted to pay again for the 2nd night that we booked for. She instead to stay for 1 night, and pay for night 2 tomorrow (Which was ok, fine.) We also wanted to plan activities in advance, but we realized there was only 1 driver/guide there, who was reluctant to book for 7:30am (which, ok, if he has a preference, that’s fine.) so we booked for 10:30am. In the morning we were picked up from camp to go to the lodge and meet the vehicle for safari. We were asked where are bags were, and we told her we were staying a 2nd night. She told us we needed to get our bags and move to a chalet farther back from the watering hole because they booked out our chalet to someone else (not really fine) We were disappointed, but shrugged it off and went home after our 1 night stay and game drive instead of what we planned our weekend for. We are chill and flexible people, so we went with the flow, but for some people, this may be a huge let down. 

There is no instruction such as “make sure if you need or want anything before we drive you to camp (like food or basics)  because we won’t want to drive you back and forth many times” it’s a 20 min drive, and they have to call in someone to drive you back. Once they drop you, that’s it, and it’s too late to plan anything else. There are no scheduled times for transferring within the reserve, and transfers are an annoyance. (It’s too far just to drive one person back and forth down a extremely bumpy road.)

Our guide was also fine, although a bit apathetic about what he needed to do. He did on this day, seem mildly annoyed with anything outside of what he was required. (To be fair to him though, maybe he was having a bad day.)

Although I am completely unaware of how much employees here are paid, something tells me that the reserve should be paying employees much more for their work, time and effort. If they were given higher payment by the employer, I feel they would (as anyone would) feel happier about what they do everyday, and in turn, the reserve would see more profit and growth. 

I wish Mokolodi and their staff all the best in the future.

title: Not our best camping experience in Botswana
text:
We reserved a campsite in the Mokolodi Nature Reserve close to Gabarone on our way to South Africa after a 3-week 4x4 Botswana trip. The Nature Reserve was easy to find and at the reception we were told to enter the park through a second gate and follow the signs to Chalets & Campsites and then to Campsites (no clear direction or indication of the time required). It turned out to be a long (4+ km) curved badly maintained stong track with big stones requiring a 4x4 and taking more than 25 minutes to find our campsite (we were the only campers). The sites are far apart (good for privacy) but with poor signs to the various sites. As we wanted to have diner in the restaurant we had to return and had an excellent diner but had to return to the campsite in the dusk and too late to light up the donkey boiler etc.
The campsite offered a boiler, shower, toilet, braai, flush toilet, dustbin, wash basin and fire place. No electricity, no lights. Cars passing during the night. And of course baboons.
Not our best camping experience in Botswana.
Our advice: come early and take your time to find the campsite.

title: Seriously dissatisfied and very expensive for the quality of the chalet
text: I was seriously dissatisfied, we asked for a chalet for 2 and then at the last minute they said the only availability was a chalet for 6 almost double the price 😡 we agreed as it had been along day .. supposedly the chalet had just been renovated…. Untrue, it was in serious need of a renovation and in the morning no water. Also the 2 person chalet stayed free … a shame it could be a loveyp’ace but it was a ripoff

title: camping near Gaborone
text:
ok its not Kutse or Central Kalahari but its close to Gaborone,
we camped thanks to our chobe 4x4 as it has all the equipment need to camp in game reserves. nice positioned campsites

title: Wonderful experience at Mokolodi
text: Good food. Wonderful service. Excellent vibe at the the restaurant. Wonderful atmosphere as place is out of town/ nature reserve.

title: First trip to Gaborone
text: We had a great time at the Game reserve. Got to see 3 different animals for the first time, Impalas, a Red Hartebeest, and the Greater Kudu. We also got to see a male Giraffe (he said he knew the sex because of its darker spots as opposed to the female ones that have lighter spots here) sitting down, waited for it to get up but it didnt for a long time. At the end of the ride we also got to see some Zebras with more Impalas. Our driver was really nice and gave us a great tour.

title: Rip offs
text: They are rip offs! You have to buy a full year membership to drive around the park with your own car. This rule isn’t communicated in their website at all, so we drove all the way here for nothing.

title: Not worth it!
text: The positive first thing: the entrance is free... however, renovations are currently underway and therefore there is hardly anything to see anyway. Not worth it.

title: Beautiful museum
text: Beautiful history of Batswana. Clean and well kept facilities . Friendly staff. Best place to visit with kids to learn of the history of the country

title: Disappointed
text: Pros: free and beautiful building hosting the museum... Otherwise quite poor in terms of quantity and diversity of things to see ...large rooms can be visited very quickly and we stayed hungry ... souvenir shop closed ...no adjacent cafe...bof ...

title: Great game experience, average accommodation.
text:
We stayed at the Rhino Sanctuary for two nights en route to Kubu Island.  There is a lot of game in this reserve - well worth driving around, especially to the various pans.  We really enjoyed this aspect of the reserve.  

The accommodation in the chalets is extremely basic, it consists of beds, a small bathroom and a table.  The upper part of the walls on the A frame is made of netting, so one is unable to "close" this area up.  This resulted in all of us being absolutely freezing during the night.

We ate at the restaurant and found the food to be satisfactory, although somewhat excessively priced.  The service was also very slow due to what seemed like minimal staff.  We assumed this was as a result of Covid.

title: 为啥说奥卡万戈是safari的天花板？
text:
不是它的动物多，也不是它的动物稀有，而是坐上小飞机在空中巡游的时候能看到湿地和草原风景，这个是最美的。

直升机🚁：295美金每人，两位起玩，如果你是一个人要么自己出两人钱，要么自己找队友
小飞机：200美元每人，一人可报。

飞行游览时间都是45-60分钟。也都是马翁机场起飞。

有人说，坐飞机来马翁的时候，民航在天上就能看到三角洲，为啥还要花钱单独坐小飞机。民航飞行高度是万米，你能看到个轮廓，而且玻璃脏你还拍不清楚。小飞机飞几百米高，地上的动物都能看清，而且是绕一大圈，腹地都能看得到，没有花钱的不是。

placeInfo.name: Moremi Wildlife Reserve
placeInfo.locationString: Moremi Game Reserve, Okavango Delta, North-West District

title: Unforgettable Moremi Safari – the real way.
text:
Our group of eight from India experienced a most memorable eight days Mobile Camp Safari in Moremi Game Reserve (Botswana) with Royale Wilderness Mobile Safari Company, Maun from 9th to 16th September 2025. The rustic and wild charm of a mobile camp is a most exciting and unique experience of the wilderness for any wildlife enthusiast. 

Not many mobile camp operators will venture into Moremi because of the serious challenges posed by the Okavango Delta with its risky water-ways crossings. But our expert Safari Guides with their intimate knowledge of Moremi did a fantastic job. We have very special words of appreciation for our Safari Guide CJ (Chika Simon Junior). He left no stone unturned to get us to the best and most exciting wildlife experiences. We will remember his amiable and cheerful nature, his genuine love for the denizens of the wild and tremendous safari expertise. 

During our stay in Moremi (3 nights in Xakanaxa and 3 nights in Xinii BOGA Campsites) and the safari drives in those areas, we saw more than sixty lions, including cubs, belonging to five different prides (namely, Budumatau, First Bridge, Xinii, Blackpool and South Gate prides), two packs of wild-dogs (one with more than 50 dogs and another about 30 dogs), three leopard sightings, a family of four cheetahs, many elephants, cape buffaloes, impalas, zebras, greater kudus, red lechwes, spotted hyenas, waterbucks, hippos, black-bearded blue wildebeests, south african giraffes, wart-hogs, ostriches, tsessebes, steenboks, nile crocodiles, chakma baboons, vervet monkeys and other animals along with ground hornbills, kori bustards, vultures, marabou storks, eagles, helmeted guinea-fowls, spur fowls, hornbills  and large flocks of a variety of birds near the innumerable water-bodies. And all these encounters were very exciting being fairly in close proximity of the animals and, in some instances, of animals on their kills. Our Guides always expertly positioned our safari vehicles for the best photo opportunities. The land cruiser safari vehicles were in top condition, which is crucial for a Moremi Safari with its challenging terrain. We were happy to see not many safari vehicles crowding important sightings --- which we often experienced in our previous safaris in Ngogrongoro Crater National Park, Maasai Mara National Reserve and Serengeti National Park, especially during the peak season.

Both our BOGA (Botswana Guide Association) Campsites at Xakanaxa and Xinii were extremely well-situated near large water-bodies and visited by a variety of animals, such as, elephants, red lechwes, impalas, zebras and hippos even during broad daylight and also by hyenas and lions during the night. To mention, for instance, one of our experiences at Xinii. While dining around 8 pm we saw in our spot-light a herd of over 50 red lechwes running panic-stricken for about one hour from one side to the other in the very large swampy reeds area in front of our Camp and elephants trumpeting. We understood that lions were on the hunt. Sure enough, early following morning we found the spoors of a large lion pride around the camp-site and saw 16 members of Xinii Pride resting in the shade 300 meters from our Camp. They were not successful in their lechwe hunt that evening. But later that day we found the pride feasting on a wart-hog kill.  

During our safari drives, we passed by Blackpool BOGA Campsite and we found that area most richly endowed with a variety of wildlife and birds. If we do visit Moremi again, we will surely target Blackpool Camp-site -- for which booking is required to be made more than a year in advance due to great demand. We also enjoyed a 3-hour boat ride from Ngami Marine Boat Station in one of the permanent channels of Okavango Delta visiting the small but impressive heronry on Ngagigwa Island. We were however not lucky enough to spot the elusive sitatunga antelope.

The Mobile Camps at Xakanaxa and Xinii were efficiently managed in every respect by Royale Wilderness Safari Team. Chef Cynthia is a wonderful person who did her best to cook us some delicious vegetarian Indian cuisine and tasty bakery products freshly baked in wilderness style. The dining tables were very well-laid out in the large dining tent overlooking the very scenic wilderness. The spacious sahara style tents with two well-furnished beds, solar rechargeable lights and ensuite bucket-shower toilets made us quite comfortable. The only issue was with mid-day temperatures hovering around 35 degrees celsius which made resting inside the tents quite difficult. Those preferring cooler weather should consider visiting Moremi in August when temperatures will be cooler and wildlife sightings also very good.

Last but not least we are grateful to our Tour Operator, Safari Frank for professionally and expertly organising our Moremi Safari from start to end. We highly recommend a Moremi Safari with Safari Frank and Royale Wilderness Mobile Safaris for a most unforgettable experience in the real safari style with intimate and immersive encounters in the wild.

title: One park that stands out.
text: Spent several days camped in the park with a professional safari outfit. A highlight of the trip through Botswana.  Hugely diverse wildlife and scenery from dry desert to waterholes attracting animals of all kinds. Also added a cruise through the wetlands in the delta. Tent camping with a fire at night and the bonus of elephant slowly moving through the campsite at night. Moremi Reserve definitely delivered.

placeInfo.name: Moremi Wildlife Reserve
placeInfo.locationString: Moremi Game Reserve, Okavango Delta, North-West District

title: Excellent Camp in Okavango Delta
text: The experience is wonderful. Everyone takes care of your needs. The food is good. The other guests are nice. You sit together at meals so there is a chance to meet others. The safaris were terrific. Our guide, G, was very knowledgeable. We started seeing animals on the ride to the camp from the airstrip. Rooms were very comfortable. It was cold so they gave us bush babies (hot water bottles) to put in our beds. Very relaxing. Saw leopards, lions, zebras, elephants, impalas. Lots of beautiful birds.

placeInfo.name: Moremi Wildlife Reserve
placeInfo.locationString: Moremi Game Reserve, Okavango Delta, North-West District

title: Good game drives, rather rough
text: We had one full day and half day of game drives. As we stayed at Gomoti River Lodge, the day drive into the Reserve was a long one and bumpy: the end of the rain season added to the bumpiness. Still, the drives were successful: we saw large game and an incredible diversity of birds, including such rarities as wattled cranes. Very much worth it.

placeInfo.name: Moremi Wildlife Reserve
placeInfo.locationString: Moremi Game Reserve, Okavango Delta, North-West District

title: So Much To See
text: As part of a tour we were lucky to spend a couple of days in this area. Wildlife at every turn. Best with a guide as they know where they are going. We camped and sadly I was unable to charge my camera or phone as the connections they had were different to what I needed.

placeInfo.name: Moremi Wildlife Reserve
placeInfo.locationString: Moremi Game Reserve, Okavango Delta, North-West District

title: Done very little for the safety, cleanliness - too low
text: Apart from the nature, service at Moremi game reserve is poor. At South Gate the staff is concentrated much more on charging your entry than delivering for your security. If not insisting on information regarding visitor's safety, they would provide very little of such. Generally, the emploies seem like trying hard to kill the time. Higene at the toilets is low, there wasn't hand soap or garbage bins.  Track signs were extremely scarce. One can easily get lost. Considering immense space and lack of phone coverage,  likelihood of grave accidents looks high. Again, it is a  great place for the Nature lovers, but it  is done very little for the safety and the convenience of the visitors.

placeInfo.name: Moremi Wildlife Reserve
placeInfo.locationString: Moremi Game Reserve, Okavango Delta, North-West District

title: Good, but Khwai Community Concession was better
text:
We spent several nights in the small village of Khwai and from there we made self-drive tours to the Khwai Community Concession and the Moremi Game Reserve on two days each. The latter is a total of almost 5.00km2 of natural park in the eastern area of the Okavango Delta. It is considered one of the most beautiful and best nature reserves in Africa, as there are large numbers of animals and is the oldest national park in Botswana. Admission costs 270 BWP per person plus 75 BWP per car. In one day we drove the route to Xakanaxa with Dombo Hippo Pools and Paradise Pools. The next day we drove to the South Gate and then to the Black Pools, Mogogelo and Xini Lagoon.
We liked the rides and also saw quite a lot of animals. However, overall we liked the Khwai Community Concession even better, because there were more water and also more animals to see through the Okavango.

placeInfo.name: Moremi Wildlife Reserve
placeInfo.locationString: Moremi Game Reserve, Okavango Delta, North-West District

title: Very interesting park
text:
Very nice park for the variety of animals and vegetation , you can spot many lions ( even outside the park ) . Inside there is a campsite.
Mandatory a 4X4 , risk of cover-up . is about 2 hours off-road from Maun .

placeInfo.name: Moremi Wildlife Reserve
placeInfo.locationString: Moremi Game Reserve, Okavango Delta, North-West District

title: Amazing park with a tremendous amount of wildlife.
text: My son and I spent 7 days in Moremi/ Savuti  in a self drive camping vehicle. There is wildlife in abundance day and night. Incredible views of lions, elephants , giraffes, wild dogs, and buffalo. We rented a Toyota landcruiser in Windhoek Namibia and had supplies and plenty of petrol for this unique journey into the middle of nowhere. The roads were not good but we knew this from previous experiences. The campgrounds are expensive but basic with water, showers, and bathrooms. Our days were consumed by driving , waiting, and observing. We would always get into camp before dark to set up, prepare for the next day. We did no night driving because everything comes into the camps at night. No fences, no deterrents, just open space. We had fires every night and that seemed to work as a slight deterrent. We were visited by hyenas, elephants, leopards, and monkeys. Everything was put away every evening. Nothing was left out which was standard practice for us. I never realized how large elephants are and how quite they are when they are walking thru the night. The camp staff sweep the sites every day so you can see fresh paw prints in the morning to see what visited the night before. I know now how curious spotted hyenas are and again , I was taken back because of their size. All and all, fantastic experience taking out the road conditions, the inconsistency of the park management, and getting used to all our curious visitors. A special place indeed.

We got the chance to see the circle of life and animals trying to hunt for there next meal. Something I will never forget! It was amazing! I highly recommend this area!

title: It's worth it
text: We stayed three days at the Moremi. Very good for wildlife viewing. Highly recommended. Lots of elephants and, with any luck, predators.

placeInfo.name: Moremi Wildlife Reserve
placeInfo.locationString: Moremi Game Reserve, Okavango Delta, North-West District

text: Absolutely upscale experience. Wild animals in natural habitat.  Smell of grass, colors, waiting for new animals showing’s up unexpectedly…..❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️

placeInfo.name: Moremi Wildlife Reserve
placeInfo.locationString: Moremi Game Reserve, Okavango Delta, North-West District

title: Looking for animals
text:
To immerse yourself in the life of the Moremi Reserve, the ideal way is to face the life of the tented camp: a group of tents, rather Spartan, with precise rules: wake up at 5.30 am and departure by jeep at 6.00 am, to attend the dawn and take advantage of the moment when the animals are most numerous.
Wandering around until 12.30pm, looking for animals; return to camp at 12.30pm (lunch and short rest); restart at 3.30pm 3 until 6.30pm (sunset) to follow other tracks.
Dinner around 7.30pm and return to the tents around 9pm with an absolute ban on leaving until the following morning. The camp, being open, is crossed by animals (elephants, hippos, felines in general) at night.
But in the hours of animal research it is explained why this reserve is one of the most important, not only in Botswana but in general: exciting to find oneself a few meters from the lions, witness the leopards mating and hunting, find oneself in the midst of a herd of elephants, zebras, antelopes, giraffes, and all sorts of varieties of birds.
An exciting and fascinating experience, not to be missed for those who want to taste the wildest and most authentic Africa.

placeInfo.name: Moremi Wildlife Reserve
placeInfo.locationString: Moremi Game Reserve, Okavango Delta, North-West District

title: Great park to visit
text: This reserve in the Mopane bush is a bit disappointing when it comes to seeing wildlife , this was better outside the reserve. Visit outside rain season.

placeInfo.name: Moremi Wildlife Reserve
placeInfo.locationString: Moremi Game Reserve, Okavango Delta, North-West District

title: GREAT LIONS IN THE KHWAI SECTOR
text: Good game in this area, the highlight being eight lions at a kill, sauntering to a waterhole and then passing in front of our vehicle heading for an afternoon nap. Lots of roads and wide open spaces can be found. It has a very wild feel to it.

placeInfo.name: Moremi Wildlife Reserve
placeInfo.locationString: Moremi Game Reserve, Okavango Delta, North-West District

title: Animali nel loro habitat naturale
text: La Riserva di Moremi è situata proprio sulle sponde orientali del Delta dell'Okavango di cui ne incorpora ben il 20%. L’idea di creare una riserva in questa zona risale al 1961 e fu approvata ufficialmente nel 1965. L’area fu estesa nel 1976 per includere Chief’s Island e poi anche nel 1992. Moremi oggi ricopre 4.870 kmq ed è considerato uno dei più bei parchi d’Africa sia per i paesaggi -combina foreste di mopane e di acacie, piane alluvionali e lagune – sia per l’abbondanza della fauna africana: grandi lagune abitate da ippopotami e dove si recano a bere branchi di elefanti si alternano a radure con grossi branchi di zebre ed antilopi. Molto probabili incontri con predatori come leoni e, più raramente, licaoni, ghepardi e leopardi.

placeInfo.name: Moremi Wildlife Reserve
placeInfo.locationString: Moremi Game Reserve, Okavango Delta, North-West District

title: Lots of wildlife
text: We were pleasantly surprised by how much we saw in the 3 days: elephants, lions, buffalo, leopard, kudu, impala, hippos, and so many birds. The park has many different landscapes, and we loved the variety of settings.

placeInfo.name: Moremi Wildlife Reserve
placeInfo.locationString: Moremi Game Reserve, Okavango Delta, North-West District

title: A true art - Chobe baskets.
text: These baskets are unique. A true art. The women that produced them are fantastic. These women are an example for our society. They are hard working, loving mothers who take care of their family members thanks to their work an need our support and appreciation.

placeInfo.name: Vuche Vuche Basket Weavers

title: Exceptional
text: I recently had the pleasure of visiting Vuche Vuche Basket weavers, and I must say, the experience was outstanding. The ambiance was inviting and comfortable. From the moment I walked in, I was mesmerized by their baskets on display. I was greeted warmly by the knowledgeable and friendly Vuche Vuche ladies. Their product quality was outstanding, exceeding my expectations.

title: Vast and surreal landscape
text:
Visiting the Makgadikgadi Pans was a striking experience. The sheer scale and openness of the landscape create a sense of isolation and quiet.

The flat horizon and changing light make it visually dramatic in a completely different way from the Delta.

A unique and memorable part of Botswana.

placeInfo.name: Makgadikgadi Salt Pan
placeInfo.locationString: Makgadikgadi Pans National Park, Central District

title: Contrasting and diverse park
text: Thoroughly enjoyed a guided tour through this park. Very different to others visited through Namibia and Botswana.  A wide range of animals and birds, and the boabab trees were magical.  To he scenery is pretty special and the sand/salt pans and plains so,etching to see.  Finished off with a beer watching the sun set over the lake and topped off by flamingos coming back in just after the sun had set.  Loved it

placeInfo.name: Makgadikgadi Salt Pan
placeInfo.locationString: Makgadikgadi Pans National Park, Central District

title: Magical
text:
Wonderful place to visit.

The flatness of the salt pans lets you view the curvature of the earth which is pretty amazing.

placeInfo.name: Makgadikgadi Salt Pan
placeInfo.locationString: Makgadikgadi Pans National Park, Central District

title: Wlidlife surprise
text:
After a few long days of slow game viewing driving in the Khwai/Moremi areas we decided on some quiet days here, we’ll pass on heading to the salt pans. We didn't expect particularly good game viewing along the Boteti river but we'd just do that. We entered one afternoon around 2pm, after dropping some tables and chairs at our SKL Khumaga campsite we set off along the river bank. And what an afternoon. A long drought for Botswana left the Boteti dry apart from a few remaining waterholes along the river. Everything was either heading to or from water. Thousands of zebra, as many wildebeest, impala, kudu and giraffe. And elephants. Down on the river bed more than 200 spread over a 15km drive. And when on the banks curving in the sand track there seemed an elephant behind every bush either coming or going to the river.
The following morning, same animals but only 10% of the volume of the previous afternoon and few ele-phant. But again by 2.30 pm literally a few thousand animals covering the dry riverbed, unbelievable.

placeInfo.name: Makgadikgadi Salt Pan
placeInfo.locationString: Makgadikgadi Pans National Park, Central District

title: Highly recommend
text: We came here as part of our Intrepid group tour & it was really good! It was the dry season so not as much animal & bird life but just the vast expanse of the salt pans was amazing.  We enjoyed a complimentary drink as the sun went down,  loved it!

placeInfo.name: Makgadikgadi Salt Pan
placeInfo.locationString: Makgadikgadi Pans National Park, Central District

title: a magnificent and magical encounter
text: meeting with magical meerkats, they are used to humans but remain wild, you must not touch them. So exceptional

placeInfo.name: Makgadikgadi Salt Pan
placeInfo.locationString: Makgadikgadi Pans National Park, Central District

title: Magnificent, exceptional, a pure moment of happiness
text: a magical, exceptional moment, after about 2 hours of 4x4 driving in the middle of the bush where we came across lots of animals. We arrived to take our 4x4 for 40 minutes in the middle of the salt flats, an unreal moment, then a photo moment, and here we arrived in the middle of nowhere for an unreal night, large sleeping bags, comfortable bed with good blankets , at this time 1 degree but we will not be cold. A meal around a wood fire, a member of staff even played the Guitar for us accompanied by traditional songs. I have no words to describe this unforgettable experience which will remain forever engraved in our memories.

placeInfo.name: Makgadikgadi Salt Pan
placeInfo.locationString: Makgadikgadi Pans National Park, Central District

title: Absolutely worth a visit.
text: Beautiful National Park, spread out over thousands of hectares. The drive through the park is beautiful and the area around the Boteti river is truly gorgeous- a miniature Serengeti plain, so to speak- with hundreds of animals- zebra, antilope, elephants, hippos, crocodiles, vultures, pelicans and other water birds, etc.

placeInfo.name: Makgadikgadi Salt Pan

title: Overnight on the Kalahari
text: ISAAC is a remarkable guide. He knows the animals and the stars. incredible evening. We had stayed in Le Too le Tau and safaried out to the salt pan.  Incredible evening of food, music, and stars.

placeInfo.name: Makgadikgadi Salt Pan
