text: à faire absolument pour sentir l'immensité de ce désert. activité proposé par notre lodge planet baobab

placeInfo.name: Makgadikgadi Salt Pan
placeInfo.locationString: Makgadikgadi Pans National Park, Central District

title: It was awful
text:
I actually stopped at a nearby lodge on route north to show my family the pans. Nature is predictable so the fact that the pans were very full and very little opportunity to actually get close to them is nobody’s fault. But it was the case. We had no place to park or just sit and enjoy the pans. No birds in sight.
The disappointment was that we were told at the gate that a lot of “locals” were inside. There was one look out point and there were probably 50 people there. It was a massive party of people falling over themselves with bottles of alcohol in hand. It was such a disappointment as we were made to feel unwelcome and unsafe. We left. So in and out of the pans in less than an hour. P300 poorer and we can claim we have seen Sowa Pan. Not a pleasant experience at all.

We saw some Wildebeest, which you can see anywhere.
I will never go back to the Bird Sanctuary, but if you go, then go in the week as I assume weekends are reserved for parties.
Last I was there it was February 2019, less summer rain and in the week. Lots less people, less water and a lot more birds.

placeInfo.name: Makgadikgadi Salt Pan
placeInfo.locationString: Makgadikgadi Pans National Park, Central District

title: True African experience
text: Until you have been there you will not be able to understand what is so special about this place. I’ve seen it bone dry where you see kilometres of flat sand and I’ve seen it looking like a brown ocean. Both are special and it is totally worth stopping off for a visit. Beware the sand! It may look dry but you are likely to get stuck!

placeInfo.name: Makgadikgadi Salt Pan
placeInfo.locationString: Makgadikgadi Pans National Park, Central District

title: Great spot. For the landscape. Less for wildlife viewing
text: The landscape itself is the highlight here. Ofcourse you get to see quite a bit of animals, but in those 4 days we didn't get to see any cats for exemple, so it's no Moremi or Chobe.

placeInfo.name: Makgadikgadi Salt Pan
placeInfo.locationString: Makgadikgadi Pans National Park, Central District

title: Peaceful break away
text: Mokolodi is a peaceful place to spend hours in nature. The restaurant is amazing and you can just sit and watch the animals or even an amazing sunset. So many things to do. The snake park and animal sanctuary is also a very impressive visit. Definitely the place to go to recharge your batteries and appreciate nature.

title: Extremely uninteresting
text: Exactly one year after the first time, we came here again since our year pass was coming to an end. This time we saw literally not one single mamal in the whole park! And again we couldn't believe that they mark SOME roads as 4x4 only. We drive a brand new Landrover and I can assure you you need a 4x4 for every single road in the park. And moreover they are so badly kept that you get scratches all over your car from branches and risk punctures at all the stony roads. And for what doing the trouble: there is nothing to see. We did hear the restaurant isn't bad but it was closed so we could not try that. But the "park" itself is rubish. Utterly rubish. Please note that I have written 2700 reviews here and there aren't many with a 1 dot note.....

title: Business meeting - Lunch
text: Mokolodi is the perfect place for one-on-on Meeting interventions. Staff are super great and the food absolutely great and good value for money.

title: Excellent camping stopover
text:
We camped here on our final night in Botswana. We didn't do any game drives as such as needed to set up our camp, but we did see quite a few animals just going to the camp site area! 
The staff at reception were just so so friendly and helpful. we had to have our covid tests printed and they sorted for us, and also had a connection at the labs, as one of our tests hadn't come through, and they called them, after hours to assist us. They really went out of the way to help us.

The campsite was clean and rustic. lovely stopover if you need to stay in Gaborone.

title: Good Place
text: A good place to spend quality time away from town, i recommend to family and friends, the roads are not as good though

title: Awful roads and no animals
text: We spent a full day driving all of the roads, seeing very few animals (yes, including birds) but what bothered us the most was that the roads were so terrible that even in our Landrover it was bad. Even roads not indicated as 4x4 roads exclusively were effectively only good for 4x4, and it was a nice dry day. So bushy you cannot see an impala if it's 3 meters far... And we have driven all over Botswana so we can compare! We only saw 7 species of mammals and to drive yourself you need to buy a year permit of 700 pula. Seeing the cheetahs and reptiles costs extra.

title: Atzaro Okavango Camp
text: We stayed at Atzaro Okavango Camp, an African Bush Camp lodge. The lodge was absolutely Magnificant!  My photos don’t do it justice!  Our villa we shared with friends was Huge. We each had a 30+ foot living space of our own joined by another “relaxation” sitting area. We had our own lounges and plunge pool. The common areas, food, drink options, and staff were all Outstanding!   We couldn’t have been more pleased !!!

title: Eerily worth seeing and impressive nature paradise
text: We spent a total of 5 days in two different accommodations on the Okavango and were able to fully enjoy this wonderful landscape during this time. Both on land there were many animals, but even more numerous were the birds on the river. We took part in several boat tours where the guides showed us many, many different bird species.

title: Magical Okavango Delta
text:
Just came back from amazing Botswana. I joined Acacia Africa's Delta, Chobe group on its 6th day when it reached Victoria Falls from Pretoria and before it left Zimbabwe for Botswana.  This review will cover only the last 7 days of the tour.

There were 7 of us (plus our driver/guide, Sam), consisting of a young couple from New York City, a group of 4 adults from Brisbane, Australia, and myself (from San Diego, California).  From the very first day together, we congealed as a group, very much like a family on a road trip to somewhere exciting, bound to have fun.

Our guide, Sam, was the best ever. He was an open book and we enjoyed and appreciated his candor. This led to us doing the same as we learned of various cultural practices where we grew up. It cultivated a sense of camaraderie, familiarity, caring and concern for one another.  We grew fond of one another, in spite of our inherent individual traits.  We had some profound discussions because we accepted that we were all intelligent individuals. And this sentiment remained throughout the next 7 days in Botswana.  In short, I would define our small group "perfect," which is unique because very few tour groups can be that due to diverse and sometimes conflicting, personalities. I know because I'm an avid traveler and have a healthy touring experience with groups.  Also, prior to joining the Botswana group, I was with another that started from Cape Town, through the garden route all the way to Greater Kruger.  There just wasn't any basis of comparison between these two experiences.

Sam made sure that we knew what we were doing at all times, and we were not exposing ourselves to danger.  He was protective all the way.  For example, he was willing to abandon his morning shower, for instance, wrap a towel and run out, just to stop me from exploring the bush around our last lodging.  That would have been very embarrassing.  Also, he delivered my dinner when I couldn't join the group due to a severe eye allergy, in spite of me telling him that I could fetch the food myself.  Since I got lost twice returning to my lodge from dinner, he patiently walked me to my room to make sure I made it there.  Because, you see, I'm 78 years old and my faculties, he surmised, were kind of defective due to age.  Of course he was wrong on that aspect.

Our wildlife explorations were ample and thrilling, which included the Chobe National Park, sunset boat ride on Chobe river, bush walks, Khama Rhino Sanctuary.  What took my soul, really was the Okavango Delta.  Though we roughed it out there a bit (slept in a tent one night, were told to keep the tent zipped the entire night and that under no circumstances do we get out of the tent at all).  Needless to say, I didn't get a good sleep that night.  But it was a notable experience.

The delta gave me a very distinct impression of the waterways and being transported by the polers from one end to the other, the surrounding serenity was etherial, a slice of heaven.  I took out my cell and typed up a poem to memorialize the almost sacred perception. I read the poem to the group after our last dinner together. Two nights prior, I think, I had the honor of reading 2 of my other poems to which they patiently listened.

Altogether, I believe I came out a better person with my communion with the delta and Botswana, thanks so much for the small family that adopted me and Sam who took really good care of us.  They are all my best friends forever, having shared a very special time together.

Profound gratitude to Acacia Africa for a well-tailored tour that showcased the gifts of Botswana to mankind, and for assigning Sam to sentinel our small group.  Heartfelt thanks to Sam for announcing that our group was the best among the 12 he had run for 2024.  I told him he would NOT likely have a better group than us, ever.

Cheers to Acacia Africa and Sam.

Maria Snow

title: The creator of the entire Delta
text: Obviously, the Okavango River is responsible for creating the Okavango Delta.  But the river itself is worthy of special attention because of all of the animals it draws during the dry season.  We were there in late May, at the end of the dry season before the rains came and surprising, the river really the only place where one sees water...the delta is completely dry (especially this past year).  So regardless of season, the Okavango River does not disappoint with all of the animals that it draws as well as its own natural beauty.

title: Must see
text:
Amazing pure nature, untouched and beautiful wildlife. 
Mokoros are used for transportation, pole drivers are experienced and nice.

title: A ne pas rater
text: Magnifique. Glisser sur l'eau dans un mokoro avec des animaux sauvages (Éléphants, Hippopotames, Crocodiles,..) est extraordinaire !!!

title: Ride the boat
text: What a fun experience to take a boat safari through the delta.  Definitely recommend the opportunity as you will experience more wild life than you can imagine...

title: What Botswana is known for and it doesn't disappoint!
text: We explored the Okavango delta with Sanctuary Stanley's camp during the dry season (late May) and I have to say that the Okavango delta is one of the most beautiful areas I've ever seen in Africa.  The amount of animals and the variety of animals that we saw...well, we've never seen that many before.  And when the rains come and the entire area floods, the same animals are still there, but their habits/behaviors change accordingly.  So we're longing to go back during the rainy season to see the entire area again...but completely new to us again.  Definitely go here!!

title: Three nights at Kwara Camp
text: Three superb days and nights at Kwara Camp in the Okavango Delta. Excellent amenities, wonderful staff, astonishing wildlife. Highly recommended. One of the best places in Africa.

title: Wow
text:
Stayed here for 2 nights as part of an Intrepid trip. Have to say this surpassed my expectations. We were taken to the campsite in Mokoros by local villagers. Once there the campsite was set up for us.
We done bush walking where we were taught about the local wildlife. Right outside the camp site we had elephants and hippos. On the first night I was awoken by elephants passing through the camp!
We bathed in a natural pool right by our camp.
This was definitely the highlight of my 2-week trip.

title: We explored Okavango Delta with Sekanka Safari
text: We spend a good week on a safari starting from Maun, ending in Kasane / Victoria Falls and loved the Xakanax area in Okavango Delta most. We opted for a tailor-made tented safari through a company called "Sekanka Safari", which we can recommend to anybody looking for a small safari company to take their family on the safari of a life time. When looking at safaris we wanted to do it all: Okavango Delta, Chobe, Moreni, Savuti, Victoria Falls, under the assumption that the areas would be very different from each other. In reality they are not that different allthough we found the Xakanaxa area to be the most stunning of all. We were very lucky with the game sights we had there and perhaps that helps with our preference for that area. After those, the game ride in Chobe felt very average. I know that sounds very snobbish but after such an amazing start of our safari tour I guess we were spoiled ;-).

title: Spectacular
text:
Amazing experience to spend 2 nights in the delta. No facilities in this untouched area.
The campsite was enough for a group, able to swim in the river (with no chemical used), had bush walks, studied tracks etc.

title: Amazing wildlife
text:
The Delta has been on my wife’s bucket list for many years, so it was great that we finally managed to visit and, due to covid, with relatively few tourists. Apart from our game drives, we decided to take a scenic flight (with Major Blue Air out of Maun) which is great if you don’t mind a small plane and a few bumps here and there. 

We saw all of wildlife we’d hoped for as well as getting a much better appreciation of the delta.

title: Imperdible
text: Experiencia para al menos una vez en la vida, aunque no hay mucha agua. Puesta de sol, preciosa. Muchos insectos.

title: Captivating
text: What a treasure the Okavango is! Serenely beautiful and peaceful,  and truly something to protect and enjoy.  Lots of animals and birds to see, gorgeous landscapes, and unforgettable experiences. Protect it!

title: Assolutamente da fare
text:
Bellissima esperienza, abbiamo sorvolato il Delta Okavango, tanti branchi di animali, scenario molto bello , ogni angolo é un paradiso, una distesa a perdita d occhio, consiglio, dopo questo abbiamo navigato con la lancia Mokoro sul Okavango, abbiamo fatto un trekking di 10 km all interno , tanti ippopotami, zebre, impala e ci siamo fermati a dormire nelle tende, un esperienza da provare , solo
grilli e il suono degli ippopotami.

title: Awesome safari
text: Game drives and boat rides with experienced guides made the entire experience an unforgettable trip.

title: WATERY WORLD WITH ANIMALS
text: An amazing place. Lots of animals to see. There were several people in our ad hoc group. We had a tour guide with us who was knowledgeable and spoke three languages,  including English.

title: Hope to come again…
text:
So diverse… from speed boat to mokoro to game drive to horse riding. And a few more….
And it changes, too. So different in Summer or May-July…

title: Nowhere better to go on Safari than Botswana's Okavango
text: The best safari experiences in Africa can be had in the Okavango Delta. Doesn't matter if you are at a lodge, mobile luxury camping or basic camping, canoeing or horseriding, you'll have the best time of  your life.

title: Anyone seen an elephant?  Here they are!
text: More animals in one day than you can imagine!  Botswana is the best!  We were with Arifcan Bush Camps.  Superb!

title: Serene, enchanting place.
text: It was amazing to be in such a special place that changes so much based on the annual rainfall and water seasons. Quiet, peaceful, solitary. The stars were amazing!!! It was so fun to camp out in the bush, listening to our neighboring hippos! The makoro rides are fun and the walking safaris were humbling and insightful, though the animals kept their distance. We barely entered the edge of the Delta and would love to go deeper into the Delta next time. Our experience was heightened by the possibility that the Okavango might not get water forever because of increased water consumption in Angola. This amazing place will be greatly changed, so go there now before its gone. Botswana is an amazing country with friendly and welcoming people!

title: TOP
text:
Uno de los lugares mas especiales donde he estado. Cuando Estuve yo, no estava Nada masificado. 
Vimos leopardos, guepardos, elefantes, 18 leones juntos, hipopotamos, cocodrilos, ….todo lo que nos habiamos programado. 
Me guataría volver.

title: One of the natural wonders.
text: This natural wonderland must be on every nature and wildlife travellers bucket list. The rainwaters flow from Angola through Namibia into this pan shaped delta into a myriad of lakes and bull-rush waterways.

title: Letaka Safaris - mobile tented safaris
text:
We had an absolutely amazing six-night journey from beginning to end. Mmegi met us at the airport and took us to our pre-Safari destination, then collected us again and made sure we were airport ready… we arrived in Moremi for a 3-night stay, and was met by our guide Lucas, it was nonstop excitement from the word go, we saw a small pride of male lions before we even arrived at camp. and then drove to Khwai for 3 nights. Each location had a different feel and experience. Our Guide, Lucas was extremely knowledgeable and did not pass an opportunity to turn every occasion into an adventure, from spotting and chasing a leopard, not just once but twice! To leading us to a herd of 500+ strong buffalo, not once but twice, different locations. We saw elephants, lions, loads of hippos, giraffes, zebra, warthogs, wildebeest, African wild cat, wild dogs, Lechwe and a rare buck called the Roan antelope. Not to mention all the birds, flora and crocodiles. The best part was 2 mating African rock pythons. The river Mokoro excursion in Khwai was just awesome. I also enjoyed the walking safari.

The camping facilities are fantastic, way more than I expected. Elliot delivered a warm shower to our en suite every afternoon and warm water to wash with every morning. Elliot and KT had our tents and camp cleaned and we had a surprise towel origami on our beds every day. This all in the African mobile bush safari!

Now the best part !!!! the food - was astounding what they created on a camp fire. KK cooked up three course dinners every night plus lunches and warm breakfasts. A freshly baked cake every day, felt like I was in a 5-star hotel! Lucas had wonderful stories to share at dinner time, and around the campfire.

Coming back from a drive, we would be welcomed with a welcome song, smiles, a cold drink and warm washcloths for our faces and hands. And a blazing campfire.

Mmegi made sure we were covid tested before we said our goodbyes…

Thank you Letaka Safaris and team for sharing your playground with us for a short while…

title: A massive water expanse - a must to see
text: We spent 3 nights on the delta camping and doing a mokoro ride. This is a must experience.  The size of the delta is not easy to comprehend. It is worth doing a helicopter flight over the delta as well as boating.

title: Great sightings of lions, leopards, wild dogs, elephants
text: Our guide Monti from Xakanaxa Camp was able to find 4 of the big 5 on our game drives in the Moremi Reserve,  Camp is very comfortable and the food excellent.

title: Well worth the trip to Oodi Weavers
text: What an amazing place. Fantastic to see real craftmanship and artistic talent.  We enjoyed the trip and are pleased to support this great co-operative

title: Stress free fly-In safari from start to finish
text: Due to Joshua we had an excellent experience arranging our fly-in safari, thanks to his great support we received throughout the planning process. Everything was handled with care and attention, making the entire experience completely stress-free. Communication with him was always prompt, clear, and professional, and we felt very well looked after at every stage.

placeInfo.name: Lelobu Safaris
placeInfo.address: Maun  Botswana, Maun, Ngamiland East Botswana

title: Beautiful Botswana!
text: Our family of 8 had a wonderful wet-season safari in Botswana, on a trip arranged by Anton Vosloo of Lelobu Safaris. We were met at the Maun airport by our amazing guides, Master and Joseph, and promptly set off on 9 days of mobile safari, breaking it up in the middle with 3.5 days at Oddballs' Camp, for walking safari and mokoro trips, including to the Xaxaba village. Our camps, on the  Boteti River, in the Central Kalahari Game Reserve, in the Okovango Delta and near Khwai were beautiful, private and safe. All were super well supported by the camping crews. The wildlife was just amazing -- herds of elephants, impala, kudu, zebra; groups of hippos, bushbuck, giraffes; occasional lions, jackals, wild dogs, hyenas, bat-eared foxes and an aardwolf; birds including bee-eaters, weavers, hornbills, cranes, storks, and the iconic lilac-breasted rollers; the list is so long. Being the rainy season, we had much of our first stops to ourselves. Also being the rainy season, plans had to be adjusted due to conditions, and the Lelobu office supported our trip really well, including arranging a last-minute charter flight to get us to our flights when the roads went south. That was extra expense, but was fully covered by our travel insurance. We came home deeply impressed with Botswana, its wildlife, its landscape, its people and its culture.

title: Excellent Company
text:
Lelobu Safaris was everything I hoped for and more. From the moment I arrived in Maun, everything ran smoothly. I was greeted at the airport by friendly staff, Moss, who made me feel welcome and safe. Communication with them before and during the trip was easy and professional. She helped me get cash at the ATM and even helped me with wifi and connecting my phone. She walked me to the bush plane company while I waited for departure. Lelobu was highly communicative by email, very professional, and very thorough in their paperwork. I didn’t know where I wanted to stay and they recommended Oddballs and it was a perfect match for what I was looking for.

At camp, everything felt like family. The Okavango Delta was literally in the “backyard,” and the staff treated us as if we were part of their home. My guide, Richard, was exceptional. He grew up in the Delta and his knowledge of the land, wildlife, and traditions was invaluable. He was respectful of the animals, patient with questions, and genuinely kind. He also went out of his way to engage with all guests, even those he wasn’t assigned to guide.

From mokoro rides among hippos and elephants to bush walks spotting giraffe and zebra, every moment was incredible. The food was fresh and delicious, and the entire experience felt authentic, respectful, and deeply connected to nature. I felt safe, cared for, and immersed. I would return in a heartbeat and recommend Lelobu to anyone seeking a truly special safari. Reach out to them to plan the trip of your dreams!

title: Victoria Falls/Chobe/Okavango Delta Adventure
text:
Lelobu Safaris helped us plan a perfect African adventure (Zimbabwe/Botswana)!   We had some very specific areas we wanted needed to explore.  Joshua Vosloo of Lelobu was extremely patient and coordinated our complex trip.  We had perfect transfers, perfect spots to stay, and extremely friendly guides and hosts.  The communication was very prompt and clear.  Our trip included Victoria Falls (the falls & Zambezi National Park) in Zimbabwe, and Chobe National Part and the Okavanga Delta in Botswana.
Our camp suggested by Joshua for Chobe was Jackalberry.   It was one of the most serene places we’ve ever slept.  It’s along the Chobe River (close to the park gate).  The staff was also extremely kind.  The ‘tent’ was nicer than almost every hotel we’ve stayed.    
Our camp suggested by Joshua for the Okavango Delta was Oddballs.   The best word to describe this place is glorious.   We arrived in a 6 passenger (including the pilots) plane on a dirt runway from Kasane.    Walked from the plane to our tent!  My God it was cool!  The camp is on the edge of water and a family of elephants was in the water a few hundred meters (at most) when we arrived.  Incredible!
Jackalberry & Oddballs were so incredible that I’m writing specific 10 star reviews for them, as well.
In summary, what made Lelobu Safaris so fantastic is that Joshua listened to what we wanted to do, coordinated the trip that we wanted, provided excellent transfers and camps, and communicated timely and effectively before and during the trip (I would recommend having WhatsApp on your phone).  The communication aspect can’t be stressed enough.  Planning a trip from 9,000 miles and 6 time zones away is stressful and difficult.  Joshua made it easy.  Call this crew.  You won’t regret it.  Visit Zimbabwe and Botswana.  It will change your life.

title: Professional Lelobu Safaris for wonderful Okavango trip
text:
After doing a lot of research for our Okavango Delta experience, we came across Lelobu Safaris who recommended a 5-day experience at Camp Khwai at a reasonable budget that we could afford. Our agent was Josh, and he timeously and accurately answered all our questions.
Finally, last week, my wife and I spent our 5 days at Camp Khwai. We would definitely return if we had an opportunity, and would use Lelobu Safaris for future safari bookings. Game viewing at Camp Khwai was coordinated by our extremely professional, informative and knowledgeable guide Emax (Emanuel). We saw a ton of game such as leopard: twice; lion (including their cubs): twice; wild dogs including seeing their cubs: twice; wild hunting dogs feasting on an impala, not to mention elephant, buffalo, hippo, crocs, jackal, hyena, etc. as well as an abundance of birds including the extremely rare Pel fishing Owl. Emax also provided lots of entertainment with his great sense of humour. Our viewing activities included a morning Mokoro ride where the canal was blocked by elephants (elephant have right of way), a day in Moremi game reserve, and a scenic heli-flight over the delta.
Our food was superbly prepared by the imaginative and amazing chef Lady G (Goitse) and her staff. The food was even more impressive and tasty given that we have a restricted diet, and the sumptuous combinations that Lady G came up with were mouthwatering. Josh at Lelobu Safaris helped with the food coordination.
Our tent was very clean and comfortable with a mosquito net for the bed, and we were given hot water bottles for the freezing nights. The solar heated showers didn’t work that well, but for us, that was a minor detail. We had a picture postcard view with the Khwai river from the balcony of our tent and saw many animals by day. At night, we had an orchestra of hippos calling, hyena cries and lion roars. Wonderful! 
The camp also advertised walking tours in its website, but in fact there are not offered because the staff aren’t apparently allowed to carry rifles. With the amount of game that we saw however, it didn’t matter. We were given a night game ride, and it was amazing to see hippos and elephant in the dark.
Thanks Lelobu Safaris for a wonderful experience.

title: Botswana Bush, Boats & Beasts
text:
Lonely Planet was impetus for inquiry at Kalahari - the idea of bush camp was intriguing, and the commentary that guides were more knowledgable than most hooked us. Reaching out via email, Lunza responded immediately and was  helpful and professional and made inquiry/booking easy. Fun to finally meet her!
Turns out LP was spot on - sleeping IN Chobe NP  is a unique experience in many ways. We drifted off to lions' roars and woke to hippo snuffles, took in the southern stars around campfire, and noshed on fabulous vegetarian & meat-based fare and MP's homemade bread. Dinners included fine enough SA red/white wine, and the bush snax and tea service were inventive and welcome!  Tents were sturdy and mobile  shower/toilet were more than adequate. 

The show stopper is, of course, the animals, birds and landscapes. Moses, our seasoned and dedicated guide, was uncannily able to make every game drive/river cruise a combo of storytelling, surprises and stellar sightings. He group up on these waters and velds, and it shows.  The best bit was the solo sunset access to wildlife-we didn't have a far drive at day's end to our bush camp, and as such  had private dusk sightings (including close up leopard with two cubs and pride of lionesses with 7 cubs standing off against two encroaching males). 

We've traveled all over the world, primarily for authentic animal and cultural  experiences, and this is as good as it gets. Ask for Moses.

ownerResponse.text: Dear Lisa thanks for the well written report ! We are thrilled you enjoyed your time with us and Mp's culinary skills as well as Moses guiding you . Please spred the word and do join us again one day !

title: Don't bother!!
text:
The drive from Nata took 4 hours, don't take to first route. Very deep sand and ruts. No problem but exhausting. 
Arrived to find the place full of rubbish and many flies that just don't leave you alone. 
The long drops have clearly had no regular maintenance as there were stacks of bottles full of urine.
Just because they are long drops doesn't mean the toilet part shouldn't be clean.
Too tired and irritating by flies to enjoy the place at all.
Noisy very large group next to us. We forfeited our 2nd night, drove to Gweta, got a pitch at Planet Baobab, lovely. No flies.  Road to labelled bad road on T4A, not such a bad road but terrible to navigate as the villagers have built over the tracks and cows have obliterated any sign of tracks.

title: Disappointing start at lookout point - didnt go all the way
text: Wanted to do a day drive to Kubu Island. Took ages in the trust office to pay and get access. The computers weren't working and we tried to help them. Then we drove to the lookout deck. This was in a shocking state of repair. The wood planks were rotten and it was badly designed so a tall person like in  our party hit his head on a part of a jutting out roof structure as you got to the top of the staircase. The road ahead looked rocky, and this first part was so disappointing we decided not to go any further. When we got back to the office to tell them we were departing there was a new Manager that we were introduced to. We explained our experience and he was surprised, but also admitted that he had never been down the road. Only been in the office. For one of Botswana main attractions this really needs to be addressed.

placeInfo.name: Kubu Island
placeInfo.locationString: Makgadikgadi Pans National Park, Central District

title: Not worth the trip just for a photo of a baobab
text: We read about a place that is supposed to be magical and beautiful. Even the journey by car over the salt pans is supposed to be very mystical. We drove almost 120 km from Gweta on a very bumpy and sandy track through the bush. You only drive very short distances on the salt pans. When we arrived, we paid €20 per person per night. The campsites, twelve in total, are all on the land side and do not have a view of the salt pans. There is little shade and there are no showers. People are also happy to do without the outhouse and unfortunately the bushes turned out to be public toilets. In the morning you are woken up by helicopters flying around Kubu Island. Kubo Island has become an Instagram hotspot that is anything but magical. It is not worth a trip for a photo of a construction site. Our return journey then took us to Nata. This route was even more terrible and bumpy than the route from Gweta.

placeInfo.name: Kubu Island

title: Tough drive to get there & back, but a Unique Place - Exclusively for Responsible & Sane Adults
text:
During our Epic Trans-Africa self-drive adventure in a 4L 4-Wheel Drive Land Cruiser with the Tent on the Roof we visited this place.

I had managed to  squeeze in a ONE night stop to capture the Sights - Complete with my Camera Gear, Lenses Combinations, Videos Cameras, iPADs, iPODs, and Tripods, I was ready for the Photo Opp to beat the band.

I did not bargain on spending 4 hrs rescuing 3 x 30 something Knuckleheads in a 2-Front-Wheel drive White Toyota, from out on the mud flats, way off the clear heavily used and marked hard surface tracks, where they had got stuck after doing some donuts. I will refrain from profanity and vulgar language, but it is tough.

Shovel, Sand Tracks, Tow Rope, Winch, Logs and Sticks were ALL put to good use. Thank you to the Aussie who came to help too - I do not recall your name.

I did not get a Single Shot of that Sunset or Night Sky, or, for that matter, a simple Thank You. Incredible, but true. Some Folks really should stay Home.

Why did I do it ? Believe me I have asked myself that Question a hundred times. Guess I must be getting soft.

Site - Cool.
Ablutions - fine for where you are.
Catering - Self
Essential - Total self-sufficiency
Accommodation - Tent and slept like an exhausted log
Driving - Tough but not impossible (we were in the Dry Season)

In Summary - rated Excellent because the experience was just that. The Rescue Issue was not Kubbu Island's fault. Just irresponsible birdbrains.

placeInfo.name: Kubu Island
placeInfo.locationString: Makgadikgadi Pans National Park, Central District

title: What a horrible place ..the worse campsite in Botswana if you can call it a "campsite"
text: I'm just absolutely gobsmacked that this place has such high reviews. People go to Botswana  to see wildlife and they usually have to drive pretty far to the campsites, This  drive took it to all new levels unforeseen for the whole world. Not only getting there really sucked, the signs   lie about how many kilometers it takes to get there (72 km sign says actually 100 km!)  and on the tire got awful drive  there was no wildlife , but just cattle and lots of it!.  I should have read the description before you attempted to get there. Seriously whose world travelers itinerary is to see a bunch of rocks and fossilized bird poop?!  No thanks so I turned around and went to another campsite more to my taste. On top of that, a friend of ours went there  and said there's no ablutions and the wind was so bad that they had to  physically hold the tent all night long  so it wouldn't blow away and there were  two strangers that drove around the camp at 2:00 a.m. in a pickup truck. This is the worst campsite of all of Botswana.  I don't get these people thinking it's good. It is God awful. If you want to see a bunch of cattle and drive to eternity for something that serves absolutely no point other than a lot of wind no water, ablutions ( not even those buckets you fill) and no toilet then to each his own

title: Magical place
text: We visited 25 years ago and were amazed again about the beauty of this utterly remote place. It is surely worth a visit once in a lifetime. Very sad that elephants have damaged some of the ancient Baobab trees. The reception area was deserted, but staff came to collect our camping fee, as we had not booked. The pit toilets were amazingly clean and water in big containers are provided for flushing. There is no water or electricity available, so you have to be totally selfsufficient. Your vehicle is your lifeline here and should be in a good condition as a breakdown may result in waiting days for other vehicles to pass. Roads are only tracks but in reasonable condition this time of the year, as many vehicles have compacted the tracks. Do NOT attemt to visit in the rainy season or shortly after when pans are tsill wet, you WILL certainly get stuck !!

title: My second great African trip organized by Gondwana Tours & Safaris
text:
For this trip to Kenya, the plan was to combine a traditional African safari with some off-the-beaten-track experience. For this, I contacted Christelle from Gondwana Tours again — she had prepared a great and perfectly organized trip to Botswana and Zimbabwe for us two years ago.

This time I wanted to combine a safari in the Masai Mara with a trip to Kakamega Forest, where I planned to focus on hiking and searching/photographing reptiles and insects. The preliminary discussions with Christelle were very useful. With her knowledge and experience, she helped me better understand the available options and what was realistically possible within the budget.

Based on her advice, I chose the North Conservancy in the Mara, which is much less crowded than the main national park and offers additional activities such as off-road driving, night safaris, and walking safaris. For the Kakamega part of the trip, Christelle found excellent guides whose expertise was perfectly aligned with my special interest — herping. She was always very responsive, positive, and open to discussing different options, and I truly enjoyed planning this trip with her.

The trip itself was great, and everything was arranged as planned. The only logistical issue was a delayed Safarilink flight from Kisumu, which caused me to miss my connection to the Mara. However, the situation was closely followed by Gondwana Tours and their partner in Kenya, Cheli & Peacock. They kept me informed of developments, rebooked the flight, and assisted me at the airport to ensure the transfer was smooth and without hassle.

The safari fully met my high expectations. Among the highlights, I would mention the night safari when we followed a pride of lions and observed their activity after dark — a remarkable experience. Equally memorable was the slow exploration of the humid African forest in Kakamega on foot, observing colobus monkeys and searching/photographing for snakes, chameleons, and insects.

Many thanks to Gondwana Tours & Safaris for organizing this trip — I hope to be back in Africa soon.

title: Un viaje inolvidable en familia por Botswana y Zimbabwe
text:
Somos una familia de tres personas (padre y dos hijas) que acabamos de regresar de un viaje inolvidable por Botswana. Simplemente espectacular! Todavía estamos con una resaca emocional y nuestra cabeza sigue allí 💔 
La verdad es que no sabíamos muy bien por dónde empezar a organizar el viaje y, después de leer varias opiniones, contactamos con Gondwana Tours & Safaris. Fue una de las mejores decisiones del viaje. Gracias a las excelentes opiniones que leímos en plataformas y foros, decidimos contactar con Gondwana, y fue todo un acierto. Muy recomendable! 

Steffi nos organizó un tour totalmente personalizado de 10 días, adaptado a nuestros gustos y necesidades. Desde el primer momento estuvo muy pendiente de que todo saliera perfecto, y realmente lo consiguió, el viaje fue rodado.

Nuestro guía y conductor fue Onx, una persona maravillosa, excelente conductor y un guía magnífico, con un gran conocimiento del país, de la fauna y con un increíble ojo para el avistamiento de animales. Gracias a él pudimos vivir experiencias únicas. No lo olvidaremos nunca y algún día nos volveremos a encontrar✨. 

Botswana nos ha impresionado muchísimo: es un país precioso y su gente es amable, cercana y encantadora. Un país que nos ha robado el corazón y que visitaremos de nuevo. 

Queremos dar las gracias a Steffi, a Gondwana, a Onx y a todas las personas que hicieron posible que nuestro viaje fuera tan especial, maravilloso e inolvidable. Sin duda, deseamos volver a viajar con Gondwana en el futuro. Con ganas de organizar el siguiente juntos!

———————

We are a family of three (a father and two daughters) who have just returned from an unforgettable trip to Botswana. Simply spectacular! We are still emotionally overwhelmed and our minds are still there 💔 
To be honest, we didn’t really know where to start when organizing the trip, and after reading several reviews, we contacted Gondwana Tours & Safaris. It was one of the best decisions of the whole journey. Thanks to the excellent reviews we read on different platforms and forums, we decided to contact Gondwana — and it was absolutely the right choice. Highly recommended!

Steffi organized a completely personalized 10-day tour for us, adapted to our tastes and needs. From the very first moment she was always attentive to making sure everything went perfectly — and she truly succeeded. The trip ran smoothly and exceeded all our expectations. 
Our guide and driver was Onx — a wonderful person, an excellent driver and a fantastic guide, with great knowledge of the country and its wildlife, and an incredible eye for spotting animals. Thanks to him we were able to live truly unique experiences. We will never forget him and we hope to meet again one day.

Botswana has deeply impressed us: it is a beautiful country and its people are kind, warm and charming. A country that has stolen our hearts and that we will definitely visit again. 
We would like to thank Steffi, Gondwana, Onx and everyone who made our trip so special, wonderful and unforgettable. Without a doubt, we would love to travel with Gondwana again in the future. We are already looking forward to organizing our next trip together!

title: Good lodges... TA communication issues
text:
Vic falls: the lodge they put me in was nice but is further away from Vic falls than most hotels. What this means is that you will get picked up first and dropped off last for group activities. This was about 45 mins for me one night to finish picking up all the other guests before the activity even started.

Botswana: Kwando is a first-rate safari and hotel operator and I highly recommend all 3 properties that I stayed at (Chobe, nxai pan, mma Dinare). The issue isn’t the property but ultimately, I didn't love the itinerary.

While I was told that it would be hot in December and recommended permanent tents, (note to others) it almost felt dangerously so. I was sick and dizzy from the heat and had to skip most of the activities to rest. Some lodges have AC but most don’t.

Also, communicating with the TA was average at best. Emails were defensive and at a certain point, I felt like I was being blamed for issues on the trip. Not exactly the relationship I want with a TA and I stopped communicating because it was unpleasant to do so. 

Communication was also disparate and not the easiest to follow. Your final itinerary will be in one online digital itinerary but certain important information will be in another file buried deep in the emails sent and sent just once.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Justine

many thanks for reviewing us and your valued feedback.
We are glad to hear that you liked the camps we suggested to you in Botswana for your safari, but are sorry to hear that you didn't love the itinerary we planned for you. Perhaps you could give us more details on this, so we can take this into consideration for the future?

Planning a safari, especially one like yours with multiple countries involved, is always complicated and involves a lot of logistics and many details. We exchanged many emails with you over many months and our quotes and suggestions were accompanied by a comprehensive digital itinerary. These digital itineraries cover many important travel related issues like weather to be expected whilst traveling, camps & their facilities, regions visiting, luggage restrictions on light aircraft's and much more.

We try and highlight as many important facts of a safari to a particular region as possible to our guests (each country has different regulations and regulations), but still rely on them to read and familiarize themselves with the specifics of their trip. This avoids issues on the ground upon arrival and whilst traveling!

We were in constant contact with you during your trip (even after hours and during the weekend), tackled the problem of your oversized suitcase at our expense and other issues. We tried to guide you as best as possible in terms of your expectation for particular animal sightings, but also made it clear that we simply can’t guarantee anything or predict nature.

We are sorry to hear that you had a very long transfer to an activity whilst being at Victoria Falls. We will flag this with our transfer company on the ground. However, transfers are usually done on shared bases (unless private transfers are booked and paid for) and unfortunately sometimes involve stops along the way to collect or drop off other guests. We try to minimize stops and unnecessary delays, but sometimes its beyond our control. Thanks for bringing this to our attention.

Once again for your feedback and for traveling with us. We value it very much.

Regards from the whole Gondwana team

title: Exciting and fulfilling time in Botswana
text: My adult daughter and I recently returned from a tour of Botswana wildlife arranged by Gondwana Tours.  It was excellent from beginning to end.  Well, not necessarily the 15-hour flight from Newark to Johannesburg, even though it was very smooth and on-time.  The Gondwana-arranged flights from Joburg into Maun, to the Camp Sites, and from Kasane back to Joburg, were all comfortable and reasonably short.  The three sites we visited were Mma Dinare, Shinde Footsteps and Bakwena.  All were excellent as the guides and staff were experienced, knowledgeable, helpful and pleasant.  The safari rides, boat or canoe rides and walks were well-planned and the animals sure showed up: elephants in the thousands (particularly in Chobe-Bakwena), lions, cheetahs, leopards, Cape Buffalo, hippos, a few crocs, and thousands of grazers (impala and zebra being most common).  At night we saw a mother hyena and her cub at Shinde.  The food and drink were very good at all three sites and they tried very hard to accommodate my vegetarian daughter.  The accommodations were certainly at the "glamping" level; nice beds, hot showers, potable water plus tea and coffee and even snacks in the tents!  I highly recommend Botswana for a tour and Gondwana to arrange everything.

Thanks Scott for your review and your kind words. Sounds like a great safari you enjoyed and we are very happy to have been part of the planning.  

Thanks for traveling with us and we love to hear from you again.
Steffi and the whole Gondwana Tours & Safaris team

title: At least one night camp is a must to get the real Chobi
text:
Incredible Safari!!

We had recently done the Serengeti and Masaai and wondered if we needed to do another safari and glad we went with our gut because Chobi was insane and so different. 

The landscape and the experience is wild and so much wildlife. Would 100% do the camp or stay in Chobi somewhere as all the action came at dawn and dusk(as you would think) and it gives you the time to really enjoy what Chobi provides for you. The camp was amazing. We went with basic camp and it was perfect. Great homey camp food, good stories by the fire, simple tents and listening to the African Lullaby to put you to sleep. The guides make you feel completely safe knowing what is out there somewhere. 

The boat in the morning with Opie was great. He helped us swiftly get through the border and provided a great boat safari, lots of knowledge, safe trip and some cool close up encounters with buffalo, hippo, Crocs and lots of intricate bird life.

Our safari guide, Godfrey, what a guy. Such a passion himself for the park and the wildlife. He saw and heard things you wouldn't even think of as a new comer to the park which allowed us to experience so much. He was also like our own personal Chobi encyclopedia and answered all of our questions above and beyond and the intricacies entwined. For example he was looking at the animal tracks In the morning to determine animal direction and where to go. From his experience the days before he knew where certain herds were. While we were stationary he could determine what were warning calls and used other animals behaviour to determine the direction of "danger" but for us "experience". This lead to seeing hippos, Crocs, all the different antelope and birds, leopards in trees and on the ground, an amazing experience with a lion pride of 3 females and multiple cubs, crazy elephant herd encounters and sunsets and meetings a few big residents like lions and also a new sub adults while also walking home with so much knowledge about how the ecosystem interacts. Even explaining how irregular it was to watch a Zebra trying to help it's mate across the river. Then to explain this indicated the mate was sick or injured and the helping zebra was overly caring given the heard was looking to move on. 

Incredible time and 10/10 would recommend.

ownerResponse.text: Thank you Sean for the fantastic report , we're thrilled you pegged Chobe up there with the Serengeti and the Mara . Op and Godrfrey are stars in our valuable team and thank you for recognising them and applauding their efforts . Do come again

title: Great Camping Safari in Chobe.
text: We just came back from a 3 day /2 night camping safari and it was great. Camping in the park is best because you get deeper into the park than day trippers. title: Amazing Chobe Safari with a Fantastic Guide!
text:
We had an incredible 3-day, 2-night camping safari in Chobe, and it was an unforgettable experience thanks to Gee, our amazing guide. Gee was incredibly flexible and tailored the schedule based on our preferences. During game drives, he coordinated with other guides to help us spot the Big Five — his dedication and knowledge really made the difference.

**Tour Highlights**
Every day on safari brought new and exciting animal sightings. The thrill of chasing specific animals across the Safari was so much fun! Beyond the wildlife, we truly enjoyed connecting with Gee, the team at Katahari Tours, and the other tour participants. It made the entire trip even more special.

**Itinerary Overview**
- 2 nights, 3 days of camping in Chobe National Park
- Day 1: Pick-up from our hotel in Victoria Falls, Zimbabwe, with smooth support crossing the Botswana border
- Morning river cruise followed by an afternoon game drive
- Day 2 & Day 3: Full days of game drives
- On the afternoon of Day 3, we were assisted again with visas and transferred to our accommodation in Livingstone, Zambia

Highly recommend this tour if you want a well-organized, adventure-filled experience with kind, knowledgeable guides!

ownerResponse.text: Thank you ever so much for the awesome review , we would appreciate you spreading the word about us and please do come again .

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

title: Frustrating booking experience and unhelpful company
text: We booked one of the Baine's Baobab campsites via their forms online and got a confirmation via email. When we reached the gate after a long drive, all the Baobab campsites were booked and we were told we could only stay at South camp which was too far at the time and less well rated. The person responsible hardly apologised and didn't offer any solution for something that's clearly an error on their side (other than staying at South camp for the regular price). Apparently an "error" that happens frequently. Frustrating experience.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

title: A special place to visit once in your lifetime
text: Driving to Baines Baobabs can be a bit challenging due to the road conditions, but once you get there you have the feeling you are the only one on this planet. No wildlife, just great views!

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

title: Standing proud on the edge of a salt pan
text:
After leaving Makgadikadi on a heavily sandy road, 20km on the A3 and we entered Nxai Pan Park. A daily entrance fee covers both parks on the same day. We didn't have overnight accommodation, all full, so would visit for the day. But there were stern warnings that Nxai Pan was tough, very sandy, 4 hours vehicle and trailer stuck the previous day so we aimed for just Baines Boabab.
The drive was OK, not too difficult. Although one stupid French man who had been driving in a ridiculous manner earlier in the day was now firmly stuck. Despite my inexperience, now 5 days 4x4 driving, I pulled him out. The boababs were excellent, standing proud on the edge of a salt pan they were huge, reputed to be over 1000 years old, excellent.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

title: Great views terrible roads
text: Baines Baobab the scenery is excellent and the toilets although long drops were good. The roads in are rutted and really filled with sand. I don’t think a grader has been there for months. If you driving a 4by 2 it’s going to be a struggle. Try entering early before sand gets hot.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

title: Definitely would not recommend this overpriced campsite full of flies
text:
We definitely would not recommend staying at this campsite. Upon arrival, we were swarmed by hundreds of flies and other stinging insects, making it impossible to enjoy the site. We ended up staying in the car to avoid them and decided it was best to leave. When we mentioned this at the entrance, there was no understanding of our situation, and we were told to find alternative accommodation, despite having paid for two days of park entry and one night of camping.
Additionally, the booking process was frustrating; I initially had contact with Godairone, who suddenly stopped responding. After a few weeks, my boyfriend contacted her and surprisingly we immediately got a reply. Overall, we really recommend not staying on this overpriced campsite.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

title: Unmissable in Nxai Pan
text:
A night at campsite no. 1: alone in the world!
Accessible only by 4x4, the road allows us to already get in the mood. 
Once you arrive, it’s magical, huge baobab trees dominate the Pan.
Campsite number 1 is magnificent and very well equipped: pitch - bush toilet - bush shower. A very beautiful baobab just for this location!
An extraordinary experience and an exceptional view of the Milky Way.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

title: If you have things to do in the area
text:
On the way to Nxay pan campsite, a visit to Baines Baobab is essential. But we’re not just going there for that.
The road is not easy and requires a very good vehicle with good ground clearance.
On site, these baobabs are impressive. The dry sides are too.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

title: Super experience but 2 nights min (pref - 3). Must be fully self-sufficient. Tough drives, but so worth it
text:
Drove here from Kubbu Island (not easy or straight forward), via Gweta (unremarkable), the A3 (dead straight & better Road surface than expected) and Nxai Pan National Park Gates - nice warm Welcome and pleasant Chap signed us in, took our Fees/monies, & directed us - Left was longer and less sand - Right was shorter but quite deep sand (Our 4x4 Land Cruiser was deemed sufficient for either option). NB. Tyre pressures.

Shorter Option was tougher but only 12 Kms, so we took it. Camp site - # 1 is Booked so we claim #2.

3 Hrs later we declare #2 a Mystery - we did not get stuck but progress was unbelievably slow. We did find #1 and with no one else in sight, we claimed it (no one ever showed-up). We never did find #2. Word of Warning we thought it incredible easy to get lost around Nxai and Kubbu - whole of Makgadikgadi Pans area.

Site #2 was stunning location, great sunset, dawn, & sunrise. Ablutions fine (for long-drop). We were a self-drive, self-catered, self-guided, & self-sufficient Team. For this Location, one needs to be. Just you the space, sky, stars, & silence. - surreal.

Loved it, but we made the mistake of only booking 1 x night. Too rushed. Commend 3 x nights minimum, then you have the time to visit Nxai Pan proper & the Watering Holes up there (to the North), where there was quite a bit of Game. we saw very little. Note that extra Drive adds about another 2/3 Hrs.

title: Zalige plek
text:
Een zalige plek om een nacht te verblijven. 
De campsites bevinden zich op begroeide eilandjes met een of meerdere baobabs midden van een grote zoutpanne.
Geen probleem want de panne is perfect berijdbaar. Wel oppassen voor stenen.
Op de campsite sta je helemaal alleen onder een enorme baobab.
De voorzieningen zijn zeer basic en je moet alles meebrengen (water, eten, brandhout). De nachtelijke hemel is schitterend!
Geniet zeker ook van de zonsopkomst.

placeInfo.name: Baines Baobabs
placeInfo.locationString: Nxai Pan National Park, Central District

title: Nxai Pan with African Bush Camps
text:
I recently spent 4 days in Nxai Pan with African Bush Camps, and didn’t want to leave. The camps are mobile: they change every week or so, and the pan, (my first salt pan experience!) was lovely. Lots of open views, prides of lions, springbok, raptors, bee eaters, bat-eared foxes and jackals. Very few other people.  ABC took great care of me: You gotta love the combination of canvas walls and compost toilets with a wine steward and remarkably tasty meals. 
My guide, Ken, was expert at spotting all manner of wildlife, wonderful company, and intuitively knew how best to position the vehicle for optimal photo opps.  I am very grateful for his good work!  I was a little early for the zebra migration:  they were arriving just as I had to move on, but this is a special place, well worth revisiting.

Ne surtout pas s'aventurer sur les pans aux alentours des baobabs en dehors des traces.

title: Once in a Lifetime
text:
AMAZING! INCREDIBLE! MESMERIZING!

While I'm sure any safari in Chobe would have been a once-in-a-lifetime experience, it absolutely exceeded my dreams. I attribute this to our guide, Leo, who made the entire trip unforgettable. Even though we visited during the off-season, the park was quieter, which actually made the experience even more enjoyable. The knowledge Leo shared with us truly set this experience apart from any others. He is, for sure, an animal whisperer. We had friends on separate safaris in Chobe at the same time, and they didn’t have nearly the experience we did.

The wildlife, of course, was spectacular. We were lucky enough to see tons of baby animals during the off-season, which made it extra special during this time of year. Leo was great at tracking all the animals. We even saw the lion king roar as he woke up for the night. 

Camping was very comfortable. The food was great, and there was even a hot shower! Jacob and MP treated us very well back at camp with hot meals and anything we needed.

I highly recommend camping, even if you're not usually a fan. Being able to stay right in the park means you don’t have to leave early, like you do with a day trip, and you get to experience things you wouldn’t otherwise, like hearing the actual Lion King's roar as he wakes up for the night! It was an experience I’ll never forget. I could go on and on about the wildlife, but you'll have to see for yourself when you do a safari with [insert safari company name here].

I’ve done two other safaris but nothing compares to this one. If you are going to Chobe...make sure you go with Leo!

ownerResponse.text: Thanks Jules for the awesome review , we're thrilled you enjoyed it so much and were fortunate to see good game in the green season . We will let the team and Leo know you enjoyed the food and the guiding , please do come again !

title: Come to Chobe
text:
Imagine sitting quietly just a few feet from a pride of lions watching them play and sleep. Then watching a large female lion stalking and attacking a giraffe. This was just one afternoon of the two night, three day tour. Other highlights included being surrounded by a herd of Cape Buffalo, seeing a new born elephant, watching giraffes graze quietly, and seeing rare birds. 

Our guide, Mr T, took care of everything. Over the course of three days we spotted nearly 100 species of birds. His knowledge of birds was extremely helpful. We also enjoyed his knowledge of the ecology of Chobe and the history of the park.

The food was provided by MP and Jacob. It was excellent. They also took care of our every need in camp. 

A brilliant experience.

title: 2 nights 3 days in the heart of Chobe National Park
text: Day 1 started with a river game viewing safari on the majestic Chobe river. At first I was excited by a few water birds, but after an hour's journey up the river into the Chobe national park, there were elephants as far as the eye could see. Hundreds of elephants! Hippos, buffalo, nile crocodiles, monitor lizards, impalas in every direction. After the river safari we were taken by game drive vehicle to the min Chobe park entrance. We had our 2nd safari of the day and saw every other animal you can think of over the 5 hour trip. Over the course of the next 2 days we did another 4 big game drives that were just more and more spectacular each time. The list is long, but we saw lions with tiny cubs, a massive herd of sable antelope, zebras, jackals, giraffes, baboons, mongooses, blue wildebeest, buffalo, impalas, kudus, warthogs - you name it, we saw it. Our magnificent guide and animal spotter par-excellence was Mr T.  So knowledgeable about every type of bird and animal. We learnt so much from him. The tented accommodation was perfect and comfortable. It's so wonderful to hear the sounds of Africa at night and wake up to the natural alarm clock of bird song. The chefs are master-chefs! Every meal was a culinary sensation, with freshly baked hot bread with every meal. I suggested that the chefs run a side-course in camp cooking and baking. I am sure there will be people interested. The whole tour was so professionally run. My husband and I had a magic time. I highly recommend Kalahari Tours. They will make your trip a dream come true.

ownerResponse.text: Dear Mayae we are thrilled you had such a good Safari with us and thank you for the lovely review! Mr T is a star and we are very lucky to have him on board our staff compliment . This time of year its not always that easy to see much game due to the rains but it seems you were really fortunate which is wonderful ! Please do come on tour with us again someday !

title: Breathtaking
text:
Moremi is a truly spectacular reserve. But not only Moremi — the Khwai Concession area is equally amazing. The amount of wildlife is incredible. We were lucky enough to see a large herd of buffalo, half a dozen lionesses with cubs feeding on a buffalo, two cheetahs (first resting and then active), hippos, hundreds of elephants, zebras, giraffes… simply breathtaking.

You can visit the reserve on your own, but it’s tricky to navigate, and if you’re inexperienced, it’s hard to know where to look. We did a guided game drive with Godfrey Tlhase from Bush Kings Safaris (bushkingssafaris@gmail.com). He knew the reserve inside out and made sure we had an amazing day. Beyond luck, it was thanks to him that we got to see everything we did.

title: Picture Perfect
text: Absolutely stunning wildlife viewing. We ticked the last of the big five here with a sighting on our last night. Great place to stay and in our tents it felt pretty rustic. Would not have seen what we did without our guides,possibly might have more photos of birds if we were on our own.

placeInfo.name: Khwai Concession

title: Very many animal sightings
text:
During our round trip through Namibia and Botswana we also spent a few days in the village of Khwai. From there, we drove through the Khwai Community Concession on two days as a self-drive. This is located between the Moremi Game Reserve and Chobe National Park. It is managed by the local community, so you don't have to pay admission. The trails were easily navigable with our Hilux with 4-wheel drive, even if it was partly deep sandy. Part of the route runs along the Okavango, which we definitely liked best. We saw a lot of animals like elephants, giraffes, buffaloes, hippos, zebras, monkeys, eagles, crocodiles,...
On the two days we saw very few other vehicles, so you had this natural paradise almost to yourself.

placeInfo.name: Khwai Concession
placeInfo.locationString: Moremi Game Reserve, Okavango Delta, North-West District

title: Wonderful but not the best
text:
Unlike the National Parks with their daily fees for international visitors entering Khwai is free. If coming from Savuti or Moremi you will have paid to access those before arriving, if travelling Maun through Mababe village you will enter Khwai no charge. I don't think this area is for first time self drivers or first time safari travellers. The game driving is not easy. It's not signposted like South Africa's Kruger, Addo etc or Namib-ia's Etosha. So to orientate your way around the various tracks, river crossings, or to avoid them, get Tracks4Africa. Just the paid Africa detail map will give you all you need. No 'directions to' option, but you will know where you are. It works on mobile or ipad android offline through satellite. 
A 4x4 is essential, some off road experience definitely preferable, deep sand, deep holes are regular, river crossings best left to those who know how. So compared to other easier national parks you will spend time watching the road and a map as well as animal spotting. And if you definitely expect cats with ease you're in the wrong place. So for beginners you may be disappointed and you shouldn't be, self drive safaris are wonderful. And while lion and leopard are hard to come by, a day here can actually seem like driving in Eden. A stunning waterway, sometimes just 10 metres wide, other times a few hundred metres of green covered wa-ter, wonderful river bank trees filled with herds of elephant, maybe 6, maybe 26, every few hundred metres, impala, waterbuck, red lechwe, zebra, warthogs in numbers in between, then hippo pools,crocodiles various birdlife and hyena. Wonderful. If you’re staying in a catered lodge then all this will be available to you without the hassle of driving or spotting, and local guides are most likely to know the regular spots for the lions and elusive leopard.

title: Chobe Crocodile Farm
text:
Entry charge to "Chobe Crocodile Farm"  was 100 Pulas and a employee guided me inside the farm that had many dogs which included a Dachshund and a Mastiff.
The employee took me to a large hallway having  Circular fish tank wells that contained crocodiles. . Was introduced to Ms  Pedzani,the caretaker and employee of this crocodile farm.She explained me the different crocodiles in different waterless tanks.One tank had only baby crocodiles which were 4 years old." Moremi" who is supposed to be over 100 years old is the prized breeding crocodile of this farm.The next crocodile of importance  " Mr Big Boy"  is the second oldest crocodile on the farm at supposedly 90 years of age. She feeds each large crocodile about 15 chickens once in three months.This particular hall exhibit of  the crocodiles was used for student and tourist tours and hence illustrations on the walls explaining the life facts of the crocodile. I was next taken to  the outdoors breeding pond enclosures which had numerous crocodiles including females guarding their eggs buried in sand.The females guarding the eggs are very protective and aggressive and one female close to the fence almost charged at me when I went too close. Ms Pedzani told me to keep my distance from the female crocodile as they can also leap a few feet and grab their  prey as seen in wildlife documentaries which in this present situation  could be my limb.She showed me the two different enclosures in which " Mr Big Boy" and " Moremi" were kept and finally while  departing met the owner, a white Botswanan  citizen Ms Sue.Slogrove who explained me her farm and the crocodile industry in Africa which is big commercial business. Her prized and most famous crocodile on her farm was " Nelson" who lived over 100 years of age expiring in 2004  and was also pictured on the stamp of Botswana.

title: Another amazing experience with African Bush Lovers
text:
Another amazing experience with African Bush Lovers. After spending 3 days with TT in Chobe last year with my partner and parents, I returned again this year with my sister for her first big trip. 

We did 5 excursions including half day boat cruise, full day game drive, Mokoro, half day game drive and day trip to Victoria falls. Yet again TT surpassed expectations as a wonderful guide and it was lovely to be joined by trainee Keto who helped us improve our Setswana!

ABL
Firstly Stanza is a very passionate person – he is a passionate family man, passionate Botswana man, passionate bush man and extremely passionate about making sure all his guests have a wonderful experience. His communication is immediate and reassuring,

Expect dust and bumps – lots of them, you are in Africa but also expect a lifetime experience.
Have a buff or mask for the dust.
Botswana is clean – NO litter
You go for the experience but it’s the people that make it special and ABL certainly did this for us.
A credit to Botswana.

We most certainly recommend African Bush Lovers and would definitely take advantage of their professionalism, knowledge and service on any future trip to Botswana.
