title: Trip Of A Lifetime, Could not have been more perfect!!
text:
An African safari has been my dream since I was a little girl and we, my husband and I,  finally decided to go for it. Due to the vast amount and variety of animals, we settled on Botswana. I went on a few Facebook groups to get recommendations on companies and right away, ABL was suggested and I had a gut feeling they were the way to go. I did my due diligence to ensure they were a reputable, licensed/registered company. Their website was filled with great information about their services and the company's founders. More on them later.  

I contacted the team, communicated what we were looking for, and got a prompt and professional response, with a suggested itinerary and cost. The itinerary sounded perfect and the price was in our budget....esp. because I was able to break it down into a few payments. I should also mention that this trip was a year and a half in the making!

Communication was always responsive between the time we booked and when we arrived. I had many questions/concerns but the team was always prompt at answering any e-mail or messages.  I appreciated that they were just as excited for us to get there as we were to go! 

We flew out of Toronto and after 3 flights, we landed in Maun on June 18, where we had a room booked at Maun Lodge for a night ( not part of the package ). 2 members of the team, Joel and IT,  picked us up and dropped us off at the lodge, where our stay was a very pleasant one. The following morning, we were picked up and were ready to begin our adventure with African Bush Lovers. 

Our first stop was Hide Inn Campsite for the first 2 nights. Upon arrival, we met the Maun team, IT, Joel, Lee, Ronnie, and Reginah.... all warmly welcoming. We then settled into a really beautiful and comfortable luxury tent. Hide Inn has a bar where we were able to purchase drinks and enjoy the surroundings. Of all the animals I wanted to see, a Lesser Bush Baby was top of my list and IT spotted one the first night and I was able to snap a picture!! My excitement was overwhelming and the team was excited for me, too! Our trip was off to a great start!! I also very much enjoyed all the birds around camp as I am a bit of a bird nerd. 

The food!!! OMG Reginah is a phenomenal chef, with great skill. There was not a single thing she made during the whole trip that we did not enjoy. And the fact that much was made on a campfire, is even more impressive. She even had me eating mushrooms, lol. Her soups were my favorite but everything made was great. The food was plentiful and presented with love. I cannot say enough good things about Reginah and her food!  

Day 2 was our Mokoro excursion with IT where we sailed in the Okavango Delta with a local expert. This included a nature walk where we learned about tracking and animal drippings as well as the flora. We enjoyed a delicious lunch at the end of our hike. We got to see hippos, elephants, giraffes and many types of bird. It was a beautiful day and on our way back to camp, I was able to buy a few items from the local village. We made it back to camp for dinner and some relaxation ahead of moving to a different camp in the morning. 

Days 3-8 offered us the real bush experience! We had 2 days in Moremi, 2 in Khwai and 2 in Suvute/Chobe. Our team consisted of Joel ( Joel of Africa ), Ronnie ( I would say lead hand/backbone of camp ), Reginah ( our amazing chef ) and Lee ( future guide and also a camp hand ). 

The camp was set up at each location with such efficiency and speed. We booked semi-luxury as we saved a few dollars and me and hubby were still pretty close, even if not in the same bed. Speaking of beds, the cots, mattresses, and bedding were extremely comfortable. In the back of the tent, a "bathroom" was set up and enclosed, providing a bush shower and toilet. I wasn't sure how the shower would work so I used shower wipes at first but then switched to using the shower. If you've never used a bush shower, let me tell you.....wash your important bits and hair, and don't worry about the rest. I managed to get a shower done in under a minute, lol. The bush toilet was also new to me. While we had a "seat", everything went down in a dug hole and flushing is by using sand to cover. After using it once, it's no big deal. In our tents, we were provided with ample lighting, bug spray, chairs just outside of the tent as well as a washing station which came in handy for washing our face, brushing our teeth and when needed, washing a few items of clothing. 
Each morning and when we returned to camp for the evening, we had a campfire which we thought was a really nice touch, esp in the evenings where me, my husband and Joel could enjoy a drink and get to know each other and/or talk about our adventure that day! During the night, it was pretty neat to hear lions roar or elephants communicating! DO NOT LEAVE TENT AT NIGHT!! 

We went on 2 drives per day and there was a new sighting on every drive. We saw....elephants, lions, a leopard, cheetahs, hippos, giraffes, zebras, impalas, waterbucks, painted dogs, monkeys, baboons, cape buffalo, bushbuck, steenbok, blue buffalo, warthogs, tree squirrels, spotted hyena, jackal, mongoose, plenty of baby animals and so many species of birds. As well as the listed sightings, we also saw and learned about the different landscapes, and hills of Suvute, saw ancient rock art, and my husband especially enjoyed seeing and learning about the baobab trees. Our morning drives included a stop for a snack and a coffee. To end the evening drive, we enjoyed the most beautiful sunsets while enjoying a cold beer. Joel would pick the most beautiful spots to stop with stunning scenery and often wildlife in sight.  These were some of my favorite moments. By day's end, our souls were full and our bodies tired but we were always excited to wake up early the next morning for the next adventure.  Everything we needed was provided on our drives....drinks...beer, water, soft drinks and snacks. The whole 6 days in the bush could not have been better. The adventure of a lifetime!! 

Again, Reginah and her cooking skills impressed us daily, we could not stop saying thank you. I forgot to mention to ABL ahead of time that our anniversary was on the 23rd but sweet Reginah managed to bake a cake for us in the bush on that day!! Candles included! And the cake was delicious! What a beautiful memory we will cherish always! 

Day 9 and 10. We finally got to meet Stanza, when we moved on to Kasane....he's the man we've been mostly communicating with for a year and a half. The welcome from the Kasane team office was so warm and it felt like we were almost reuniting with friends. Along with Stanza the team included Lame, Letso, Katie and Thato. We were given a gift back with hand-painted mugs. Now here is something truly special about African Bush Lovers. THE ATTENTION TO DETAIL. My mug had,  along with my name, the Chobe River on it, and my husbands had a baobab tree on his. Things that each of us loved or were very excited for. Personalized for sure. We also got shirts with our names and a map of the route of our safari as well as hats with the company logo!
 
Day 9, we had a beautiful cruise on the Chobe river until sunset, where we saw a huge Nile croc, elephants, and hippos.
And finally, on day 10, our final day, team member Letso brought us to Victoria Falls. The falls were absolutely beautiful, more majestic than I imagined and our lunch with Letso was very enjoyable. He's such a wonderful and great guy! 

Days 9 and 10, our accommodation was at Travelodge and I have to say, it was a really beautiful hotel with great staff! It was a nice way for hubby and I to spend our last moments just him and I talking about our adventure. 

Our farewell was on day 11 where we were brought to the airport but not before going to the Kazungula bridge, where 4 countries meet. Never have I been more sad to end a vacation. It truly was perfect.

Some notes:

Joel ( Joel of Africa ). What a great guy. Smart as heck, had such passion for nature and wildlife, expert tracker and when he knew there was something we wanted to see, he made it happen. My husband is into science, history, and geography and watching him and Joel exchange knowledge was really sweet to watch! His driving skills were excellent on the sometimes rough terrain, only getting stuck once, LOL. All the same can be said about IT. Both these gentlemen made quite a positive impression on us! The team as a whole had the utmost respect for nature, and the knowledge which we appreciated. 

Botswana as a country was extremely clean, IMO. DO NOT LITTER, leave only your footprints behind!

We went during the winter season. Morning and evenings were chilly but during the day, it was HOT!! Pack accordingly.  Speaking of packing, I have a few tips: Ladies....pack sports bras. The terrain is rough and you want to be secure!  I found the dry, dusty air to be irritating to my throat so I recommend bringing a mask for the drives, pain reliever, chapstick, and throat drops. Pack a winter hat and gloves for the morning drive. Bring a small amount of laundry soap to hand wash. Extra moisturizer is a must also....my skin got really dry! 

Be prepared....some of the drives from place to place are quite long, however, there is lots to see along the way! 

We are smokers and ABL was accommodating to us having smoke breaks. But please, DO NOT litter your butts. We used water bottles with a bit of water for our butts. And we did try to be respectful smokers. 

A final note about African Bush Lovers: We cannot say enough good things about this company. We were treated like family and every single person on the team was warm, friendly, professional and above all else, eager to make our trip perfect. We are very laid-back Canadians who like to joke and tease and the team joined in, making it feel more like we were with family!  This trip changed us, and brought us closer as a couple. It brought me back to my love of birding and photography, which I had been neglecting. 

If you are looking for a one-of-a-kind safari experience that is priced fair, African Bush Lovers Travel & Tours is the way to go. Our trip far exceeded our expectations and was even better than I had dreamed! 

Thank you to the whole team for giving us memories we will cherish forever!

title: What a terrific tour company!
text:
For many people, including me, a safari trip is a once in a lifetime experience.  This makes selecting a company to arrange a safari a particular type of quandary – the stakes are high, since you don’t want this one opportunity to go sour through a less than optimal tour company, and there are not many people who have used multiple companies, so there is a dearth of reliable comparison information.

That’s the situation I was in about 18 months ago, once my wife and I decided to embark on safari planning.  I looked around at various data sources and reached out to a few companies.  From the responses, I took a deep breath and selected Gondwana Safari and tours.

And what a great decision it was.  Mark Hathaway and the folks at Gondwanda were there with me throughout the planning process, suggesting a good order to the stays (which was perfect, BTW), finding the right camps to stay at, as well as a great hotel in Victoria Falls.  Mark was a terrific source of information, including the best time to go (again, seemed to be perfect timing), what to pack and take, what to tip and what not to bring, as well as providing materials that let us understand the places we were going to.

He even went above and beyond – way above and beyond – to get a personal item I left behind in Victoria Falls returned to me as I boarded my flight to Harare.  Pretty amazing.

Oh, and the safari?  My wife and I travel a lot, and I travelled even more before I met her.  We both agreed – this was the best vacation we had ever taken.  It truly changed our way of understanding the world we live in.

As I said, I have not used other safari companies, so I can’t say if other companies are as good as Gondwana.  But I really can’t imagine that any other company would be any better.

title: Perhaps an overated site, hardly any  birds - lots of wildebeest though
text: There's a lot of hype about this place before we came here. It's about an hours drive from Gweta. The name appears misleading as we hardly saw significant numbers of birds. The only ones we saw were sandpiper, ducks and about 5 pelicans far out from the shore.  One pelican made a fleeting appearance near the shoreline but not enough for decent photos to be taken. However,  we saw heaps of wildebeest on the  salt pan. The place is not busy. There are a few raised lookouts near the lake for spotting wildlife. The winds can be pretty strong here with the flat landscape,  hence you may need a jumper. Entry fee is payable. With bird migration,  bird populations here are perhaps seasonal.

title: Worth a tour
text: What’s not to like.  An organised open 4wd tour organised through the hotel. Plenty of birds including flamingos, beautiful sunset and lovely scenery. As part of a trip to the Makgadikgadi Pans it’s a no brainer

title: Disappointing Nata Bird Sanctuary
text: Really not what I was expecting. Poorly maintained, very little info at the gate and very low numbers of birds. You really have to work to see birds. It’s what you expect of Africa, but all the hype that is written on the net and in brochure raises your expectation way above what you see. A simple map handout, good signage and a blackboard with current sightings would really help with visitor experience and expectations. Unfortunately cannot see that happening.

title: Much ado about little
text: The so-called Bird Sanctuary was lacking birds. We saw a total of 6 birds in the park, which is not surprising given the dry weather. The tour through the park was therefore quite uneventful, as was the sundowner. We could have saved ourselves the trouble, as the long journey was definitely not worth it.

title: Nata Bird Sanctuary with Joy was a joy!
text: Our tour of the Nata Bird Sanctuary was arranged through Nata Lodge our guide was named Joy. He was such a good tracker and had lots of experience with birds. Unfortunately they did not get the rains as expected last year so the pans were dry which meant no water birds came when we were there. We did see the wildebeest, ostrich, jackals, rabbits, springbok, zebras and dozens of different birds.   Joy was funny, he was an expert in finding and identifying birds and he even brought tea and biscuits for us too.

title: Wonderful day out
text: This place may divide people, but we absolute loved it. They have done up the viewing hides so they are really very nice. Wildlife will depend on when you go, but in November we saw lots of wildebeest, jackals, and different birds. The horizon was pink with flamingoes and pelicans in the pan. I’m guessing some people might drive in and wonder what all the fuss is about as it’s a flat plain with some wildebeest , but if you take the time and give this community run place a chance it is really very special :)

title: Probably better in the wet season
text: This time of year the birds are few and far between.  We did get lovely shots of a mated pair of Ostrich.  The rosy spoonbills were in action as well.  We could see how great it would be here when it’s wet!

title: Brilliant place, great birds, wildlife and landscape.
text:
Always a great visit, but this time (August 2022) the pans were full of water. We've seen a few pelicans and flamingos here before, but this year the pan had hundreds of pelicans and other birds. They were fishing in large "rafts" close to shore. We also had good sightings of Kori Bustard and antelopes.

Don't use the viewing platform, it is no longer safe. But it's not necessary.

Friendly staff at the reception. We used the toilets then drove out to the pans. The roads were dry and OK (Fortuner 4x4, but a normal car is OK when it is dry).

We had excellent lunch at nearby Nata Lodge, also great place to stay.

title: Une belle balade
text:
Pas d'animaux à voir au moment où nous y sommes passé.
Mais c'est quand même sympa de se retrouver seuls au milieu de nulle part et d'apprécier les paysages.
véhicule 4x4 nécessaire

title: Winter visit review!
text: We didn't saw a single bird! No, I am exagerating, there were about 12, including 3 pelicans, but not more than that. I'm sure it's completely different in the wet season, at least, I do hope so.

title: It was awful
text:
The sign at the gate clearly says no picnics. I certainly have no issues with a picnic, but we were met at the viewing tower with about 50 people falling around with loud music and bottles of alcohol everywhere. It seems to be a good weekend spot for some of the people living in Nata for an excursion. I would not come over a weekend. It was unpleasant and considering the pans were full of water, it was the only place to get close to the pans. So we basically drove in, saw some Wildebeest on the way in, no birds and a hoard of drunk people hogging the only viewing site.
At the gate the lady did say that there were many Motswana inside, but we should try and find a spot for ourselves. I didn’t know what she meant by the comment until we reached the pans. My family were very disappointed in the experience and from arriving at the gate and leaving it was less than an hour. A waste of time and money on this occasion.

title: Visit the salt pans
text: Took an afternoon self drive with my parents and some friends. Made it to the watch tower very easily and enjoyed a lunch there whilst overlooking the salt pans. They are currently full of water so you cannot go onto them. Saw some pelicans but flamingos are not yet there due to water being too high. Also saw some wildebeest. Lovely day trip and well worth the visit if you’ve never been before. If you don’t want to drive there yourself or do not feel your vehicle will make the dirt road you can book a trip with Nata Lodge and you can go in one of their vehicles

title: African Guide Academy & Kwapa Camp - An extraordinary structure in every aspect
text:
If you are looking for an educational experience that allows you to immerse yourself deeply in nature and understand it better, accompanied by highly experienced instructors with exceptional human skills, this is the place for you.
Kwapa Camp is located in an incredible place, rich in wildlife, ideal for learning about tracks and signs, approaching potentially dangerous animals and birdwatching (on foot or driving).
The tents are comfortable and provide everything you need, the food is amazing and the setting in which you eat it is simply magical.
An experience that will inevitably remain in your heart! The only real problem is that at the end of your stay, you will have to leave a place that will leave you with a tremendous desire to return!

placeInfo.name: African Guide Academy
placeInfo.address: Rodeo Drive 1 Matlapaneng Ward, Maun, Ngamiland East Botswana

title: One of the best experiences in the African bush!
text:
Wow!  I’ve made two visits to African Guide Academy and Kwapa  training camp to learn about the  wonder that is the Okavango Delta with the best trainers - JC and Sevara. I did the Nature Guide course in 2022 and two weeks of the Trails Guide course this year.

 I researched a lot of options before choosing  AGA. The camp is on the edge of the Delta and the only other vehicles you will see belong to AGA. It’s remote - no fences to be seen and there’s plenty of wildlife in and around camp.  The staff are exceptional, the meals delicious, the tents comfortable. There is nothing finer than waking up to the dawn chorus or wondering what is snuffling past your tent at 2 am!

This year we had phenomenal elephant encounters on foot, along with buffalo. In the two weeks that I was there we also tracked lions and another group had leopard encounters. It was thrilling to learn about approaching PDAs ( that’s Potentially Dangerous Animals)  And being with a group of like minded people, those as passionate about wildlife as I am and as eager to learn, meant that we soon felt like old friends. 

AGA sponsors a local guide with each course. About half of our group of ten were doing the course for the BQA or FGASA qualifications, the others for enrichment.

I also love that AGA allows one to split the course if one has time constraints. I plan on going back for another two weeks to finish Trails Guide.   It truly is a life changing experience

placeInfo.name: African Guide Academy
placeInfo.address: Rodeo Drive 1 Matlapaneng Ward, Maun, Ngamiland East Botswana

title: Highly recommended AGA!
text:
Everyone should do their nature guide and trails guide training, it is the best experience of your life!
AGA are professional and provide the most incredible guide courses. 

Thank you to everyone who made this course possible: Aiden, JC, Bernie, Byron, Massi and Grant! Shoutout to all the wonderful staff, can’t thank you enough for the beautiful food and service: Warren, Rex, Kitso, Gotso, Shuffles and Stan! 

Felt at home. 

To our trails groups: Ntabiseng, Gee, Tom, Don, Boesi, Amos, Mr T, Chili, Kawana - miss you all!

Highly recommend African Guide Academy

placeInfo.name: African Guide Academy
placeInfo.address: Rodeo Drive 1 Matlapaneng Ward, Maun, Ngamiland East Botswana

title: Geniale Zeit im Camp Kwapa der African Guide Academy (Trails Guide Kurs)
text:
Im Sommer durfte ich zwei Wochen im Kwapa Camp der African Guide Academy oder ehemals Okavango Guiding School verbringen. Dort habe ich einen Teil des Trails Guide Kurs gemacht und ich kann nur sagen: Es war wundervoll! 
Vorab: Die schlechte Kritik, die hier super präsent ist, ist absoluter Murcks! 
Ich hab mich so wohl im Camp und mit den Trainern, Mike, Grant und Evans gefühlt! Alle drei haben eine geniale Art, das Wissen und ihre Erfahrungen zu teilen und vor allem jedem mit ihrer so argen Faszination und Neugierde anzustecken. 
Da keiner von uns Kursteilnehmern auf einen zertifizierten Abschluss hinarbeitete, wurden unsere Kursinhalte an unsere Wünsche angepasst. So konnte man jeden Tag ganz neu und aufregend beginnen, es wurde immer relativ spontan entschieden, wie der Tag aussieht und daher an dem angepasst, was in der Nacht geschah. Das war hervorragend! Und trotzdem haben wir eine Unmenge gelernt, durften viel erfahren und super tolle Begegnungen mit Tieren erleben, und auch unser neues Wissen „direkt im Feld anwenden“; es war ja trotzdem noch eben ein Trails Guide Kurs. 

Für mich war es ein absolut geniales Erlebnis, ein Eintauchen in die Natur, wie es besser nicht geht, ein Leben in absoluter Spontanität und Einklang mit dem, „was im Busch lebt“. Jederzeit wieder!
Auch das Team im Camp ist super nett und ich habe mich super wohl gefühlt, da die Atmosphäre unter einander sehr freundschaftlich war. 

Ich bin wirklich sehr dankbar für die wenn auch kurze Zeit bei der AGA. Ich werde definitiv wieder kommen, um im afrikanischen Busch meine Neugierde dieser Umgebung, der Natur und der Tierwelt aufleben zu lassen.

placeInfo.name: African Guide Academy
placeInfo.address: Rodeo Drive 1 Matlapaneng Ward, Maun, Ngamiland East Botswana

title: A bit of the history of Botswana.
text: Central square in central Gaborone with statue of local leaders who fought for the independence of Botswana. There are some plates explaining about various historical moments and local figures (men and women) who contributed to the autonomy of the country. It is worth learning a bit about the history and culture of the place.

title: Without the guides there is no point
text: Very informative. I left the place feeling I know more about the history of Botswana. The guides also brought more life to the place, and they were very good and engaging in the way they explained the history. However, without the guiding or the history learning, there really isn't much to do or see here

title: Three Dikgosi Monument
text: It is one of the few attractions in Gaborone, but worth a visit. The resident tour guide will walk you through the place as he gives you a short lecture on the history of the Batswana people. There isn't much to see and you can finish the tour in less than an hour.

title: This was my first time going to a monument. It exceeded my expectations. I left that place enlightened and knowledgeable
text: I needed a change of environment and fresh air. I hadn’t been to monuments before. The monuments are amazing. I felt more relieved and at ease.

title: Great hosting scenery
text: Great pace to have night events, we had a party to host and the space was perfect and the event went smoothly. There is something about the three chiefs at night that make them more spectacular.

title: Only 3 statues
text: Only 3 statues in the city center. There is security at the door and 2 people came to ask what we were doing there. There is some information around the statues.

title: Impressive
text: The tour guy gives the place more life and character. Was such a pleasant experience.  Would highly recommend it to anyone interested in the history of the country

title: Connect with the history of Botswana
text:
This place is worth visiting. It literally takes you back to history class. I loved the energy of the site keeper who had the whole story off head, and the energy he used to say the word Pula. When you visit Gabs, do pass by this place and connect with the history of Botswana.

May be the seats can be improved.

title: À voir absolument
text: Contrairement à la statue de Sir Seretse Khama devant le parlement du Botswana qui est inaccessible au public pour des raisons obscures, ce monument grandiose ( le seul de la capitale ) rend hommage aux trois grands personnages historiques du pays , l’accueil est agréable et facilité par la personne en charge à l’entrée .

title: 3 statues and nothing else
text: So Botswana has a great history around these 3  chiefs, but they have not put much effort into this attraction. A few dilapidated seating areas and nothing else.

title: Worth the Visit
text: Impressive monument. Got to learn how the three Chiefs petitioned to have their country outside the influence of Cecil Rhodes and his companies.  Our guide was very knowledgeable and made the experience fun. Worth the visit.

title: Excellente virée au plein cœur de Gaborone
text:
Notre guide était excellent.

Une virée en début d’après midi sous un soleil ardent, et un guide excellent qui nous relaté l’histoire du pays et la signification de cet emblème. Ces trois chefs, leur bravoures et leur altruisme. Un endroit à visiter pour les passionnés de l’histoire africaine

title: History on display
text: To be fair, there isn't much to do in Gabs. This monument is worth a visit and admission is currently free. Try to visit during a time when guides are likely to be around. I went for the first time and just took a quick walk around. There isn't much written information to tell you what you are looking at. The second time, I was with my family and a guide offered his services. He was well informed and we got a lot more out of the experience. It was a good way to learn more about the country's history.

title: A Must for All Visitors to Botswana
text: Compulsory stop for anyone who wants to know the colonial and modern day history of Botswana. The guides are knowledgeable and friendly and offer insights into Botswana history that provide context for a visit to the country and understanding its people.

title: Exceptional!!!
text:
Thank you very much Vincent and Julie for allowing us to live this extraordinary experience in the best possible conditions. Everything was simple and fluid: all we had to do was be surprised by the encounters, the beauty and the diversity of the landscapes along our perfectly balanced itinerary, and appreciate the fact that we always had the best place to sleep on the camp!
The independence and freedom that self drive offers give a unique dimension to this adventure. It is with stars full of eyes and memories full of head that we leave Botswana. Thank you again!!!

title: A great stay thanks to a TOP organization!
text:
Thanks to their experiences, their perfect knowledge and their empathy for Africa, Julie and Vincent were able to organize for us an exceptional and unforgettable SelfDrive trip of more than 3 weeks to discover Botswana, Zimbabwe, Zambia and the Caprivi Strip. Nickel on everything: the organization, the rhythm, the circuit, the routes, the choice of accommodation alternating between Campsites (exceptional for contact with animals), Lodges and Hotels, their advice, their availability, their attention.
Thanking you once again, Julie and Vincent for this stay which will remain engraved in our memories.
Annie and Philippe

title: 4x4 rental in Botswana
text:
Julie and Vincent allowed us to find a fully equipped rental 4x4 for 10 days of autonomy in the country. All discussions were very efficient and friendly and the delivery of the vehicle went very well with the rental company. We were even able to contact them during our stay. They are helpful and therefore allowed us to experience this fabulous trip to Botswana.
To contact without any hesitation :)

title: Namibie/Botswana avec Tawana Self Drive,  voyage de notre vie
text: Grands voyageurs mais très peu en Afrique, habitués de l’organisation par nous même de nos voyages, nous avons, par manque de temps, fait appel a Tawanaselfdrive pour un voyage de 1,5 mois en Namibie et Botswana. Julie et Vincent nous ont organisé un voyage qui vaut beaucoup plus que les 5 points de Tripadvisor. Nous n’aurions jamais pu faire seuls un tel programme. Tout a été parfait du début à la fin. 1 mois en selfdrive pour toute la Namibie et 2 semaines en fly-safari pour voir l’essentiel du Botswana. Et finir a Vicfalls. Même les animaux étaient tous là pour nous. Les lodges étaient fantastiques. Nous étions reçus partout comme des clients uniques. Et chaque fois que nous avions une question, Julie ou Vincent répondaient immédiatement. Quel confort. Nous leur attribuons sans hésiter la palme du meilleur spécialiste de la région. Merci encore pour ces moments exceptionnels. Vous nous manquez déjà.

title: Okavango
text:
Guides were knowledgeable
One of the cheaper options 
Saw nice birds and other wildlife
A good experience if you love birds because the ride there was bird paradise. 
The ecosystem is very interesting and beautiful 
Properly planned and proper pickup time 
They had nice vehicle for the dirt roads
Well prepared.

ownerResponse.text: Thank you for traveling with us, Jonpaul, and thanks you for the good review. We are glad to hear you fully enjoyed the experience :)

title: So good
text: It was so nice trip. So much satisfied 😌

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for traveling with us and thanks you for the good review.
We are glad to hear you fully enjoyed the experience :)

title: Great tour with great experiences !
text: We had a great tour of Okavango Delta by mokoro. The weather was supposed to be bad, but the sun was shining all day 😊. The drivers were super friendly, nice and showed us a lot and explained everything ! The Bush walk was great and we were very close to some animals !!!

ownerResponse.text: Danke für die super Bewertung und danke, dass Ihr mit Kalahari Breeze das Okavango Delta erkundet habt. Wir freuen uns zu hören,  dass Ihr random zufrieden seid und tolle Erfahrungen auf dem Tagesausflug gemacht habt.

title: Highly recommended!! Great time in the Okavango!!
text: We took the day trip with Kalahari Breeze Safari to the Okavango. We had a wonderful time. We saw hippos and the poler(the person operating the canoe) were very careful to keep a good distance making us feel very safe. We had a great picnic lunch. The broght Nando's for us. We saw lots of Impala and Zebra. There was a huge herd of Wildebeest coming through. We even had the oppurtinty to operate the mokoro. That was real fun but it was a lot harder than it looked. Maria from the office was great to deal with and answered all my questions promptly. The best part was after we were dropped off at the hotel at the end of the day an hour later someone from the office came back to our hotel becuase we left our power bank in the vehicle. I didn't even realize that I had left it there. That is wonderful that they would do this for us. Highly recommended

ownerResponse.text: Thank you Jeff for traveling with Kalahari Breeze and thank you for taking the time to share your experience online. We are glad to hear you fully enjoyed the tour and our service and hope to welcome you back in Botswana one day.

title: Very professional and relaxing day trip for solo travelers and groups
text: I had a very good time on the Okavango Delta. The tour was very well organized from beginning to end and it was all as advertised. The guides on the water were very professional and made you feel safe and comfortable and we got to see more animals than I expected. All in all a very nice experience and I can recommend this tour to everyone who wants to explore the delta in a relaxing way.

ownerResponse.text: Thank you for traveling with Kalahari Breeze and thank you for taking the time to write us a review.  We are glad to read that you fully enjoyed the experience and our hospitality.

title: Mokoro
text: From start to finish. The staff team and the Mokoro pole'ers were great well spoken, calm and knowledgeable. Great way to spend a day in the Delta

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your review, Roelof, and thank you for traveling with Kalahari Breeze. 
It was our pleasure showing you the Okavango and we are glad to hear you fully enjoyed the experience and our services. We hope to welcome you in Botswana  again one day

title: Do Not Book!!
text:
The worst experience!!! Do Not book with them. The absolute worst customer experience. They were late picking us up and blame us for not waiting for them. We waited 45 mins. After not being able to get in touch via phone nor email. Pickup time was 8:30 but their office didn’t open until 9am.  We went to the Falls in hopes of meeting them there @ 9:15 via taxi. After our return to the hotel we were able to connect to Wi-fi, we see missed calls. Once I return the call Maria had the worst attitude and blame us for leaving the hotel. Apparently the driver got lost. These things do happen but she claimed the we should’ve waited and we didn’t pay that much for the tour anyway. Zimbabwe is a civilized country and we should’ve had service to see that thru were trying to get in touch with her. She claimed she could not refund the money and good luck  with our bank getting
The money back. Please book with someone else. The worst people.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Tambera, 
again, we would like to sincerely apologise for the late pickup on your transfer. It was a major holiday, office hours were not running at normal times and staffing was low. We acknowledge that our driver arrived at your accommodation 30mins late due to a mix up of hotels with similar names and was notified that you had already left the premises in a taxi. Do note that at this point, there was ample time to get you to your activities including Livingstone Island  which was booked for 14.30pm. Unfortunatelly, our attempts to reach you via WhatsApp and Email were unsuccessful as you were offline until 13:00h. You requested a cancellation and a refund to which our reply was that Viator will be responsible for issuing out customer refunds since you booked via them. Viator will contact you concerning your refund request. We hope the rest of your stay has better experiences. Thank you for booking with us.

title: Amazing experience and amazing landscape.
text: I had an incredible time in the Okavango delta, the local guides were eager to answer any questions and teached us a lot. The unique ecosystem of the delta was espectacular, and although the tour is not focused on animal sightings, we were very lucky and got to see quite a few.

ownerResponse.text: Thank you for the good review, Javier, and thank you for traveling with Kalahari Breeze Safaris.  We hope to welcome you on one of our tours again one day.

title: Once in a lifetime
text:
Dear Mary,
A big thank you to you and your entire team for the wonderful Botswana trip we had the pleasure of experiencing with you.
You have put together 14 varied days that will simply remain unforgettable for us.
We started with 3 days in our own tent in the Moremi Game Reserve. There we spent many exciting hours with our extremely dedicated guide Clifford in the safari car, took wonderful coffee breaks and especially watched the hippos in peace. At the camp, O.G., Douglas and Matchana took great care of us and our physical well-being.
After a cozy night at the Waterfront Guesthouse in Maun we went back to our own tent, this time in the Okavango Delta. Our dear Guide Master never tired of going cross-country with us in search of lions and leopards, which we then of course also found successfully and observed extensively. On the way to our trip with the mokoros (where we went up close with the elephants) we got an insight into the original hometown of Master, which we had not expected. The wonderful places where we were allowed to enjoy our sundowners also remain unforgettable. Also in our camp we were wonderfully looked after again by the almost same squad - dear Jerry replaced Matchana well.
After that we slowly went back to “civilization”. The driver Chanda drove us calmly and safely over the Makgadikgadi Pans and Elephant Sands to Chobe. Once there, we were able to experience a beautiful boat safari and felt very comfortable at the Hotel Chobe Riverview Lodge. 
The last stop of our trip was Victoria Falls at the charming Samanzi Luxury Cabana hotel. Here we were able to relax and let ourselves be pampered after our exciting trip to the imposing Falls. The end of our trip was a romantic SunSet Dinner Cruise, where we reviewed the last few days.
We say THANK YOU for the great composition and preparation of the trip, the great team, the reliable accompaniment and the wonderful moments we were able to experience. We felt very comfortable with you, which was also due in particular to the great atmosphere in the team and the special people.

ownerResponse.text: Danke für die tolle Bewertung, Sabine und Christian, und danke, dass ihr mit uns gereist seid und wir euch ein bisschen von Botswana zeigen konnten. Wir freuen uns zu hören, dass ihr rundum zufrieden seid und Eure Safari in besten Erinnerungen behaltet. Vielleicht sehen wir euch ja eine Tages wieder in Botswana. Grüsse vom ganzen Kalahari Breeze Team

title: Wonderful day trip in Botswana!
text:
Wonderful tour, friendly and knowledgeable guides and driver!!
Beautiful way to see the Delta in Botswana!! Definitely a must do!!!

ownerResponse.text: Thank you for traveling with Kalahari Breeze and thank you for taking the time to write us a review. We very much appreciate it and are happy to hear that you enjoyed your tour with us.

title: To see wildlife more closely is to go camping near watering holes. At night
text:
Excellent trick. We are very well accompanied. The 4 people are very attentive with a lot of humor.
It is an unforgettable experience especially in the okavango surrounded by nature in the camps

ownerResponse.text: Thank you for the good review, Brigitte, and for sharing your experience and the pictures. We are happy to hear you fully enjoyed the wildcamping safari with us. Thank you for traveling with us and we hope to welcome you on one of our tours again one day.

title: Extraordinary experience
text: Very good organization, nice and knowledgeable guides , nice Mokoro ride and bushwalk! The round trip was also very eventful.

ownerResponse.text: Danke,  dass ihr mit Kalahari Breeze Safaris das Okavango Delta besucht habt und danke für die gute Bewertung. Das bedeutet uns viel.

title: Magic and authentic
text:
I took part in a 6 days / 5 night mobile safari in the Okavango Delta, through the Moremi and Khwai reserves, with Kalahari Breeze Safaris. As a solo traveller, I was able to join an existing group, and despite my inflexible schedule, Kalahari Breeze’s team was extremely accommodating.

This stay was magical, a real immersion in the heart of wild nature. The guide and all the team on site were professional, passionate and very attentive. The organization was perfect, the meals were delicious and we lacked nothing.

We had the chance to observe incredible wildlife: cheetahs, leopards, lionesses with their young, hippos, elephants... The landscapes were breathtaking. And sleeping in the open with the sounds of animals all around is an experience I will never forget.

I will leave without hesitation with Kalahari Breeze to discover another region of Botswana. A big thank you for this unforgettable adventure!

ownerResponse.text: Merci, Angélique. Merci d'avoir partagé ton expérience et d'avoir participé au safari avec Kalahari Breeze. Nous sommes heureux d'apprendre que tu as pleinement apprécié l'expérience du camping hors réseau avec nous. Ce fut un plaisir et nous espérons t'accueillir à nouveau lors de l'une de nos excursions.

title: Okawango Delta, Chobe N.P. and Vic Falls
text: What a great trip: Okawango Delta, Chobe N.P. and Vic Falls. Maria has put the trip together perfectly, organized and has responded to all our wishes. She was there for us before, during and even after the trip at any time as a contact uncomplicated. The 2 days camping in the Okawango Delta are especially noteworthy - in the middle of the wilderness with a great team who cooked and spoiled us. The transfers were very reliable and carried out by very friendly drivers.

ownerResponse.text: Danke für die super Bewertung, Katja und Martin, und danke, dass Ihr mit uns Botswana bereist habt. Wir freuen uns, dass Ihr rundum zufrieden seid und mit tollen Erinnerungen heimkehrt. Dich und Deine Familie in die Wildniss zu entführen, war uns eine Freude und wir hoffen, Euch eines tages wieder zusehen.

title: Definitely going again.
text: Maria is excellent. Great communication. Very happy. Vehicles, drivers, cooks, attendants, guides - everyone was pleasant, happy and respectful.

ownerResponse.text: Thank you for the good review and thank you for traveling with Kalahari Breeze.  We are happy to hear you enjoyed your safari with us and our hospitality and are looking forward to welcoming you in Botswana again one day.

title: In the middle of nature: Elephants in front of us, a hippo behind us! 😱🐘🦛
text:
Organisation in advance was perfect: clear, honest and reliable communication, no exaggerated promises. It has been said quite frankly that there is no guarantee to see wildlife – and it is precisely this honesty that impressed us. But what we experienced then simply flashed us!
From the first minute we felt that everything was thoughtful and respectfully planned. Our guide was not only incredibly friendly, but also very experienced and full of knowledge about nature, animals and life in the Delta. His calmness, respect for the wilderness and sense of security made us feel good at all times.

The encounters with elephants, birds and hippos were intense and genuine – not a staged “safari experience”, but pure, untouched nature. The feeling of gliding in a small mokoro through the water arms of the delta, accompanied only by the sounds of the wilderness, was indescribable. 

On the way back there was a portion of pure adrenaline:
A herd of elephants blocked our path, while a hippo made it clear that we were traveling in its territory. Our guide stayed calm, acted thoughtfully – and got us through the situation safely.

We had no expectations for this trip – and that’s exactly what made it our most intense experience here in Botswana.

Thanks, Kalahari Breeze Safaris, for this unforgettable experience! 🌍🙏

ownerResponse.text: Vielen Dank für diese tolle Bewertung, Lucie. Und vielen Dank, dass ihr mit uns das Okavango Delta erkundet habt. Es war uns eine Freude euch auf unserem Tagesausflug zu haben und gerne würden wir euch noch tiefere Einblicke in das Okavango Delta geben. Wenn ihr eine Tages wieder nach Botswana kommt, würden wir uns freuen Euch auf einer mehrtägigen Safari richtig tief in die Natur mitzunehmen. Danke nochmal für euer Vertrauen und Eure Entscheidung das Okavango Delta mit uns zu erkunden.

title: Incredible Experience! Just the Kind of Tour I was Looking For
text:
Was scouring all over the interweb looking for a 2-3 day budget (yet adventurous) type of safari in the Okavango Delta. Kalahari Breeze Safaris hit the spot! 

The KBS website was filled with a variety of tours including the 3 day 2 night Wild Okavango Camping Tour, which was exactly what I was looking for. The website also contained lots of helpful information regarding safari and upon emailing KBS, Maria was nice and communicative. Chose the credit card option to pay, which was seamless. 

Our guide was Master and sure enough was a master of his craft. We literally saw every animal imaginable including 4 of the 5 Big 5 (except the Rhino). Also the never seen so many colorful birds in my life. If you think a Cardinal is pretty, wait till you see a Liliac - Breasted Roller! 

The campsite was fully equipped with humoungous tents for the safari participators and a friendly support staff led by Jerry, who were on top of their game attending to the needs of us including making amazing food. And there was a lot food! It honestly felt like royalty. 

One more thing I loved about this safari is that the location of the game drive was in its own concession area meaning there were only a few safari trucks roaming around. It felt like we had hundreds of thousands of acres to ourselves! 

Truly an unforgettable experience!

ownerResponse.text: Thank you for the review and thank you for entrusting us with your Okavango Delta Safari. We are happy to hear you fully enjoyed the wildcamping experience and that our services exceeded your expectations. We hope to welcome you in Botswana again one day to show you more of our beautiful country.

title: Serene and wonderful delta - but beware of sunstroke on the nature walk
text:
The canoe ride was beautifully serene and the totally unspoilt delta was absolutely lovely. We saw loads of birds and quite a few animals (hippos, wildebeest, warthog, zebra, mongoose, antelope, etc.) too, a huge bonus. A wonderful experience. The picnic lunch provided was very good and welcome after all our exertions.

However, it didn't start well when there was a miscommunication between office and driver/guide (Craig) about pickup location of the 2nd guest to be collected, resulting in me having to sit waiting in the vehicle for 45 mins (having got up early for the 7.15 am pickup) before he finally found out where to pick her up. We ran 35 mins late for the next two pickups and the guide did not even apologise to anyone. 

Then, along the way, he informed our group of 6 Europeans that we would be able to choose how long the bush walk would be, from 30 mins to 1 hr. 30, depending on how we felt. However, when we arrived on the island after the canoe journey, the local guide/poler (who called himself X-tra) took us on the walk and gave us no choice - he walked painfully slowly for a total of 1 hr . 45 in the blazing sun with no shade, in the heat of the middle of the day; it was 34 degrees and although I had a hat, suncream, lots of water, I got sunstroke and felt very unwell. It was the first time in my life experiencing this and I've visited around 60 countries, many of them very hot ones. Although I had to sit on the ground a couple of times when the guide stopped to tell or show us something, as I thought I might faint otherwise, and when he asked if I was ok as I seemed tired, I informed him I felt a bit faint with the heat, but he took little notice and made no accommodation. I was unwell for 24 hours, still feeling faint and nauseous on my 17-hour plane journey home the next day.  (I learned afterwards that it's a dangerous, emergency condition that can even lead to brain haemorrhage in severe cases.) I think the length (and maybe the timing during the day) of the walk should be adjusted for the conditions, and welfare of the clients should be taken into account - particularly looking out for signs of sunstroke/heatstroke on hot days.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your feedback.  We apologize for the delay during the pick up. Sometimes clients change their accomodations and only notify us when pick ups are already underway.  When this happens, the other pick ups scheduled on that day are affected and minor delays can be expected. 
Unfortunately, Botswana being a desert country the temperature that you experienced on your walking safari are our normal daily temperatures. We do our best to inform travelers in advance of what conditions to expect and how to prepare and we supply unlimited drinking water to try and mitigate the effects of severe heat exposure. Regarding the duration of the tour we get contrasting feedback from clients with some arguing that the walk is too short and others preferring a shorter duration.  We try to meet our customers halfway on this issue and each client has the option to stop the walk at any time on request. We also forwarded your feedback to the community trust and hope this will lead to future travelers having a better experience.

title: Pure nature, plenty of time for wildlife watching and photography
text: We came across Kalahari Breeze Safaris while looking for an Enterprise that offers safari tours in the Okavango Delta. Even if the design of the homepage still has room for improvement (as of September 2025), all useful information for a camping safari tour can be found on it. The quick and competent feedback from Maria on our requests, however, has already given us the feeling of having found the right Enterprise in the travel preparation. That's how it was. Maria warmly welcomed us at the airport in Maun. Also during our safari tour Maria kept asking if everything was okay. Our guides Master and Clifford are very close to nature themselves and enabled us to make great wildlife observations again and again. Their enormous knowledge of nature and the ecological contexts has always amazed us. Her understanding of photographers is also commendable. They parked the safari car in such a way that the animals were not disturbed, but so that the light for photographing looks optimal. Hats off, I hadn't experienced anything like this until today. The great crew made sure we didn't miss anything while camping. The food was very delicious, I didn't expect to be cooked out in the bush so varied, e.g. that there is lasagne. The tent, camp bed and the rest of the equipment were very new and functional. We didn't miss anything. Big praise to Mary and her staff, we would like to get back on the plane and go on tour with you. Thank you for everything!

title: Bird viewing is great, the walk was interesting, just hope you have a seat so you can relax during the ride.
text:
It was an interesting experience that I'm happy to have participated in once but doubt I would do again. My experience may have been different if I had a seat, however they ran out and I had to balance cross legged for the whole duration. 

It was great to see the wildlife from low viewpoint. The birds were absolutely spectacular. 

The bush walk was fascinating once the guides spoke to the whole group and not just 1 participant. Bravo in particular was very knowledgeable with tracking, animal behaviour and local plant uses. Lunch consisted of a cold Burger and salad from a local take away chain. 

Be prepared for a very fast and bumpy trip to and from the Mokoro station.  Also make sure you take provisions in case you need to go to the toilet as it's bush only - which is fine - just lucky I had provisions on me as I was unaware.

title: Very special Safari experience
text:
Overall, we were really impressed by Kalahari Breeze Safaris.
 
We booked early so were able to fully tailor the trip, and found Maria really helpful in putting together an itinerary which we loved, providing lots of info and advice based on what we said we wanted to see.   
 
We joined together a 3-night camping trip in the Okavango Delta with a further 4 nights camping in Moremi and Savute, ending the trip in Chobe and Victoria Fall.  We loved the wild camping and found it a highlight to sleep in remote locations amongst the wildlife. Although Maria stressed it was not a luxury safari, it was certainly not a budget experience, with nice large tents, comfy camp beds, ‘en suite’ toilets and good, filling food 3 times a day cooked on the campsite fire, or in an adapted oven buried in the ground.   The food was really good – and they had no issues with providing gluten-free alternatives for my wife throughout the trip.
 
We had two guides during the trip – Master in Okavango and Clifford in Moremi/Savute.  We found both to be excellent and really friendly – we loved listening to their stories around the campfire at the end of the day.  Both guides were local to the area we visited with them and their knowledge of the area and the behaviour of the animals really added to the trip.  We couldn’t complain about the animal sightings, including a couple of close encounters with Leopards, a pride of Lions fighting over a recent buffalo kill, and a rare sighting of a Brown Hyena. 
 
We found the lodges in between the camping trips to be of good quality and very comfortable. The final part of the trip in Zimbabwe was handed over to a local tour company and they provided equally good service.  In particular, we found our guide who took us around Victoria Falls park to be really friendly, and knowledgeable which helped make that part of the trip really memorable. The lodge in Zimbabwe was the best of the trip.  
 
We booked from the UK but picked a local safari firm as we believe it is better to contribute to the local economy, rather than go through a third party.  We are really glad we did as they were helpful throughout – answering my many questions before we left, providing a lot of guidance throughout the booking process and when we arrived in Botswana. 
 
Botswana itself is a lovely country with very friendly people, loads of wildlife and stunning scenario.  We will definitely we going back, and will not hesitate in using Kalahari Breeze again.

ownerResponse.text: Many thanks for traveling with Kalahari Breeze and for taking the time to write us this wonderful review. We are very happy to hear you enjoyed your safari and our services. It has been our absolute pleasure taking you on this journey and our whole team would be more than happy to see you again one day! Thank you again for entrusting us with your Botswana vaccation!

title: Amazing camping safari
text:
We did a fantastic 4 nights/5 days mobile safari from Kasane to Maun with nights and game drives in Savuti,Khwai and Moremi. Everything was as we had discussed and organised with Maria!  Our tour guide Clifford was amazing! He was very professional, knowledgeable. informative and very helpful throughout our tour. The games drives each morning and evening were great: most of the credit for this was due to Clifford. He was relentless in his pursuit of interesting sightings but at the same time was completely respectful of the wild life and their environment. He was always mindful of our comfort and our safety. We saw so much wild life! Lions, Lionesses with their cubs, cheetah, a rare brown hyena , elephants with their young, beautiful bird life and much much more. It was for us a truly unforgettable experience.  On our arrival at the first nights’ camp site we had been welcomed by Jerry, OG and Douglas with a welcome song. They had prepared a very comfortable camp - lovely big tent with comfortable camp stretcher beds, attached bathroom and warm shower. Our versatile cook had prepared the first of a series of delicious meals and after we had dined in the most amazing surroundings we retired to be warmed and mesmerised by the flames of the fire..Each of the camp crew were at pains to make our camping experience just that little bit more comfortable, interesting and enjoyable - including an impromptu and lovely choir performance around the fire one night.
Wild camping was an intense and amazing experience - perhaps a little bit more intense than we had prepared ourselves for. We had fallen asleep to the sound of lions roaring in the distance but were awoken by the crunching of bones as a pride of lions devoured a kill just outside the camp. Now, in hindsight, one of the more memorable experiences of the tour, and one we would not wanted to have missed! Thanks to Clifford, who with his obvious knowledge and wide experience, gave us great confidence in our safety at all times throughout the tour!
Thank you to OG for the delicious meals! Thanks to Jerry and Douglas for your wonderful hospitality! 
We have nothing negative to say about our booking experience and our tour experience with KBS.
I would thoroughly recommend booking a  tour with Maria and her team.

ownerResponse.text: Thank you for the wonderful review, Monica and Brian. Its been our pleasure taking you on the crosspark wildcamping safari and showing you a bit of the beauty of our country. We are glad to hear we created beautiful memories. Thank you again for traveling with Kalahari Breeze.

title: More respect for animals
text:
We were really looking forward to this tour. 
Staff and supply was good.
Everyone involved was very friendly. 
We would have been very pleased.
We stopped our tour because we couldn't stand the handling of the animals.
You could see all the animals from the car very well and take photos. 
Unfortunately, the animals were followed so massively and disrespectfully when they wanted to withdraw.
At the same time, there were always another five cars in the park, so an agreement took place between the drivers.The animals were fed in from several sides to be able to take a good photo. 
This was unbearable for us personally. 
To the driver we have done our opinion on this kind child.But.....
It was not an easy decision for us to drop out, because it was all very expensive, of course. 
We would like to see more respect for the animals. Then we would have loved to finish this tour. 
It's all like Disneyland. Everything is based only on the photos for the customers. 
The looks of the animals were just hard to bear.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you kindly for your feedback. It is unfortunate that you did not enjoy the gameviewing experience.

As this was your first safari experience in Africa, it is understandable how you may react to such upclose animal interactions in the wild. And we also understand that clients perspectives and reactions are different as attested by the positive reviews that we received from other members of your safari tour.

The whole essence of a photographic safari basically relies on the guides ability to track, observe and photograph wild animals in their natural enhabitat and in understanding their behavior during these interactions.

We pride ourselves in being able to offer private, up close, wild and authentic photographic safari experiences, whilst maintaining harmony with our natural surroundings and it's inhabitants in a responsible and respectful way. In today's overpopulated world that is a rare product offering.
It is truly something special to be able to share this experience with those able to visit us and those who have travelled with us over the years will agree on that sentiment.  But we also acknowledge that travellers views,  backgrounds and expectations are not always the same.

title: Unforgettable 11-Day Safari in Botswana with Kalahari Breeze
text:
Our 11-day safari through Botswana was simply fantastic – we spent 8 days on a Mobile Safari in Okavango Delta, Moremi Game Reserve and Chobe National Park. Individual planning with Maria was personal, professional and straightforward from the start – we felt well looked after at all times.

The organization was completely reliable. From the high-quality camping equipment to the top-equipped Landcruiser to the fantastically located camps – everything was perfectly prepared. Especially the feeling of sleeping in the middle of nature, sitting around the campfire in the evening and listening to the sounds of Africa was simply magical.

What made this trip truly unique was the wonderful team: warm welcomes to the camp, a chef who surprised us daily with varied and very delicious food, and guides who with a lot of knowledge, patience and passion brought their homeland and wildlife closer.

We were very lucky to see elephants, giraffes, zebras, buffaloes, hippos, countless antelopes and bird species as well as even leopards, cheetahs and lions.

Conclusion: One of the most impressive journeys we have ever made – authentic, natural and emotional. A big thank you to the entire Kalahari Breeze team for this unforgettable experience! 100% recommendation.

title: The most wonderful, sensational, life changing experience that I can possibly have imagined and so highly recommend!
text:
I have recently returned from the most sensational, life changing, out of this world (and any other superlative you can think of!) experience on safari for 10 days in Botswana with Kalahari Breeze Safaris.  In fact I have only returned to Australia in body; my heart and soul are still out in the wilds of Botswana immersed in the bush living with the animals!  

Beautifully organised from the Australian end and executed equally superbly by Maria and Chris at locally owned Kalahari Breeze Safaris.  Add our wonderful guide Clifford and the guys in our fabulous support team on top and it has to be one of the best (maybe the best) travel experience I have had.  I haven’t stopped talking about to anyone who will listen!   With glowing recommendations for Kalahari Breeze for anyone wanting a real and very authentic, immersive experience!  

The attention to detail was outstanding.  From endearing little touches in camp to accommodating special dietary requirements with ease to ensuring our comfort whilst giving us a real taste of their country, the support team went about their work with joy, infectious enthusiasm and a beautiful smile.  As for our wonderful guide Clifford what can I say.  Chilled, laid back, calm with a love and knowledge of the wilds and animals of Botswana and a real desire to share it with us, Clifford was the special ingredient that gave us the deep and spiritual immersion that made the adventure all the more outstanding.  Always looking to the best spots to find animals while navigating 'roads' (aka tracks) which reminded me of the outback roads of my childhood in NW NSW to fielding our endless questions with grace and a dry sense of humour.  

Going on safari has always been on my bucket list.  I now know it is only the beginning of my connection to Southern Africa, particularly Botswana.  As someone famous said, I'll be back!  

So put it on your bucket list and bring your bucket list forward.   Just do it!  And did I say how highly I recommend Kalahari Breeze Safaris.

title: What a pleasant way to explore the delta!
text:
I did this mokoro tour of the Okavango Delta as part of the Kiboko Adventure Safari and it was amazing.
There were two of us in the mokoro (dug-out canoe) with the pilot (the man who uses a long pole to push us through the long grasses, reeds and water lilies).  We also had a tour guide who told us about the flora and fauna that we saw along the way.  We got to see birds' eggs resting on lily pads; tiny little "Jesus" birds which walked across the surface of the water;  and beautiful, beautiful water lilies - a veritable carpet of them floating on the water. 
The guide and his pilots were friendly, knowledgeable and very adept at manipulating the mokoros.  
It was such a peaceful way to view the delta from the perspective of the water's surface itself.  No loud engines;  not looking down from on high.  Just the quiet dip of the poles and the sounds of the wildlife.
I highly recommend seeing the Okavango Delta this way.

title: A multicultural travel throughout a fascinating landscape
text:
A trip to Old Palapye is definitely worth a stop f one is going up to the Okavango or wishes to spend more time in less known places of beautiful Botswana. It was not easy to find the access, but the guides signalled us to stop in the right place. The office is actually a shed and a tree, but the guide we had was both knowledgeable, kind and proud about the history of his Country. The visit takes one to the ruins of a church built by the London Society. The brick facade is still standing, and the relic of the apse at the back. The roof is gone as most of the interior. It is an eerie sensation to stand amongst the ruins of a church that is definitely "English" in style, but to see around the spiny vegetation and the grey rocks of the southern part of Africa. 
The track then leads to a miracle...Because Botswana in September is dusty and dry, there are no clouds in the sky and apparently there is no surface water. The miracle is a small canyon where a creek flows and falls in a small cascade of bubbling water. The water is pure, there is nothing above, and tastes cool and fresh. It was a gift. Our guide told us stories surrounding the water and then we went back, happy to have seen this wondrous place.

title: Interesting enough
text: The ruins of the church are interesting, the others few and not so much. There is an entry fee now. The small gorge in the same enclosure, 5 km further, is very nice but not so much as the one on the north side

title: An excellent lodge and experience!
text: Incredible stay!  This is a small but excellent lodge in the Kalahari.  The location is wonderful and animal sightings were remarkable.  There is a water hole close to the central lodge building that brings a constant flow of animals to visit within 50 m of the lodge.  The units are very comfortable and place for privacy.  Our guide was Knowledge, he, together with our bushman tracker did a great job finding a brood range of animals, including Rhinos.  The lodge also offers an opportunity to interact with ancestral nomadic bush people and to learn how they survived in this environment.

title: Camping in the Central Kalahari, a special experience
text:
We spent 2 nights with our 4x4 with roof tent in the Deception Pan campsite with only 6 sites far apart and without facilities other than a long drop toilet and a bucket shower. No other facilities, no water, no electricity, no fire wood, for longer stay solar panels recommended.
Not much game in the open planes.

title: Avoid Gifa, if your plans change he will not refund any money
text:
After doing some research we decided to book Gifa (operated by a man named Dimpho Motlhabi) for a large group (20+) transport lasting about 10 days in Botswana and South Africa. Our first conversations were very pleasant and the company was very prompt and professional. About one month before we were to arrive Dimpho reached out to explain that we had not confirmed and that he could only hold our spot for a few more days unless he was paid for the trip. We asked about their refund policy and found it satisfactory so we sent the money. Four days later, the trip was canceled and we immediately notified Dimpho that we would no longer need his services and would like to start the refund process. That’s where our nightmare began. 

When we asked for a refund since the trip was canceled an entire month in advance, Dimpho claimed that we would only receive a 70% refund since he had already paid for hotels, drivers, and food (food??), etc. Even though on the website and in our WhatsApp chat he specifically wrote that cancellations within “72 hours only 50% will be refunded” and “cancellation in 7 days left 30%.” To reiterate, we cancelled one month before the trip date and four days after sending Dimpho the money. But the best we can hope for will be 70% of our money back. 

We have messaged Dimpho almost daily asking for updates on our refund and he is always busy, or out of town, or being investigated by BURS, or had his accounts frozen etc. then the promises started.

On Tuesday 6/27/2023 “everything is okay now we will be able to send refund very soon,” then did not reply to our repeated attempts at clarifying when that would be. On Monday 7/3/2023 “I will see that it is sent to you before this end of the week,” no refund. The following Tuesday, “just got here now 6:30am will see through that,” again no refund. This trend has continued into August and we still have no refund. 

All in all, our experience has been horrible. Dimpho/Gifa routinely go days/weeks without answering our texts or calls and every deadline/promise that he has made has been utterly false. Dimpho might be wonderful at pickups and drop-offs but he has lied to us repeatedly and is a dishonest business man. It has been almost four months since we asked for a refund and we still have not received a dime. It's about $6,000 that he's refusing to return. 

Be warned about doing business with Gifa or Dimpho Motlhabi.

If you are in the city and are going to move inland, it is highly recommended that you do your shopping here as you will have the opportunity to have greater availability of products. Having said the above from our own experience, when we were in Maun, which is the fourth most populated city in the country, availability was very limited.
Enjoy Riverwalk Mall because: LIFE...is a JOURNEY!

title: Very popular and active center even after 20 years.
text: We passed the Shell, KFC, Spar & FNB on the 500m walk down from our stray at Indaba Lodge. With a nearly full parking lot on all 3 sides, we were surprised how busy this place was on this weekday at noon. We checked out the east side’s restaurants and the movies at the upstairs cinema before shopping at the Pick N Pay. The interacting locals were friendly and well dressed, walking the open halls(with several souvenir stalls) of the ~80 stores.

title: No Riverwalk or River View
text: The name Riverwalk is misleading as there are no views of water and fountains or a boardwalk overlooking a lake. It is a typical mall dominated by South African chain stores.

title: Amazing experience not to be missed
text: We stayed at Chobe Marina Lodge for 4 nights.  Regarding price.  It was an all in price with hotel, local flights and safari included.   African Odyssey activities centre is based at the hotel. The first evening we went for sunset cruise which we enjoyed.  We saw various animals an found the guide very informative. We also did a morning cruise at 9.45 to give us a rest from the early mornings. The first morning at 5.45 we joined Mike the game ranger and our safari experience in Chobe National Park started and got better day after day.  The park is famous for elephants.  There are 60,000 and we saw many of them.   We did 2 game drives per day (apart from those quoted above) and after the first morning had Mike to our selves.  On the first drive we saw Lions which had not been seen in the park for some time.  Elephant, Impala, Baboons and Giraffe. Also many birds. The following afternoon we went further into the park and saw many of the same animals but in different areas. To see Elephant eating grass in the river up to their necks and interacting togeather is something I never thought I would see.  Also many hippo groups.  As the days went by Mike took us further along the river and into the park. We saw Buffalo and a lion kill from the day before.  On the last day we went the furthest of all and saw lionesses, a honey badger (not seen for a very long time) and a leopard also not seen for ages.  We had many close encounters too, handled very efficiently by Mike who we can not praise highly enough.  He is also a very good photographer and often took my camera and got brilliant shots.  We have done other safari's in Africa but this was magical.  To be on the edge of the river watching a lone elephant drinking and having a mud bath, who then walks so close to the vehicle I could have touched him was the best ever.  The sounds of Africa in a setting like that can not be described.  It is something I will never forgot and all thanks to Mike.

title: Pangolin provided a "one of a kind" India experience beyond just tigers
text: I just returned from the Pangolin India Tiger Safari. It was an amazing experience. Before the trip Daniella at the office was very helpful and answered any email question promptly. The tour Hosts, Sabine and Charls, are very knowledgable regarding wildlife photography, both how to properly photograph the animal and then processing techniques. This old curmudgeon was able to learn and retain a couple of new techniques from them. The lodges are some of the nicest that I have stayed in. They were Bagh Tola, Pench Tree Lodge, and the Bori Safari Lodge. The were Bandhavgarh NP, Pench NP, and the Bori Wildlife Sanctuary at Stapura NP. I liked the first and last parks the best, but had incredible Wile Dog sitings at Pench. One important items that I learned is that unlike some lodges in other locations that allow you to include gratuities on your credit card in India that was not possible and currency exchange outside of the airport or ATM use was not convenient. The local "handler", and lodge owner of Bagh Tola, Jason Fernandes, was an extremely gracious and made the trip one of a kind by providing a life altering experience during the "Holi" holiday. Almost all of the naturalists/drivers were great, but my personal favorite was TK at Bori Safari Lodge.

title: Experience of a Lifetime for everyone - Must do for Photographers
text:
Booking: My wife and I went on the Pangolin Chobe, Delta, Kalahari (CDK) photo safari in September 2025.  The best way to describe the experience is SURREAL! I am a nature photographer and visiting Africa and photographing the wildlife was a bucket list trip for me and my wife. 
I have followed Pangolin photo safaris through their YouTube channel and website for a few years. When we decided to go on this trip, I filled out the form and Samantha McDonald, a lovely agent contacted me via email.  After several weeks of email & phone conversations wit Sam, I booked us for the 10 day CDK safari experience a full year in advance! Sam was an amazing resource of knowledge, tips and patiently answered all our questions for the next whole year and throughout the trip! I highly recommend her!

Experience: Pangolin is a first class operation. It is run by photographers for photographers. If you are a photographer, don't think twice - Book this trip now before they fill out for 2027. If you are not a photographer, don't worry. Just simply watching these animals and birds in their natural habitat is breathtaking. Their goal is to send us home with world class wildlife photos which your family and friends will think are worthy of being in magazines! To that end, they have an experienced Canon photographer with us offering excellent coaching on exposure, shutter speed and angles to get the best shots for that particular scenario.  They give everyone interested a professional camera (Canon R6 MKII in 2025) and a great lens (Canon RF 100-500 in 2025) and abundant training.  Our coach Sara Jenner was amazing and her  energy and enthusiasm was infectious.

Accommodations: The places where we stayed  were 5*! The new Pangolin Voyager houseboat, The luxurious and spacious tents at Shinde Footsteps and Dinaka camp were incredibly well appointed for a place in the middle of wilderness! The food was amazing with a lot of local fare and it catered to everyone's needs, which included some Indian curry for me and many dishes for the guests who were vegetarians.

Wildlife: The guides at Chobe, Delta and Kalahari were amazing! They knew where the animals and birds were and what the best locations for getting the best photos!  They are expert trackers and follow footprints to locate the wildlife! We got to see so many varieties of birds and mammal species.  Every day we did two game drives that were 4 hours in duration.  The day starts with a 5:30 am breakfast, 6:30 am game drive for 4 hours, 11:30 lunch, a short break, 3:30 tea, 4 pm game drive, 8 pm dinner. The days are jam packed with opportunities to see wildlife.  We saw a leopard, cheetahs, a rhino, so many hippos, lions & cubs, Hyenas, the endangered wild dog, wildebeest, elephants, elephants and elephants, giraffe, baboons, and hippos to name a few.

Cost:  This Chobe, Delta, Kalahari trip is not inexpensive.  At $13,500/person for this and the round trip airfare from the US costs a good chunk and required a lot of planning and saving.  I thought that it was expensive. Having completed this trip, I will confidently say that the experience was worth every penny and you'll end up with amazing memories and so many awesome pictures.  I am an extremely value conscious, comparison shopper and I highly recommend that you do your own research about these safaris. I did mine.  There is NO ONE that provides such an exclusive small group 5* experience at this price.  I found that even those through Costco (We travel a lot through them) were almost twice the cost for a similar length and those did not include such intensive game drives.

We are looking forward to booking our next Pangolin Safari in 2027.  Hope to see you there!

title: Pangolin Photo Safaris - the very best!
text:
An unforgettable experience.  I recently completed the Chobe River/Okavango Delta/Kalahari with Pangolin.  The trip exceeded my hopes and expectations.  Excellent accommodations at every step. 

The brand new (July 2025) houseboat on the Chobe was extremely comfortable, and the staff/service was excellent.  We saw an amazing amount of wildlife almost constantly, and the river guide, Laskey, was fantastic.  The river experience was uncrowded and we were able to go where we wanted without having to compete with other tour boats

The Ker & Downey camp on the Okavango was very comfortable, and again excellent staff/service/food.  The drives through the delta were an unforgettable experience, with plentiful animal and bird life.  A great guide again, Bobbie, who really added to the experience, and we saw a tremendous amount of wildlife throughout the visit

The Kalahari visit was at another Ker & Downey camp, this time very luxurious and comfortable.  The same excellent experience, plenty of wildlife, good food and service at the camp, all in all another great stay.  Our local guide, KLB, was again absolutely first-rate.

The overall trip management by Pangolin was first rate.  They had people to help us at every stage on the tour, to ensure we had the best possible experience.  Our trip host, Karolina, was fantastic - her friendly and positive approach contributed greatly to the group experience, and she gave all of the guests, regardless of experience level, really useful photography pointers.

I can’t recommend Pangolin highly enough.  If you are looking for a photo safari outfit, look no further!
