title: All round winner of a tour
text: Wow! I am so glad I did this safari. After a Covid delay of five years we finally made it to the "Chobe, Delta and Kalahari" 10 day safari.  My partner and I started this trip as "phone photographers" basically,  and after the ten days tuition and guidance on provided cameras we are hooked. This tour has three nights on the Voyager houseboat , four nights at Okavango Delta, and two nights on the edge the Central Kalahari. These two camps were at Ker & Downey establishments and every place was fantastic.  The Pangolin houseboat is a little bit old and is getting replaced soon, but on board everything was clean and comfortable. The staff were amazing - so friendly and meals were delicious. Up early for morning river trips or game drives with our wonderful photo host Will, for whom nothing was trouble, we were astounded by the wildlife and the skill of our driver/guides. Their placement of vehicles to get good shots, and Will's technical guidance helped us come home with  memorable good photos.  Afternoon brought another trip out for photographs and sundowners as the magical African sunsets coloured the sky. All arrangements  booked through Pangolin were well planned and communication with our consultant was always timely. I have no hesitation in recommending Pangolin Photo Safaris and hope  I can return one day.

title: A very memorable learning experience
text:
Just recently returned from the best of Chobe safari. We spent the first three nights on the Pangolin voyager on the Namibian side of the Chobe river and the next three night in the Pangolin hotel on the Botswanan side. We booked the trip for the opportunity to photograph the wildlife, but also to gain valuable knowledge and understanding to improve our photographic skills. 

The whole experience completely exceeded my expectations. The warm welcome from Guts and the team when we arrived at the hotel was a brief introduction before when went across the river to the Panogolin voyager via Namibian immigration. Our photo host Janine, was quick to assess our experience and expectations before we went out on an evening boat safari. We spent the next two days on morning and evening boat safaris getting opportunities to photograph Elephants, Hippos, crocodiles, lots of species of bird and the magical experience with the female leopard. The Pangolin voyager staff were very freindly and the accommodation was very comfortable and clean. The food was excellent with plenty to eat (You won’t go hungry). After three nights we went back to Botswana to stay in the Pangolin hotel. The hotel is a lovely design and also very clean and comfortable. The rooms are a good size with plenty of space for camera equipment and plenty of sockets for charging. The game drives from the hotel were a mixture of land safaris and boat safaris where we photographed lions, giraffes, zebras, hyenas etc. As with the voyager, the staff at the hotel were very welcoming and the evening drinks around the fire pit preceded the chefs explaining the food for the evening before serving it. The food here was exceptional and the whole team encouraged everyone to keep their glasses filled and enjoy themselves. The photo hosts took turns to have dinner with us so we could get to know everyone. If you are thinking about going on one of these safaris on your own, you will easily integrate with friendly like minded people. We were in a group of seven and it was a pleasure to spend those six days with a group of people we had never met before.

After returning home I am reviewing the images I captured and editing to enhance them. Janine (our photo host) was continually coaching us throughout our game drive to ensure we thought about composition, exposure and camera settings. In between game drives she gave us tutorials on capturing images and editing our photographs in Lightroom. Her coaching was excellent to ensure we had the best chance to get some great photos and she was also very knowledgeable about all of our different cameras.

If you are looking to have a photographic safari holiday where you learn more than you thought you would and have the best opportunity to photograph the wildlife, this is for you. Be prepared for long days getting up at 04:30hrs, but it is really worth it when you review your photos and experience the early mornings on the river. Attend the tutorials as they are so helpful to help improve your skills.

Thank-you to Guts, Janine and the team for making this a memorable experience. I would definitely like to return on the future.

title: Wow!! It doesn't get much better than this!
text:
We have recently returned home from the  6 day Best of Chobe trip. We embarked on the journey with really high expectations based on what we had read about Pangolin Safaris, then trip itself exceeded our expectations by some way.

The three nights on the Pangolin Voyager houseboat were magical and each morning and afternoon we were straight out onto the Chobe River in the dedicated photo boats. We had some fabulous sightings and photo opportunities and really enjoyed the different feel that our images had from shooting low down on the river.

The three nights at the  stunning Pangolin hotel were equally good, we did three land based safaris and three more  water based safaris.

Our photo host Janine was outstanding and led our group of seven superbly. She was supportive and encouraging throughout what was a very hot trip (36 - 40 degrees!!). She helped us develop techniques we don't use regularly at home, such as shooting high and low key images and slow speed panning. At the hotel, Janine gave us presentations on different aspects of post production in Lightroom, particularly how to edit our high and low key images successfully.

We were very fortunate that we were part of a fabulous group of people who got on well and really enjoyed what was a very unique experience. All of the staff we met were excellent and provided great service.

Our trip was one of very few we have been on where we can say everything was perfect and I can't thinks of anything constructive to suggest that could improve it further.

title: Great trip with Pangolin!
text:
We recently returned from the scheduled 6-night Best of The Chobe Trip with Pangolin. We loved everything, and our photo host, Walter, was fantastic. 

The first half of the trip was based on their comfortable houseboat which offered unique photo opportunities on the Namibian side of the Chobe. Iconic African scenes unfolded as we cruised up and down the river between photo sessions. Capturing the tiny Malachite Kingfisher in “Lily Land” in beautiful golden light was a highlight.

The last half of the trip was based out of the Pangolin Hotel where we photographed the amazing wildlife of Chobe National Park by both land and water. Lions, leopards, elephants, hippos, giraffes, all kinds of ungulates, and a dazzling array of birds filled our memory cards, while the staff at Pangolin provided outstanding food and friendly service. 

My wife was new to photography, and Walter did a great job of guiding her into both the basic and creative elements of capturing wildlife through her lens. I appreciated Walter’s approach to photography which provided new ways of looking at subjects, and his expertise in getting us in the right place at the right time to capture an image.

Pangolin provides a unique and intimate way of watching wildlife in a small group setting. I think any nature lover would enjoy their services, but photographers in particular will appreciate their expertise and approach to turning beautiful African scenes into treasured photographs of the wildlife of The Chobe.

ownerResponse.text: It's wonderful to hear how much you enjoyed your stay with us at both the houseboat and the Pangolin Chobe Hotel - Walter is delighted that he was able to make such a positive impact on your trip!

title: Pangolin problems
text:
Pangolin photo safaris has some problems which need to be addressed. Information provided to us prior to the safari was not always accurate. One of the drivers on the tour was quite unprofessional driving between us and our photo subject. In one case ruining a once in a lifetime opportunity. Another time driving between us and wild dogs, interrupting the actions there were being photographed. We came to Botswana at that time especially for the birds. Due to the weather conditions birding was difficult. One place where we had success was in a photo bunker. We were told we had the bunker all day. Settling in late afternoon to catch the birds in the great evening light, we were surprised when we were told there was a crisis and we had to leave the bunker. Some VIP wanted to use the bunker so we were expelled. We did not appreciate being treated like second class tourist.
Have been in contact with Toby of Pangolin I enumerated in much greater detail  the many more negative instances we had on safari. Hopefully they will be corrected. In the mean time the ruined photo opportunities still leave a bitter taste.

ownerResponse.text:
Hi Charlie,

Apologies for the late reply but I have been reaching out to our partners in The Okavango and Kalahari (which you mention above) to get their take on things. I also took the time to chat with other members of your safari group and your photo host to get their take on what happened.

As I understand it you had some exceptional sightings and photo opportunities throughout the safari. Its unfortunate that there was a moment where a potential shot was effected by the other vehicle but this was in no way done on purpose. In the heat of the moment when two vehicles are positioning themselves for the best shots it can happen...very rarely...but it can happen. 

The beauty of being on a private concession (which you were) is that you can off road at a sighting as opposed to being on a road - where there is no way to get in each others way - but is nowhere near as good photographically. 

As to the time in the bunker hide at Dinaka I believe that your group was there for the whole morning alone and it was asked if it would be ok if you went to the other hide to allow some other guests a chance to experience the bunker hide you were in for some of the afternoon session. No VIPs barging in. Not sure where you got that from. 

The camp sleeps up to 14 people when full and the hide takes around 6 comfortably so its only right that everyone gets their fair share of time there. With all the other subject matter around i think thats only right. You wouldn't like to be told you can go because someone else wants to stay there the whole day would you? We have never said anywhere that we would have exclusive use of one particular hide for the whole day.

In your mail to me you also alluded to the fact that the Pangolin Chobe Hotel is made of concrete and you didn't like that - "wasn't to your taste". I find that odd as I am sure you would have seen videos and images of the hotel before you arrived so not sure why that was such a surprise. We love the modern feel of the hotel and less wood in a country where there are plenty of termites is a sound long term strategy.

When you were on the houseboat you said there was a problem with the aircon and fan not working. As was explained to you at the beginning of your stay onboard only some plugs in the cabin have power all night and thats where the fan should stay plugged into. 

I could go on but I think it is fair to say that we have obviously failed to meet your expectations on many levels and for that I am sorry. We strive to try and make everyone's safari experience the best we can and obviously in this instance we have, in your opinion, fallen short.

I wish it was different but I guess we will have to live with a two star review in perpetuity and hope that people see it for what it is! 

Toby

title: So close to brilliant.
text:
I joined one of the scheduled Pangolin Photo Tours to Chobe and Botswana. It was a very mixed experience. The locations chosen for the tour provided a great mix of photo opportunities from Elephant families at the Chobe River, to being surrounded by a lion pride in the Kalahari. The big picture was really well organized and it should have been a brilliant tour. Unfortunately, it was in the details of the tour things were not looked after. Our photo guide was relatively inexperienced and missed several opportunities to enhance our tour and on one instance literally make an unforgivable error.  For me the most serious issues were due to the combination of the local driver/guides from the Botswana lodges and our photo host. The worst was one morning when guests in one of the two vehicles were set up for a literal "shot of a lifetime" when the 2nd vehicle (with our photo host on board) literally drove into shot not once but twice! The first time they disturbed the resting lioness who was going to be the subject of the photograph causing her to move on and our vehicle to have to reposition to try to line up the sunrise shot again. Only to have it ruined for the second time.  It is highly unlikely I will ever have the opportunity to take that shot again. 

I would like to recommend Pangolin Tours, however, if you are an experienced photographer I would suggest a chat with the organizers ahead of time to ensure you have the best experience.

ownerResponse.text:
Hi Alison,

Thank you very much for your feedback on your trip with us and I would like to apologise that it did not live up to expectations.

We would welcome the opportunity to take you on a photo safari again in the hope that we can make up for this missed shot with plenty of others.

You know where we are!

All the best

Toby

title: Photography instruction to go from zero to hero in a few short days!  With plenty of tips for the more experienced of us
text:
I'll make this short, there are no superlatives good enough to describe our time with Pangolin.

We booked the Best of Chobe safari as my wife's first foray from North America.   I had been watching all of the phenomenal YouTube content put out by Pangolin and knew they were the company to go with.  They met and exceeded every expectation I had, and I had a lot.

Our guides, Ray and Charlies, were excellent, knowing the game well and how best to position our boat or game drive vehicle for the best photos.

Walter served as our photo host.  Not only is he an excellent photographer, but he's an outstanding teacher.  I do a lot of wildlife photography, and my wife is/was a complete novice at digital photography.  By the end of the trip she was turning out outstanding images, and I learned more than a few tips.  Well worth it for both of us.

Typically, you'll do two game drives per day, either by custom-designed photography boats or game vehicles.  We had four meals daily, and the evening meals were some of the best I have ever had.  Also, while on the game drives, there are snacks, water, coffee, or sodas.  You won't go hungry!

While we both had gear (Nikon for my wife. I carry Canon R3 and Canon R5 ) they do have a loan department from which they will provide (free of charge) Canon 80Ds and 150-600mm lenses.   

The photo hosts are well-versed in the major camera manufacturers and models.

Again, I can't begin to praise Pangolin, Toby, and Guts for the incredible customer service and photo safari.

ownerResponse.text: How's wonderful to hear such great feedback, I will pass on your regards to Walter and everyone else mentioned, Im sure you came away with some fantastic images!

title: Exciting and Memorable Photo Safaris with Pangolin
text:
My wife & me and a delightful & memorable experience with the Pangolin Masai Mara Photographic Safari. Our interactions with Pangolin started almost a year earlier - Pangolin photo safaris are very popular with Wildlife Photographers and are in great demand, so one has to start early.

Sam, our Tour Planner, was patient with us and sorted all our issues in time and with understanding. we were travelling from India and had many issues to be taken care of. Thanks Sam.

Pangolin arranged our flight tickets to and stay at Samburu Saruni for our unescorted trip to Samburu. Very nice experience there. Our stay at Tamarind Tree on three occasions, airport pick-up and drop were meticulously arranged for. We thank Nira & Andy for excellent ground handling at Nairobi.

The Photo Safaris at Masai Mara was superlative. We stayed at the Mara Bush Camp Luxury Lodge inside the Masai Mara National Park. The Tents were alongside a small river, with about 50 Hippos lazing and grunting all through the day and night. 

The Camp Tents were large luxurious, with all amenities, large beds, working table and fully equipped bath/ toilet area. The food was professionally cooked and served by trained Chefs and catered to various food requirements. 

The Photo Safaris were undoubtedly the highlight of the days in Mara. Each large Pangolin Safari Jeep would have just 4 guests with competent Guide Driver and the very capable and eager to help Pangolin Photoguides. Charl, Sabine, Nelis and Andy were all professional & highly-rated photographers, who accompanied us in turns. I learnt a great deal about Wildlife photography and camera settings. Manual with Auto ISO and Eye-focus for birds were game changing for me. Thank you Charl, Sabine, Nelis & Andy.

We were lucky to see the Migration in full swing from the Jeep and also from the Hot-air Balloon. We also saw the Mara river crossing by the Wildebeests and Zebras, apart from the big cats, big five, most other animals - many with cubs and also birds. I have taken thousands of photographs, which will keep reminding us of the Kenyan Wildlife and the utterly capable Pangolin team.

Thank you Pangolin team for giving us the most exciting and wonderful wildlife experience so far.  Hopefully we meet again!

ownerResponse.text: How wonderful that you had such an amazing time on our Migration trip to the Masai Mara, it truly is a stunning location to photograph. Thanks for taking the time to write this review, lets hope you are able to join one of our other trips or join us here in Botswana!

title: Pangolin: Fantastic company for a Photo Safari
text: We chose Pangolin Photo Safaris for our first visit to Africa from the US and we can't say enough good things about the trip and the company. From our stay at the Pangolin Hotel in Kasane, Botswana to the Great Migration in the Masai Mara in Kenya, the experience was incredible. The photo hosts were very helpful and the guides in the jeeps and boats knew all about the area as well as the natural and human history. They could find us wildlife and get us to a vantage point where were were able to have great views of the animals. As a birder, I was very pleased that the guides could always come up with an identification for what we saw. The accommodations in both places were extremely comfortable and we were very well taken care of by the staff. The food was some of the best that I've ever tasted. We have absolutely no complaints about our trip and many, many thanks to give to those that work for Pangolin Photo Safaris.

ownerResponse.text: Wow how amazing - what a trip to include both or Chobe experience with our Masai Mara trips, Im sure it was an amazing combination that meant you came away with many stunning images, thanks for the great review!

title: Perfect photography trip in Masai Mara
text:
This was our second trip, this time to the Masai Mara to see the Great migration. we stayed at the Mara Bush camp and it was a wonderful experience. Not only did we see the great 5 and migration but many more beautiful moments shared with fellow guests. our group of 16 had 4 cars with a driver and the Pangolin photo guides - happy to see Sabine and Charl again! Every one of them an expert himself with certain special interests in wildlife changed the cars daily to give as an even more profound and interesting look at nature and photography. The accommodation is wonderful, directly on the river with lots of hippos and at night a Masai with a spear brings you back to your (luxury) tent. The camp is fairly new therefore the rooms are Wonderfull with a large deck overlooking the river. Everything was very well planned and executed by Pangolin Safaris. The food in the camp and on the trips were delicious and in fact incredible for being out in the bush. Lunches were served underneath beautiful old trees with wildlife surrounding us. 
Leaving in the morning at 6 am and returning at 7 pm is intense but if you are into photography and wildlife this gives you the best chance to see everything. It is on some days possible to stay in the camp half days but not on all. The highlight was the Balloon trip with lunch afterwards - Sergei: best pilot ever! 
...and there is always help with post production too. the pictures added are I-phone only. 
Tipping is extra for the drivers and Lodge stuff - but can be done with CC.
Thanks to our guides Sabine & Charl, Nellis and Andy!

ownerResponse.text: Hi Barbara - wonderful to hear that you enjoyed our Masai Mara trip, Charl & Sabine will be absolutely delighted that you had such a great time! Hope to see you in Chobe soon!

title: Best photographic tour in Botswana
text: A truly bespoke photographic experience. My wife and I took a seven-day tour both on the Chobe River and Chobe national park. This tour could not have been improved in any conceivable way. Perfection in all aspects from start to finish. For anyone interested in photography in Africa whether an expert or a novice photographer this is a good as it gets. Sabine was the consummate photo guide. Professional, warm, generous, and highly skilled as a photographer and most importantly as an educator both in photography and as a naturalist. She was incredibly helpful to my wife who was a novice photographer but even with some personal photographic experience I learned a lot from her, and I got the best pictures of my life. Her knowledge of the animals was also amazing. The motel and boat staff were the most friendly and caring people I've ever met anywhere in the world. The accommodations both on the houseboat and later in the motel were very comfortable and the food and drinks were excellent and abundant. The photographic boat was certainly the best on the river. Where other tours had their guests sitting on lawn furniture, we had 360 degree swiveling chairs with an Arca Swiss gimbal to mount our cameras and with only 8 single row seats on the boat there was never anyone blocking your view. On land in Chobe national park, we had open safari vehicles with no windows to block our view or cameras. They even had coffee and insulated parkas to warm us up both on the boats and safari trucks. One of the things that Pangolin did particularly well was match our interests with other guests who were equally interested in photography. As far as photo tips I took most of my pictures on a full frame camera with a 100-500 zoom. However, for some bird photography and long-distance shots I was happy to have a 600mm lens with a 1.4 teleconverter (nice but not necessary). If something exciting was happening such as lions at a kill, we were able to extend our stay rather than rush back to get an early lunch or evening sundowner which was still waiting for us when we got there. Although what you see may vary slightly depending on the time of year, we saw an unimaginable amount and variety of wildlife (Cape buffalo, baboons, giraffes, zebras, wild dogs, crocodiles, hippos, all kinds of antelope, lions feeding, leopards, vast number of elephants with babies, and some of the most colorful birds I have ever seen). Thank you, Pangolin for making this trip the greatest of my life.  Rob

ownerResponse.text: Thanks Rob - it's wonderful to hear that you had such a fantastic trip (was there anything you didn't see?), plus its sounds like you got some great shots also! thanks agin for the review!

title: Our first photo safari and we loved it!
text:
We spent 2 nights at Pangolin Photo safaris at Kasane and couldn’t be more happy that we took the decision to come here! We are not serious photographers and so were a little apprehensive before coming that we may feel out of place amongst other seasoned photographers we expected to meet. Our fears were allayed from the moment we arrived- everyone made us feel welcome and assured us we would be fine. Our photography host was Janine and she was brilliant. Over our 4 sessions (3 on the boat and one land safari), we were given a practical cash course on photography 101 (including one session on theory!). Janine was a big reason why we enjoyed our stay as she was so friendly and willing to answer our every question. We appreciated her clear, simple explanations of different camera settings, rationale for each etc. Even our 12 year old daughter enjoyed the experience and could follow along. 

The design of the boats is excellent and played a big role in providing us with a great experience. The boat seats can swivel 360 degrees so we can quickly turn to wherever the animal(s) or bird(s) happens to be. The cameras are mounted on a strong steel frame which can also swivel completely to point to any direction. With Janine’s regular and clear instructions on what camera settings to have for different shots, it made it so easy for novices like us. And it  also allowed us the freedom to enjoy the wind on our faces and the beautiful landscape!

Viewing wildlife from a boat compared to the typical land safaris has its own special charm. By the time we reached Pangolin in Kasane we had already spent 8 days in Botswana safari lodges (15 land safaris in total) so had already seen a lot of wildlife, but seeing the animals and birds from a boat in the Chobe river gave us an altogether different experience. For example, watching the big herd of thirsty elephants bound into the river or getting up close to the birds on our boat without scaring them away. 

We particularly enjoyed our two morning photo safaris along the Chobe River. We saw abundant wildlife (we were particularly lucky to see 8 lions, in addition to lots of elephants, buffaloes, impala and numerous birds (including getting great photos of the white-fronted bee-eater, malachite kingfisher, little bee-eater etc).

The rooms are clean and functional. Food was really good both in terms of spread and flavor. Chef Robson accommodated the different dietary requirements of our family and each meal was filling and satisfying.
 
In short if you are interested in wildlife/safaris and want to hone your photography skills, there is no better way to learn than join a photo safari experience like Pangolin, set amidst the natural beauty of the Chobe River with abundant wildlife and beautiful birds!

We will now practice what we learnt and are already looking forward to another photo safari with Pangolin!

ownerResponse.text: Wow!! Such a wonderful read, thanks so much for taking the time to write such a great review! Janine will be delighted that she was able to get you some amazing shots and give you a wonderful experience! Thanks very much!

text:

However, they will also book a custom trip and accommodate specific requests/itineraries. For the latter, you might not always have a photo guide but the wildlife guides were all incredibly knowledgeable and well versed in how to position the vehicles for photographers. All in all, well organized, amazing guides, very accommodating, spectacular place.

title: Come River, Delta and Kalahari safari
text: We are recently returned from an excellent Pangolin Photographic safari. Firstly we stayed on the house boat on the Chobe  River with the stunning sunrises and sunsets, crocs, hypos and Ellies. Next it was Pom Pom camp in the Delta with so many leopards and a pair of black mambas doing a courtship dance. This camp was an unexpected stay due to technical difficulties at the planned camp but was a wonderful reserve with fantastic staff. Finally we were at Dinaka in the Kalahari with the beautiful black maned lions. Our experience was completely enhanced by having Danielle as our photographic guide. She was a great in every respect but particularly in juggling the diverse needs of the 8 of us in the group with very differing levels of photographic experience. A big thank you to Danielle for encouraging me as a complete novice and making sure our photos on future safaris will be of a much higher standard!

title: Day light robbery!
text:
I am reproducing a letter that I wrote toHello!
This is to bring to your notice the following:
1) Nine of us were booked by ULINDA Safari Trails for travel from Savuti to Kasane.
2) We were carrying bags weighing around 200kg (incl. hand bags) while the pilot Mr. Oliver declared that the same weighed 430 kg!!
3) We travelled from India by Ethiopian Airlines and we knew precisely how much each bag weighed and there was no chance that the bags could have weighed 430 kg.
4) On Aug.26th, 2023 we did travel from Victoria Falls to Chennai (India) by Ethiopian Airlines and most of the baggage tags are attached for your perusal - you will notice the weight of each bag in these and that the claim of your pilot is far fetched. The total weight is only 120 kg plus another 15kg for a person who did not travel to India. 
5) Your pilot refused to weigh the bags in my presence when he escalated the issue. What was he expecting? This question remains unanswered.
6) Your pilot informed us that only half of the bags will be loaded and did exactly this.
7) We spent USD 1550 for ferrying the bags to Victoria Falls when we realised that your Pilot had duped us.

This kind of attitude once publicised will only be a great disincentive for people to travel to Botswana, leave alone by your airline!

On a perusal of the issue you will realise the genuineness of my claim and kindly reimburse the amount of USD1550 that we spent on ferrying the bags (arranged by ULINDA SAFARI TRAILS) to Kasane.
 Mack Air and as expected, there has been no reply!

It was a nightmare to deal with the pilot of the aircraft A2-OKG! It is high time that such occurances are curbed for the good of promoting tourism in Botswana!

title: had great hopes, but these were nowhere to be found ... not a good experience
text:
very cheap to get in, polite and friendly staff but the experience was a huge let down. saw a few antilope, a  couple of zebra, some ostriches 
vervet monkeys  and plenty of sewer pipes (waiting to be installed and fitted?) It's also a sewer outlet which is situated  towards the end/edge of the  park with evidence of  wipes and paper on branches from when it's been flooded and the smell is awful!  There are  signs to be careful not to drive off track or you'll get stuck in the "mud" 🥴  I would say that it's a  public health hazzard. Let's hope that the many broken  sewer pipes are mended and replaced  soonest!

title: Thrills
text: It was a very good experience. As you'd expect, we did what the place is intended for. Go in, see animals, go out. Nothing much to do beyond that. But it's definitely quite an adventure if you're a wildlife lover!

title: best thing to do in Gabs, what isn't saying much....
text: Mixt feelings; bad roads and works at several places, so bad that even our new Landrover Defender had some difficulties with all the mud. But very friendly people at the gate and lots of animals to see. We were here for the second time in two years and never have we seen so easily water monitor lizards has here, and eland antilope for exemple.

title: Faded figures needing time and guide to decipher.
text:
We drove 30 miles southwest of Gaborone on paved roads to the small town of Manyana following Google. The faded sign “Manyana Rock paintings 20m” was emblematic of the fenced and locked site itself. A man soon arrived and offered to call the caretaker but said the art is difficult to make out. 
We decided to inspect the interesting 20’-30’ high stone walls from the 30’ distance and read the wikipedia research; “artworks were made by the Khoikhoi or the San people between 1100 AD and 1700 AD. The paintings are found on five cliff areas around the rocky hill. Today, the site is fenced and protected as a National Monument.”

title: Tranquility at its best in Gaborone
text: Ferris ride to the restaurant sweet and short (I enjoy water cruising and would have loved a longer ride). Restaurant staff friendly, food was good and clean.

title: Fantastic Family Time
text: Excellent trip with a great pilot that shared very informative information. This was a very special family experience. Highly recommended.

title: Okavango Delta from the air.
text:
What an amazing experience to share with Alida and Lauren. I have been to the delta many times yet never seen it from above. It is the cherry on top and one well worth completing. Our pilot, Brett, was very professional and well informed of the local area. Seeing the river from above is difficult to describe. The lakes connected with hippo trails and dotted with water lilies, the plentiful red lechewe and the treat of seeing the shy and rare sitatunga was a delight for the senses. Great pods of hippo and quite a few elephant families were spotted on the verdant plains. Thanks to early rains there was no evidence of fire which create havoc and often leave the area blackened and unsightly at this time of year.
Helicopter Horizons gets my approval and is well worth the extra spend.

title: Very nice, but very expensive for a flight shorter than booked
text:
We booked our scenic flight throughout tour, and was surprised by the high prices; which jumped a lot from 2024 to 2025. For a 45 minutes flight we payed 285USD  per person, the equivalent of a return flight with a line company between 2 distant European capital cities. We wanted to pay with USD, cash, but they accepted only brand new banknotes. And we had the surprise that the flight actually lasted only 37 minutes, practically reducing the time over the delta by about 25%.
Otherwise great that we were able to see quite a lot of wildlife: gnus, warhogs, zebras, elephants, giraffes, and hippos.

ownerResponse.text:
Hi Adrian,

Thank you very much for taking the time to share your feedback. We truly appreciate every opportunity to review and improve the experience we offer to our guests. I have looked into your comments and would like to provide a bit of clarity:

Our rates increase year on year to accommodate fuel increases and inflation e.t.c. approximately 6% was the increase from 2024 to 2025. Unfortunately we cannot compete with commercial airline flights inter Europe as we’re an entirely different experience, offering incredible private, doors-off, narrated scenic flights, operating remotely in the Okavango Delta.

Regarding payment, we are required by the banks in Botswana to accept only newer USD notes, which is unfortunately beyond our control, but we apologise for the inconvenience.

In terms of flight duration, our records show a total start-end time of 43 minutes. Flight time is measured with onboard technology offered by our manager, from engine start to engine shutdown. During busy operational periods, air traffic control/tower, navigating all light aircraft can occasionally shorten or extend take off times, unfortunately creating slight unforeseen delays on the odd occasion. Our pilots do their very best to time their scenic perfectly, it is however not an exact science, in some instances we are also flying over the scheduled 45 min.

Again, thank you for the feedback and for the opportunity to clarify, both here and in the office. We did contact your guide directly after, offering a solution. We are delighted you enjoyed the wildlife sightings, flying over the Okavango Delta by helicopter is a must do. We hope you enjoy the rest of your time in Africa.

title: Vegan Trip to Botswana was Magical!
text: It was an amazing once in a lifetime experience, that I got to experience twice.  I visited on my own in May of 2025, and just loved every moment with the animals, that I decided to return in January of 2026 with my new fiancee.  The first time I tried the mobile safari with 3 nights at Deception Valley Lodge.  The mobile safari allowed us to get very close to the animals in their own habitat.  But the lodging experience was a bit rough.  So the second time we decided to treat ourselves to luxury and only stay at Lodges.  It was 3 nights at Moremi and 3 nights  back at Deception.  Helene was super helpful in making sure everything was vegan during the mobile safari.  She also coordinated with the lodges to make sure they only served me vegan food.  I really enjoyed the camaraderie of traveling with my fellow vegans.  The staff was absolutely amazing.  They really cater to your needs and make a point to get to know you.  Botswana is magical and is mostly undiscovered.  As opposed to safaris in Kenya and Tanzania, or places like Bali, that have been overrun by tourism.

title: Honeymoon Safari
text:
My wife and I went on an 8 night honeymoon safari in Botswana with Shangana Safaris, spending two nights in the Okavango Delta, three nights in Moremi and then three nights in Khwai. We camped rather than staying in lodges. 

As we are both vegan, we were unsure about our safari options when first looking around, and Shangana Safaris offer a vegan safari package which was our main reason for booking with them. 

The food on our safari was incredible and you would never realise it had been prepared and cooked in a camp site rather than a restaurant kitchen. We had an amazing range of different meals each day and were never left hungry. 

Our guide, Joseph, was both an incredibly friendly and welcoming person as well as a very knowledgeable guide who cares deeply about the conservation work within the game reserves. His expert experience meant he was able to spot animals from far away and follow tracks and animal patterns, to ensure that we had amazing sightings of so many different animals which we would never have found by ourselves. 

We had some last minute flight time changes coming into Maun, my wife’s luggage was lost by the airline and I experienced some motion sickness while in the camp when it was too late for me to find a shop to buy tablets in. Helene and David worked around the flight changes for us, helped to track down our luggage and arrange for it to be transported to the camp, and purchased motion sickness tablets to be brought down for me. 

We really felt that everyone involved with our safari went out of their way to help with any issues we had and to make our stay as comfortable and enjoyable as possible. We are both so thankful to everyone for making our honeymoon so special and memorable for us.

Botswana as a country has the most amazing game reserves and wildlife. 

We can’t recommend Shangana Safaris enough!

placeInfo.name: Shangana Safaris Okavango Botswana

title: Two phenomenal safari adventures with an experienced company.
text:
We went on two separate safaris with this company with a group of friends. The first one was 7 days and we went to the Moremi game reserve and the Khwai community concession area. Both were full of wildlife and birds. The guides were so knowledgeable about all aspects of the area, including knowing the birds, which is a special interest of ours. They also were very proud of Botswana and knew and shared many details of this wonderful country. We traveled with 12 people and had two guides with safari vehicles, four staff and one chef for support. The guides were very respectful of the wildlife and the area, and really worked hard to enable us to see a real variety of wildlife. The camp staff made our tent camping very comfortable and the food was plentiful and delicious. 
      Our second safari was also 7 days and we went to the Makgadigadi pans park and Central Kalahari park. This time there were on six of us, one guide, two camp staff and one chef.  the same comments for above, go here as well. The guide was so accommodating to our interests and we saw so many new animals and birds. He was so patient stopping when we asked as well as when he saw something of interest. He really know the flora as well, which is so integral to understanding the challenging conditions of this area. Again, the camping was comfortable and food plentiful and delicious. 
     I would highly recommend this company.

title: Fantastic experience with Shangana Safaris
text:
We worked with Helene from Shangana Safaris and couldn't be happier with the safari experience she arranged for us.  The trip included safari camps in Botswana and Kenya, plus a stopover in Victoria Falls, with multiple bush plane flights and ground transfers.  All the logistics worked seamlessly which allowed us to enjoy the experience.  We essentially asked for door to door service, which we've never done before while traveling, and Helene delivered.

On our arrival in Maun at the start of the trip, Helene graciously met us at the airport and drove us to our lodging at Maun Studios, which we found to be charming.  The staff were friendly and helpful, even driving us back and forth to a local restaurant that evening since we didn't rent a car.  From Maun, we flew to Leroo La Tau, Xakanaxa, and Chobe camps in Botswana, followed by a stopover in Victoria Falls at the 528 Guesthouse, and then to Kenya where we stayed at the Karen Gables Guesthouse in Nairobi before completing our itinerary at Ol Pejeta and Entim Mara bush camps.  Without exception, all camps were excellent with top notch service and friendly, knowledgeable guides.  The lodgings in Maun, Victoria Falls, and Nairobi were also excellent and we recommend each place highly.  The food was great, with only a minor quibble that vegetarian options at some lodges were somewhat limited.  But staff members bent over backwards to accommodate us, whether it involved the food, daily activities, or other logistics.  We highly recommend all the establishments above.

Helene also arranged specific excursions for us, including a hot air balloon ride in Kenya and a visit to the the northern white rhino sanctuary in the Ol Pajeta Conservancy.

All the bush plane flights were fun and added to the experience by providing an interesting view of the varied landscapes, plus wildlife spotting opportunities.  We took a helicopter ride over Victoria Falls which was somewhat expensive for the short ride, but we would still recommend it.  Our trip was in May and the falls were thundering with huge plumes of spray which was beautiful.  The staff at 528 Guesthouse were very helpful, arranging the helicopter tour as well as an evening dinner cruise.

All this added up to a fantastic safari experience that exceeded our expectations.  Whether it was the big cats, elephants, crocodiles, giraffes, zebras, wildebeasts, jackals, hyenas, ostriches, rhinos, hippos, beautiful birds, or the many other animals we were fortunate to see, it was the experience of a lifetime and we're already thinking about future trips to Africa.  We enthusiastically recommend Helene and Shangara Safaris.

placeInfo.name: Shangana Safaris Okavango Botswana
placeInfo.address: Exit 5 Disaneng, Maun, Ngamiland East P.O.Box 319 Botswana

title: Vegan safari
text:
I just returned from a 6 night camping safari with Vegan Safari Africa/Shangana Safaris. Our guide Joseph was extremely knowledgeable, kind and a wonderful host. He truly cared for us to have a great experience and went out of his way to make sure to spot and track animals and get us as close to them as possible while still remaining safe. The rest of the staff were welcoming and accommodating and the tent accommodations were comfortable. I loved the camping experience as it felt like we were really experiencing the African bush. The vegan food was fresh, delicious and plentiful.

title: Incredible Mobile Camping Experience in Botswana
text: We went mobile camping as part of our trip through Shangana Safaris and had the most incredible experience.  We spent three nights at Moremi, three at Khwai, and two at Savuti with the same staff.  Our guide, Master, was phenomenal, as compared to all the other guides we saw on our three week trip and consider him a friend (as well as the other staff).  We asked about animal behavior, local culture, bird sounds, animal identification, the environment, plants, etc. and he knew everything.  He could spot animals at an instant and could track prints to find the big predators.  We had a large cabin tent, cots with mattresses, rugs inside, hot water morning and night, and a private latrine and shower area attached to the back of the tent.  The staff for the two of us consisted of our guide Master, a chef, and two helpers that set up camp and took good care of us.  The owners, David and Helene, supplied great food, snacks, and drinks.  We would have a morning drive starting in the dark, a coffee/tea/snacks break at a nice location (table, chairs, table cloth) mid-morning, and were back at camp for lunch.  The afternoon drive started at 4:00 and then we would set up at a nice animal viewing location to watch the sunset with snacks/drinks.  We would arrive back at camp after dark, have dinner, sit around a campfire, and start it all over again the next morning. This type of camping allows you to camp in the parks so you do not waste travel time, hear the animals at night, smell the wild sage and basil permeating the air, experience a night drive at one park, and avoid many of the crowds you see at Chobe.  We highly recommend Shangana Safaris!

title: Exceptional  A CKGR Safari I Loved it!
text:
I’ve lived in Maun for over 30 years and finally treated myself to a  mobile safari. I joined a friend from Australia who was keen to see the Central Kalahari, and we booked with Shangana Safaris. This was a good  decision.
Effortless and genuine. Locally-run outfit knows what they’re doing, everything just worked, with no fuss.
We saw large herds of oryx and springbok, and had the luxury of enjoying it all without crowds.
Comfort in the bush. Warm beds, spotless bush ensuite, and little extras when you least expected making for a fantastic experience ,surprises like homemade yogurt, fresh breads, desserts, and wine by the fire. 
A fantastic crew. Master (guide), Moffet (camp hand), and MG (chef) made us feel welcome, safe, and thoroughly looked after.
It was relaxed, real, and I believe good value.

title: The most memorable and beautiful experience ever
text:
Helene and her team really looked after us and paid attention to every detail to make sure our trip was unforgettable. 

From the calls in advance to go over everything and understand all of our wishes for the trip, then meeting us at the airport and introducing us to our most wonderful guide Joseph, then of course the adventure began and we went on the experience of a lifetime! 

We were taken on daily tours of the bush, served great vegan meals every day, went on a dugout canoe ride, tried basket weaving and camped in the middle of the Moremi game reserve for multiple nights where we got to see these incredible animals living in their natural environment. 

We saw lions, elephants, wild dogs, giraffes, zebras, buffalo, wildebeest and more! Joseph is incredibly kind and knowledgable and was always teaching us things about the wildlife. It's hard to put into words, but these memories will stay with our family forever. 

Thank you Helene, David, Joseph and team!

title: Look no further you have found your next Botswana Safari!
text: Shangana Safaris created a trip of a lifetime for my wife and I! We worked with Helene who helped us create a 13 day trip to Botswana that included a stay at Maun Studios in Maun upon arrival. From there we did Shagana's 8 night Maun to Kasane vegan mobile safari. Followed by 3 nights at the Bakwena Lodge in Kasane. The whole trip was amazing and Helene's communication throughout the entire booking process was great. The Shangana / Vegan Safari Africa 8 night vegan safari was definitely the highlight of the trip. During that portion of the trip we were accompanied by only one other guest from California and then three team members from Shangana. The Shangana team during the mobile safari included Joseph as our guide, MG was the cook,  and Obi who assisted MG and set up camp. Joseph was very knowledgeable and a great guide. He really made the most out of everyday, and by the end of the trip we truly felt spoiled on how well he was able to track animals as well as share so much information about the conservation efforts Botswana has performed since their independence. We were also amazed on how delicious the food was during the camping.  MG’s culinary skills were amazing and we were surprised on how every meal was different. It even gave us a few ideas to try at home.  And we can’t forget Obi! He was very nice and was on top of any request we had while we were at camp. We are so glad we chose to go with Shangana Safaris and would choose them again if we ever make it back out to Botswana (which we hope we do!). Shangana's passion for the environment and conservation, communication, and affordability are a just a few of the many great qualities that should make them your next choice for a Botswana safari.

title: Fabulous time
text:
We stayed for 5 nights recently at the hotel. This is our 2nd stay with Pangolin as the first time in August 2018 was just a half day event.

If you planning your first safari and not into photography yet, may I suggest you make Pangolin your first stop n stay. As others have mentioned this a photographer’s dream as the setup is catered to the needs of a photographer even like a novice like me. 

The Chobe river is one of the most accessible place in Botswana for wildlife sightings and in abundance. This makes sightings easy and photo opportunities plentiful. Just going on a safari with your iPhone or pocket camera, does not do justice to the trip. The best part of Pangolin, everything is taken care of and you just bring home the memories in a SD card.

The room setup is clean, comfortable with lots of storage space. Food and drinks are always available. There is a photo editing room and the photo host (Zac) are there to help you with your editing in between the activities. 

The team (Guts, Dan, Zac, Matt, Doc, Chef, Walter, Shorty) are full of fun. We had 3 different photo hosts (Guts, Zac, Walter) over the 6 days and you can tell the superior technical skills n quality of teaching of the photo hosts. One feedback for further improvement, the photo host should not be focus on taking photos on his own camera (only for a minimal), your guests are the priority and so what if the photo host miss the shot of a lifetime. Too bad I guess.

Would definitely revisit the place and encourage you to make a trip and get Guts (the master) or Zac (upcoming) to be your host.

ownerResponse.text: Thanks Michael! Great feedback and really glad you had such a great time with us, Ive passed on your words to all mentioned and lets hope we see you again soon!

title: A Fabulous Time with Pangolin Photo Safaris
text: From photographing wildlife to the food to comfortable accommodations to organization, Pangolin Photo Safaris knows how to do it right. I went with 2 friends and we all had a wonderful time.  I'm not sure if this is the right safari for someone with absolutely no interest  in photography, because every boat or land game drive is designed to maximize photo opportunities. Plus one of the key features of each drive is that you are accompanied by a photo host who provides exceptional guidance on improving your photography skills. We were very, very fortunate to have Sabine as our photo host.  She was superb at making everyone on the trip, from beginner photographers to more advanced photographers, feel absolutely comfortable asking questions, seeking advice, and increasing their photography skills. The guides are also top notch.  How they see  the tiniest of wildlife while driving a land cruiser or a boat is amazing.  They help spot the wildlife and then work hard to maneuver the vehicle in a position where everyone gets a good photo shot. I've been on other safaris where the tour guide is more interested in getting  his/her own photos than helping the clients. This is definitely not that type of tour!  Sabine spent 100% of her time helping us get the shots that we wanted.  She also spent time in the afternoons providing one-on-one instruction on photo editing/reviewing our photos. These were also invaluable sessions. All in all, if you want a unique photography experience that leaves you completely satisfied at the end, you will not go wrong with choosing to go with Pangolin.

ownerResponse.text: Thanks so much - you are absolute correct in that this is a safari for all levels of wildlife photography enthusiasts! Its great to hear that Sabine was able to help you improve your photography and im sure the results were some cracking images! Thanks for the fab review!

title: Our Pangolin Experience was Exceptional
text:
We picked Pangolin for our first trip to Africa, as we're avid photographers and had become enchanted by the Chobe wildlife and the Pangolin crew and hotel, watching their adventures during the pandemic from our home in the US. We opted for the "Best of the Chobe" package, which split our seven-day trip between the Pangolin Voyager House Boat and the Pangolin Hotel. 

This trip was absolutely spectacular--well organized, well run, and extremely hospitable and accommodating to everyone who is visiting. The entire staff made us feel welcomed, appreciated, and the rapport within our group and the others staying at the hotel was wonderful. 

We had the unfortunate experience of losing our baggage on the way to Botswana, and we got daily updates from Dan and Ndibo, who were calling the airline each day on our behalf, offering to bring items and our bag to us on the houseboat, if it arrived. The whole team went out of their way to ensure we had everything we needed, tracking down toothbrushes, hairbrushes, and even a photo card reader for us to improve our stay. 

Our group was mixed in terms of photographic background and skill, and our photo host Will  was excellent and ensuring everyone understood how their camera worked, without slowing things down for experienced photographers. Our boat and game drive guides also were incredible in their knowledge and ability to help us get the 'perfect' positioning for our shots went a long way--even going out of their way to ensure that I got multiple attempts at the perfect shot of a malachite kingfisher, as we hadn't seen many. 

The food and service were also exceptional and we're already discussing when we'd like to visit again.

This was one of the first trips I ever felt emotional about leaving the place, people, and experience behind--it's just that perfect. If you're on the fence, just book it! You won't regret it.

ownerResponse.text: Thank you so much for taking the time to write such a beautiful review, ourselves we are just chuffed that you enjoyed your stay with pangolin both on the houseboat and the at the hotel! lets hope we see you again soon!

title: Absolutely fantastic: people, location, food and photography !
text:
As an introduction to Africa and to safaris in particular, Pangolin Photo Safaris could not have done anything better to enhance our experience. From the quality and professionalism of the expert photographers’ tuition and the guides abundant knowledge of the wildlife of the Chobe area to the very high standards of the accommodation and cuisine in particular. Guests were welcomed back home to the houseboat or lodge hotel by warm, enthusiastic staff who clearly enjoyed working for Pangolin.
The expert photographers were able to engage with all guests regardless of their prior photographic experiences or ability. In our case I was able to demonstrably improve my skill set from being a competent landscape photographer to now being able to capture both the essence and detail of subject matter that actually moves and has a mind of its own. Over the course of the holiday my wife went from being a strictly ‘point, shoot and not expect much’ to being able to take photographs that she was happy, on occasions, to share with others. I thoroughly enjoyed seeing her confidence grow and for her to actually begin to enjoy a hobby that I have long appreciated. Pangolin Photo Safaris either provide you with an excellent camera and lens or the experts give guidance on using your own equipment.
The level of service by the staff and the commitment to customer satisfaction is evident from the outset. The photo hosts join their small group (no more than eight) for sundowners and dinner and seem to take a genuine interest in ensuring that their guests get the best experience possible. My wife also enjoyed one afternoon just soaking up the environment, with a pair of binoculars, without concentrating on the photography. That was fine too.
Ours was a truly memorable holiday. We left with a strong desire to return and to do go on another Photo Safari with Pangolin.

ownerResponse.text: Thanks Roy such magic words! Its great that you were able to have such a great experience and im certain you managed to get some great shots during your stay! Till the next time.....

text:
Wonderful experience and customer service.  Booking was easy and I received same day answers to multiple questions by email from Joy and others.
Our flight was cancelled due to a storm but rescheduled for the next morning without a problem.  A driver picked us up at our Maun hotel and returned us afterwards - very convenient.  Kane, our pilot, was professional and gave just the right amount of info (not too much).  We found Wildebeest, Zebra, Elephants, Hippos, Giraffes and Crocodiles.  I'm in a wheelchair and getting into the front seat was not a problem (with help).  
NOTES:  Sunscreen on your arms is a must.  I used a 200 - 600mm lens (full frame) on my DLSR and it was too long.  100 - 300mm would have been better at 500 feet up (150m).  I'm 6' 1" (185cm) and fit in the front but would not have fit in back.

title: Flying over the Delta at sunset is priceless
text: Even though we went in dry season and the Delta had little water, it was an amazing experience. The views of the landscape at sunset are spectacular. We booked the last flight that was at 17:00. We were two couples, each in a helicopter. The pilots were very nice and explained what we were seeing. The helmets have a built-in microphone so you can talk quietly. You can tell they have trained eyesight because they saw animals that we didn't even intuit. The flight leaves Maun airport, so you have to carry the passport on top (we had to go back to the accommodation to get them) and pass a security check (liquids, weapons, etc.) You can carry a backpack that is stored under the seats.

title: Amazing service from Horizon Helicopters raised the bar, and delivered memories to last a lifetime.
text:
Helicopter Horizons is the first helicopter tour my wife and son had been on, and from start to finish, this company exceeded our expectations and raised the bar (see note below on why they raised the bar!).

We initially booked a 45-minute flight from Maun airport at 3 pm, but then discovered from our safari director that it would not be possible because we were due to go to Moremi that day and the gates closed at 6:30 pm. Horizon was very quick to respond to my query about changing the flight, and we switched our flight to Khwai Airstrip and opted for 30 minutes.

On the day, the helicopter flew into Khwai on the dot, and a very helpful pilot (sorry, I can’t remember his name :-( ) provided an excellent experience. The Okavango Delta is a pretty magical place, but Horizon literally provided an otherworldly perspective—something we will never forget for the rest of our lives.

So, why did Horizon raise the bar? As part of our visit to Southern Africa, we also booked a helicopter flight in Zimbabwe to see Victoria Falls. That company’s flight on the day was shorter than advertised, and we just kept comparing it to the amazing service from Horizon! Horizon’s helicopter duration was exactly as advertised and a bit more. Now that’s exceeding your customer’s expectations! Horizon raised the bar—well done and thank you!

Thank you very much to Joy, Bonolo, and Ludo. And last but not least, thank you to the helicopter pilot for a truly unforgettable experience (sorry I forgot his name). So, if you are in beautiful Botswana and the Okavango Delta, definitely book a flight with Horizon.

ownerResponse.text: Hi Chevaan, thank you so much for taking the time to review and for your positive feedback. We are so glad that you and your family enjoyed your experience with us and we hope to see you in the future again. We have shared your kind words with your pilot, Zac, and those mentioned above. Thank you again.

The restaurant has good food that we all enjoyed. We sat under a shady bit to enjoy our meal. Unfortunately the water park  was closed for the winter (southern hemisphere winter) so I can’t comment on that. The park was clean and tidy and the staff were helpful and friendly.

title: Spectacular rock art within easy access of Shakawe region
text: This was our second visit to Tsodilo Hills. After several weeks of self-drive safari adventures through Moremi and the Central Kalahari, we thoroughly enjoyed viewing spectacular rock art under the tutelege of our local guide. The hills themselves are striking as one approaches the site, rising out of the flat plains. No booking necessary. Hiking was easy, offering access to a range of rock art and a switch for the typical focus on wildlife viewing. Visitors should be aware that the roads within the facility include traversing deep mud, so a 4x4  is must. Highly recommend cabins and camping available at nearby Drotsky's Cabins.

placeInfo.name: Tsodilo Hills
placeInfo.address: Near Sepupa Northwest Botswana, Shakawe Botswana

title: Were expecting more
text: From our accommodation near Shakawe, we drove first about 20 kilometers via the A35 to Ncamasere, then via a sandy and partly corrugated-iron road to the entrance to the UNESCO World Heritage Site Tsodilo Hills. At the checkout you can choose between different guided hikes, we chose the Rhino Trail. This cost 50 BWP for adults, 10 BWP for children plus 120 BWP for the guide. After paying, you drive a short distance to the parking lot of the small museum. Guides will then wait there for the booked tour. On the day of our visit, unfortunately, only one guide was present, as according to his statement, the others were at a funeral. We therefore had to wait quite a long time, as he had yet to make a tour with other tourists. When this was done, he took another lunch break, so we had to wait 1-2 hours in the car until our tour started. This remained well below our expectations, as we did not walk too much, but drove a large part by car. I don't know if that's normal, but other reports have mentioned hikes. The guide explained the rock drawings there at numerous stations, which was quite interesting and worth seeing. However, in Patagonia we did a similar tour at the Cueva de las Manos (Cave of Hands), which was much better organized. Therefore, there is still a lot of potential and room for improvement...

title: Beautiful view to nature in Gaborone.
text: Beautiful view to nature in Gaborone. To access Kgalle Hill you need to make a medium easy trail through the so-called "Sleeping Giant". It needs some physical conditioning, but the view is worth it.

title: Worth it. But follow someone at the start otherwise you might get lost.
text: I climbed Kgale hill with my 21 year old daughter on a Sunday. It took about 50 mins to get to the top. I last climbed it in 1975 and have a photo from the top. Sadly the final bit is now blocked off by a radio tower. It’s a decent steady walk. Surprisingly clean but not well signposted hence the 4*. Luckily we followed someone otherwise it would have been stressful.  We took an iDrive (Uber equivalent) to the car park at c8:30 am to avoid the heat. It was quite busy with lots of families and even a scout group at the top. We felt totally safe. There is not a great deal to do in Gaborone by Western tourist standards so worth doing this.

title: Well worth the climb.
text: This is a great place to go and hike. The view from the top is fab, as you can see the Gaborone Dam. It can get busy on weekend mornings and the track is narrow. Some litter which is really sad to see.

title: The 3 S's 》Splendid, Superb & Spectacular
text: Splendid properties, superb service & spectacular game drives were our experience  each day of our safari thanks to Helene's expertise & personal touch in booking & making all arrangements. She synthesized numerous emails from my friend & I into a magical itinerary that suited both our tastes & preferences without either of us having to settle & created the trip of a lifetime for us. She truly paid attention to our desires & made them a reality. We were always looked out for every step of the journey traversing 6 lodges/camps in 4 countries over 2 weeks & every regional flight, boat ride & vehicle transport was expecting us & ready to keep the thrills coming & joy flowing. Whether it's your first trip to Africa like my friend's or fourth visit to the continent like mine you won't find a better planner or have a better experience with anyone else or another company.

placeInfo.name: Shangana Safaris Okavango Botswana

title: Amazing vegan safari!
text:
We had an amazing safari experience! Helene arranged a fantastic tour for us with our mobile tented safari. We felt like the locations were well chosen and each gave us a different perspective.  She also arranged our pre and post stays. The Maun
studios, Chobe Bakwena Lodge and 528 Guesthouse were all wonderful. Good thing we had an overnight in Maun studios presafari as the airline lost a suitcase. On
our mobile safari, all of the staff were friendly, diligent and
 professional.  We appreciated the fact that Shangana does not use radios in their safari vehicles.  The radios invariably result in safari becoming an artificial Disneyworld/spectator sport- mobs of safari vehicles surrounding each sighting.  Without the radio,  the guides can really use their skills and we have a true adventure each day with sense of real Africa  Special commendation to our guide Master whose expert tracking
skills and knowledge of the animals placed us in the right places at
 the right time to have some wonderful experiences alone with the
 wildlife without the safari paparazzi.  This included being surrounded by hundreds of elephants crossing the river and hanging out with wild dogs during their afternoon siesta.  My favorite was when we came across a crowd of safari vehicles watching a leopard hidden in some grass eating her kill. He asked us if we wanted to join the queue as night was falling. We decided that we did. But after our turn when all the other vehicles moved on, he suggested we linger a bit longer.  We got to see a National Geographic special unfold -as a hyena followed the scent to the leopard who raced with her dinner up a tree.  The hyena waited patiently for the bone scraps that eventually fell then left.  The leopard came down again for a final cleanup. So many magical moments.  All the tourists rushing off to the next photo op missed it. As for camp-the tents and cots were comfortable. The food was excellent.  I loved that we had local dishes like pumpkin stew. I was especially impressed with the gourmet vegan cheeses-even better than what is available in the US! I could eat those cheeses with that crispbread everyday! Our Botswana safari was a fantastic adventure. Compared to my earlier safari in Tanzania, the Botswana safari was a far more authentic experience. We got to be fully immersed in the African ecosystems.  It was amazing to hear elephants and lions walking through camp at night while we were safe in our tents. A honeybadger even broke into our toilet tent to get the water in the basin.  How cool is that? This was a REAL safari !

placeInfo.name: Shangana Safaris Okavango Botswana
placeInfo.address: Exit 5 Disaneng, Maun, Ngamiland East P.O.Box 319 Botswana

title: Amazing experience
text: My wife, our eldest daughter and I spent a week in Moremi and Khwai with David and Helene's incredibly proficient team.  Apart from living inside a Nat Geo magazine for the week, the guides and support crew made the week for us.  Nothing was too difficult for them and their knowledge of the environment and wildlife was fascinating and very educational.  Camp locations, camp facilities and vehicles were all excellent - as was the food - catering for all dietry needs - nothing overly flash but very wholesome and more than satisfying!  We'd love to return to Bots (well, Maun specifically) and spend more time exploring an amazing part of our planet.  Thanks Helene, David and team for a wonderful experience.  PS After Moremi we spent another 10 days kayaking on the Okavango with David's brother, John.  That was an equally rewarding experience!  Hence the urge to return!

placeInfo.name: Shangana Safaris Okavango Botswana

title: Experience the wild
text:
Truly immersive experience in nature's wonderland-Okavango! Each day was filled with fascinating encounters with animals in the wild, observing their behaviour amongst themselves and interacting/communicating with other species.The incoming waters at  the Delta was a veritable Noahs Ark- attracting the entire animal kingdom! The big 5 - in the first morning.
Highlights-witnessing a leopard cub dragging an impala up a tree, wild African dogs feeding 14 pups at their den, two male lions marking their territory around our camp
Shangana Safaris' Helene was professional , always available and organised a very safe and enjoyable experience for our family - parents and twin girls
Our camp guides were extremely well informed, camp catering was generous and of a high standard

placeInfo.name: Shangana Safaris Okavango Botswana
placeInfo.address: Exit 5 Disaneng, Maun, Ngamiland East P.O.Box 319 Botswana

title: Good value!  Good sightings, recommend it!
text:
We  (a family of 3 with a teenager) did the 4D3N Delta + Moremi tour with 2 nights at Lazy river and 1 night at Moremi.
David (owner)/Helene  did our booking. Both were very responsive. 

The Lazy river camp site was next to a quiet and beautiful lagoon. The shared toilet/shower onsite were clean.  The bucket shower worked well, the water was warm enough.  Elephants and hippos were nearby. We could hear them. 

Moremi portion: We saw 3-4 sightings of lions and one with 4 cubs (pretty close to them!).  We were happy with the sightings as  we were only there  for  a day and half We didn't see the leopard - time was a little short.   Other animals were spotted (giraffes, elephants, red leches, ostrich, jackals, even a honey badger!)  The camp staff and guide were helpful in general.  This was our last stop for our  3-week long trip. If we had more time, the adults would have stayed for 2 more days.  But my teenage kid had enough game drives ...(she had more than 20+ game drives already by the time we got to Moremi).

A suggestion: maybe do the mokoro trip at Ditshiping station if time allows. We had free time to go a little further on the mokoro trip day.

placeInfo.name: Shangana Safaris Okavango Botswana
placeInfo.address: Exit 5 Disaneng, Maun, Ngamiland East P.O.Box 319 Botswana

title: Moremi day safari
text:
This was the most amazing experience……… a day safari they said… we didn’t expect what we got at all……. 
The ride out  to Moremi was beautiful… we stopped for hot drinks and beautiful rusks, then we were off…… 
We had an amazing guide Joseph who stopped at nothing to track the animals. We were so lucky to see a plentiful of giraffe, elephant, hippo zebra, ostrich, impala, and literally hundreds of marabou storks…… when we thought it couldn’t get any better we found 3 beautiful lionesses resting under shrubs, we had the pleasure of taking in their magnificent. 
Then as we turned to leave we found 5 small cubs with another lioness that had been watching us. Just amazing…… 
Just for the record our lunch was 5* , it was like a bistro meal. A laid table, with an abundance of beautiful homemade foods. 
We can’t recommend this company enough, 
Thank you

title: Camping at Khutse Moreswe campsite
text: Khutse camping with my VIP multi repeat guest and mostly we stay at Moreswe 01. The most remote camping area is very important to our stay.

title: Faulty rescue system
text:
The reserve is nice, BUT! And this is a big one: Don't go alone! The reserve fails to provide you with emergency contact details in the event of an accident. Nor do they have emergency or recovery vehicles on site, or at least not one at all times.... This is a significant problem since if you experience car problems, you're on your own. Also, know that cellphone service and connection are VERY limited.

*Note this is a wild reserve and you really don't want to get stuck without shelter (for example a car) for long periods of time.

title: Nightmare in Khutse
text: Please go in a convoy when visiting this reserve, if your car breaks down you are on your own. No safety measures are communicated with you at the gate and there is limited signal. They also only have one vehicle so if it is off site, you will have to wait for either the conservationist in the nearest town or for them to return. Our car exploded and we had to walk 14+ km to get signal. After informing the gate that we are stranded, no haste was made to help us as they did not have a vehicle on site (they were patrolling and would pick us up when they got to the part we were stranded in. The conservationist from the neatest town hurried to our rescue but we were stuck for 4 and a half hours and had to wait for them for an hour in the dark. Please be careful as we were told this happens quite often.

title: Herds of elephants
text: Good booking. Very empty. Just you and the herds of elephants. Only by 4x4 pickup truck. It is a 1-hour drive from Letlhakeng and then drives on soft sand within the reserve. The images are from National Geographic.

title: Breathtaking.  Worth the paperwork
text:
There is no doubt that this is something of a hidden treasure. It is not easy to get to - a long and corrugated 60 miles from Letlhakane - but worth the effort.  On arrival in mid April there were only 3 other couples in the whole park. Over 6 days we only saw 3 other vehicles, but 26+ elephants, 8 lions, herds of springbok, family groups of kudu, hartebeest, wildebeest, giraffe, ostrich and dozens of bird types.

It’s a bit of a palaver booking and paying. The camps within the reserve ar administered by Bigfoot Safaris and need to be booked prior to arrival. Entry to the park is controlled by DWNP and must be booked ahead and paid for on arrival (in cash!)  
Because we were travelling and had little airtime and spotty wifi we managed to arrange to pay Bigfoot over the phone but DWNP required a meeting with a ranger in a Choppies carpark upon our return.

We stayed in Molosi 01 and Maleswe 02.  Both camps are close to pumped waterholes. You can see the waterhole from Maleswe and are on the elephant commuter route to/from at Molosi (where elephants had wrecked the bucket shower and tried to push over the latrine.  But hey! That’s what you’re paying for.

title: wild camping at its best
text: Yes this was  a fantastic game reserve. Lions walked right past our tent. we rented a stunning4x4 from Choe4x4 in Maun. Drove through the Kalahariand spent our last three nights in kutse. we saw Cheetah, elephant Lion and san people. its thick sand but our land cruiser was very powerful and the thick sand was no problem

title: Awesome View of the Okavango Delta
text: After 7 days of getting an "African Massage" and thoroughly enjoying all the wildlife, my wife and I looked forward to the helicopter flight included in our travel arrangements and a different perspective of the Okavango Delta.  We were not disappointed!  Hippos, elephants, crocodiles were everywhere along the winding Khwai River and its endless marshes.  From above, it seems the Delta goes on forever, Great views! What an awesome experience our Pilot Brendon provided for us.  Professional and super courteous he narrated so nicely what was going on below.  Make sure the doors are off!  Thank you Brendon and Helicopter Horizons for a most memorable experience.

ownerResponse.text: Hello Mark, we are so happy to hear you had a wonderful experience flying with us and the pleasant words for Brendon. It all sounds magical, it truly is a unique way to view the Delta. We hope to have you back flying with us in the future and create even more memories.

title: Magnificent.
text:
Life has gotten in the way of me writing this review as our flight was in June 2022 but better late than never.............. 
Our trip was originally booked for 2020 but as for many others C19 had other plans for us. HH rescheduled without any issues and for that we were grateful. At all times correspondence was swift. 

To the flight itself......Unforgetable, one of those experiences which will last us a lifetime.  Our pilot was a lovely young man called Dylan, professional and friendly. We had the sunset trip with a sundowner stop. We had Okavango "The Sentinal" Gin rather than Champagne, we much prefer gin and the locally made gin is delicious.  

We had doors off the helicopter so that we were able to take photos. The scenery, the sunset brought me to tears. Stunning, simply stunning.  It might be a wee bit costly but as we get to our twilight years and realise that you have to live for today as tomorrow is never certain, I say do it now. You won't regret it.

title: Take a walk for a quick stop view
text:

Information Boards are helpful to give context.

placeInfo.name: Boab Prison Tree

title: Boab Prison Tree
text:
The Historic Baobab tree is situated in front of Kasane  Police Station.  which was hollowed out and used to hold up to 14 people.This particular Baobab tree played a major role in the administration of justice, distribution of mail and labour recruitments in northern Botswana. It was used as a gaol before the advent of modern prisons in the area.
The prison baobab has some cavities which served as prison cells in historic times. There are some windows like openings which appear to have provided both light and ventilation into these prison cell.
