title: Better than we imagined!
text: What an amazing trip! I normally plan all our trips, but with a trip to Africa for a family of 4 I was overwhelmed and knew I needed help. A quick chat with Ian, and I knew I made the right decision. He asked great questions to get to know our family and what we were looking for and made excellent recommendations. It was better than we could have imagined and everything went so smoothly thanks to Ian and Lorna’s great planning. The logistics from one location to the next involves a lot of coordination, so you really need someone with experience to plan it all out. We started in Cape Town, then went to three camps by African Bush Camps, and then ended at Victoria falls. The camps were fantastic and the service was incredible. Our kids are 9 and 11 and we all had the best time. We’re already starting to plan where in Africa we’ll go next!

title: The Ultimate Experience!
text:
From the moment we stepped off the plane in Cape Town to the moment we boarded our plane for home, our trip was perfectly planned, organized, coordinated - there was not one glitch!

Researching safari companies is overwhelming and a friend recommended Ian at Ultimate Africa Safaris. Ian’s unique experience helped guide and define our safari adventure. He developed an itinerary which met our wants, needs and budget!

The trip exceeded our expectations and we plan to make a return trip very soon with Ultimate Africa Safaris. Thank you Ian and Lorna!

title: What an adventure!!!
text:
We normally plan our own adventures but for our first ever trip to Africa we reached out to Ultimate Africa Safaris for their expertise. Ian Proctor created a perfect itinerary for us, took care of all the transfers/accommodations, and gave us the peace of mind that all the moving parts of this adventure would come together smoothly.

We could not have hoped for a better trip...

We flew Seattle, WA to Doha, Qatar. We had an overnight layover so we booked a room at the hotel inside the airport. The hotel was a great place to rest and we were able to get up in the morning and walk right to our gate without having to worry about checking our luggage or navigating security again.

First stop was Cape Town, South Africa. 
A driver picked us up at the airport and delivered us to a boutique hotel, Kensington Place. Two days of private cape and wine country tours and then it  was time for Safari.

Second Stop was Khwai Leadwood Camp, Botswana.
Our driver delivered us back to the airport where we caught our flight to Maun, Botswana. After a short stay in the private Wilderness Air Lounge we were off to Khwai for 3 nights. The tented rooms were amazing, the staff was attentive to our every need, and the wildlife did not disappoint. Game drives, scenic helicopter flight(with 3 night stay), mokoro canoe excursion...WOW is all I can say about our stay.

Third Stop was Linyanti Bush Camp, Botswana. (3 nights)
We transferred via helicopter...What a great way to go!!! Again the tented rooms were amazing. Every detail was taken care of to make sure we had wonderful stay. One thing we didn't realize was how much delicious food we would encounter...Breakfast, Tea stop on morning game drive, Lunch, Afternoon Tea, Sundowner on evening game drive, and Dinner, all accompanied by wine pairings or your favorite drink of choice.

Fourth Stop was Camp Okuti. (2 nights)
A short plane ride transferred us to the final camp. This camp was a little more self service than the first two but still paradise. Every activity we did provided something memorable that made this adventure special.

Final Stop Victoria Falls, Zimbabwe. (2 nights)
A plane transferred us to Kasane Airport, Botswana. A driver helped us pass through the border to Zimbabwe and brought us to Batonka, a nice boutique hotel in Victoria Falls. The next morning our driver picked us up for a tour of the falls, we had lunch overlooking Zambezi river, and finished the trip with a nice dinner at Three Monkeys restaurant in town.

The only bummer was we had to go home...Our driver picked us up that final morning and brought us to the Victoria Falls Airport. We flew to Cape Town, then back to Doha, and then finally back to Seattle.

Thank you Ian and Ultimate Africa Safaris for helping deliver a trip of a lifetime. I would recommend your services to anyone considering and African adventure. I truly hope that we have the opportunity to use your services again in the future.

title: Excellent advice and bookings for a Botswana adventure
text: Ultimate Africa Safaris did a wonderful job in planning and booking my Botswana safari.  Lorna and Ian kept me posted throughout on their action plan on how to achieve the perfect adventure for a solo guy.  Through their extensive network of Botswana outfitters and safari lodges, they were able to get me the best value and experience for my budget.  Extremely pleased with their professionalism.  In the end, I was able to get a private safari guide and vehicle for almost all of my two week trip.  Not only that, I would be staying at three well-situated and luxurious Great Plains Conservation lodges.  Thanks so much... and shall book again.

title: Incredible African safari!
text:
Our family recently returned from a truly, Trip-of-a-Lifetime, in southern Africa.  

We began in Cape Town, then to Botswana for two safari camps, and then a short visit to Victoria Falls before heading home.  The accommodations were lovely, the safaris were absolutely incredible, the staff were delightful, welcoming and helpful, and the food was so delicious!  Khwai Leadwood was gorgeous, and every aspect (our Guide DT, host Nicco, chef CB, and all the staff, accommodation, food) was impeccable! Not to mention the amazing wildlife!  We also loved our stay at Linynanti Bush Camp - we saw even MORE wildlife!  Our Guide Joe, hostesses Jess and Selly, and all the staff were awesome and so down to earth.  We loved how they ate meals with us, allowing us to get to know each other, and they were just SO MUCH FUN!!  Truly, going to miss them all.  We also met some lovely fellow travelers from all around the world.

Ian and Lorna were fantastic!  Their knowledge, guidance, attention to detail, and patience with us as first time travelers to Africa, made every aspect go so smoothly.  We felt very well taken care of.  I very highly recommend Ultimate Africa Safaris!  …and I can’t wait to go again!!

title: African Safari Travel Advice
text:
My wife and I travel the world extensively. We like to go to places that are off the beaten path but known for specific experiences - South East Sulawesi for diving as an example. We recently went to Zimbabwe and Botswana. Here is our secret for ensuring the best experience possible:

Before you go find someone who is intimately familiar with the area. Even though we lived in Europe for 3 years we always find a local expert to help us understand the current conditions. Before we plan our trip. Unfortunately, this is a difficult thing to do. Almost all travel agents do not travel extensively to the places they book. This is the issue. They really don’t know these places well. So even if you find an agency that has high marks from travelers they tend to book to many locations and really have no idea what is happening on the ground.

The most important thing you can do before you go to Africa is find someone who has been to the area where the camps are recently, been to most of the camps, knows the organizations running them.

Why? Conditions change in these camps during the seasons, the animals that were there in the past may not be there anymore, the camps age quickly, the managers change. It is all very fluid.

You want an expert who has traveled these areas extensively for decades, is known by most of the important camps managements, understands the animals, guides, and the changing dynamics of the weather and what it means for the camps.

That is the best advice I can give.

My wife and I use Ian Proctor at Ultimate African Safaris. An amazing expert and our go to for South African trips.

Ultimate Africa Safaris Inc., Issaquah, Washington USA

title: Perfect For Wildlife
text: As part if our tour we stayed for 2 nights in a tented camp and had game drives during the day which were spectacular. The drive in from Maun was long and slow on rough roads but we did see wild life on the way and the area was full of fantastic viewing.  Great place to spend with a guide as they know where to go!!!

title: It’s a great place for a simple day hike
text: stunning views and peaceful hiking trails, but be prepared for a bit of off-road adventure from the gate to the gorge...perfect for nature lovers seeking a quiet escape

title: Great experience, super recommended!
text: Pat and all his team went above and beyond to make our trip to Botswana memorable. They set comfortable mobile camps in remote, exclusive locations and let us discover the beautiful land they have the priviledge to call home. Pat is extremely knowledgeable about wildlife and especially birds and we really enjoyed spending time with him exploring the Okavango Delta. Thanks for everything! Mattia and Mariavittoria

title: It couldn't get any better
text:
We were allowed to do a 4 day Mobile Safari with Pat at the Moremi Game Reserve at the end of August. We can highly recommend him. 
Pat has extensive knowledge of wildlife and plants, impressed us with his tracer skills and even speaks a little German. 
The camp and the team were also great. Glamping at its best. Of particular note is Chef Mosh, who catered for us deliciously every day. What he conjures over an open fire is incredible. 
We certainly weren't the last time with Pat on the road!
Thank you, Pat for this wonderful experience!

title: Unforgetable and fun experience!
text: This Safari trip was a dreams come true. The whole experience was amazing and every single day was unique and awesome. Not only the places from Botswana through Moremi, Savuti, Kasane to Victoria Falls in Zimbabwe were great, but also the game drives, airplane trips, boat trip and visits were so much fun and we learned so much about wildlife, saw so many fantastic animals and planta. Overall the big difference was Pat and his amazing crew. They were simply the best!!! Thanks you so much!!!

title: Precious memories to treasure forever
text: My adult son and I were taken by Pat Malatsi on a 5 day safari to Moremi Game Reserve in the Okavango Delta. It was a fabulous adventure with Pat as our guide. His knowledge of the birds and other animals was extraordinary. Not only did he know the birds names he could mimic their calls. He had great knowledge about the flora and the environment as well. The camp site was extremely well organised and the food and drinks were delicious and abundant. I felt safe even when we could hear the lions roaring at night and Pat showed us all the animals we went to see and more. I would go on another safari with Pat at the drop of a hat and highly recommend his safaris.

title: One of the best natural history guides ever!
text: Patrick is one of the best guides we've ever had. He speaks with great understanding of the wildlife, ecology, and history of Botswana. His knowledge of birds is amazing, and he's a wonderful teacher with excellent English. He and his staff are so friendly and welcoming that by the end of the trip we felt like family. We would highly recommend joining one of his trips!

title: Pure Magic: each day something new, something different, something exhilarating.
text: Pure magic. I am trying to decide what I loved best. Of course the unique animals and birds – Pat is an exemplary birder – but I also very much enjoyed the coffee mornings, and the evenings around the fire. Briefings, or catch-ups of the day. Pat’s grandmother's family live at the top of the Delta so Pat has the knowledge (and experience) to place the whole ecological system into perspective.

title: Trip of a lifetime with Pat Mobile Safari!
text: This was a long awaited, highly anticipated trip of a lifetime, and Pat Mobile Safari did not disappoint!  The accommodations (sleeping tents/bedding/showers/dining tents) were very comfortable and the game drives exceeded all expectations.  The staff in all camps was excellent, very friendly and highly accommodating.  Our every need and request was met,  and the food was sumptuous and delicious!  Our guide, Pat Malatsi, grew up in the Okavango Delta, and is exceptionally gifted, capable, personable and knowledgeable of all flora and fauna of Botswana.  He made it fun and exciting, going above and beyond to make sure our wildlife and birding experiences were top knotch. We visited four camps over 12 days….Okavango Delta, Moremi Game preserve, Savuti, and Chobe National Park…with bush flights between camps.  We were quite astonished at how close the wildlife allowed you to observe them under the expert guidance and instinct of our guide Pat.  The trip, quite simply, exceeded all expectations.

title: Unforgettable Experience
text: This safari was so excellent and above our expectations (which were high in the first place).  The camp staff worked hard to make each person's experience pleasant.  So friendly and attentive.  The food was outstanding.  I am gluten-intolerant and special effort was made at each meal to provide me with food I could eat.  In fact, there was quite a bit of jealousy over my chocolate muffins for breakfast. The accommodations were very comfortable and allowed everyone to enjoy being in the bush.  Our leader, Patrick, is tremendously knowledgeable of the plants and animals of the region, and their behavior.  He made great effort to make sure everyone was happy with what they saw and experienced.  We learned so much, enjoyed so much.  I have to add that my wife and I did not know the others in our group initially, but the environment was such that we were able to become good friends.  Maybe it was the nightly great campfires and star shows.  It was a life experience.  We will be back!

title: Pat's Mobile Safaris offers the very best  wilderness camping safari experience in Botswana, bar none!
text: In my 20+ years of traveling to Botswana and organizing safari trips for friends, Pat is the very best for mobile safaris, and I have used several different outfitters over the years. Patrick's knowledge of the wildlife of Botswana is legendary. His ability to identify all bird species by sight and sound is truly incredible, and he always seems able to find the predators for his clients from cheetahs and wild dogs to the lions and leopards. Patrick is also expert at reading track and sign as well as his ability to identify plants, insects and even the amazing constellations in the night sky. Patrick's camp staff are excellent and make being in the African bush and unforgettable experience. Patrick loves people and is an absolute  delight to be with.  In my mind, no one offers a better bush camping wildlife safari in Botswana than Patrick Malatsi. He is truly amazing.

title: Amazing safari adventure
text: Pat Mobile Safari provided us with an amazing safari experience.  We did a mobile tour of 4 sites in Botswana.  Pat's team set up camp in all four areas with "comfort camping" tents, 3 amazing meals per day, afternoon tea and cocktails by the fire.  We did two game drives per day and got unreal closeups of all of our target animals and more.  Pat's knowledge of each park, its environment and the biology was the best we have ever experience.  In addition, to the big animals, Pat provided terrific birding opportunities.  This is a terrific addition was we could learn about birds while observing other wildlife. Three people in our group identified over 200 different birds.  Pat was not only an eagle eye at spotting animals but also could identify animals by their calls and provided safe viewing experiences by understanding the animal behaviors.  Overall, Pat was the most knowledgeable and enthusiastic guide we have ever had in the 20+ guiding trips we have taken around the world.  Could not have asked for a better experience.

title: World Class Art in Middle of Kalahari
text:
The Art here is world class.  Worth a drive from Maun just to see it. The studio houses hundreds of paintings and lithographs. Also you can usually see women making traditional beads from ostrich shells.

Also visit the Kuru Museum and Cultural Center, a small community run gem that explains the history of the San.  If it isn't open, just ask around town and they usually come over to open it.

AND a young women recently opened D'Kar first restaurant across from the kgotla. Very typical food and great to support the town people

text: Beautiful campsite between the baobabs and with large swimming pool and cozy bar. You walk around the grounds and then you see something like 20 large baobabs and the necessary smaller ones. Own ‘hut’ where you can sit under with electricity and braai. Water is at the sinks/toilets. Showers vary a lot in temperature when showering, give little water and it is rather dark.

title: Not friendly or good service
text: I recognize the bad reviews before. The staff isn't very helpful or sincere friendly. If you order food during the day you are not allowed to sit in the restaurant but have to sit at te bar or pool where there is no table. Laundry service is a problem, they don't want to wash for example underwear. The internet doesn't work most of the time. The pool is freezing so there's nothing to do there. Except going to the meercats, which quite frankly is also very very expensive. The bathrooms and toilets are falling apart. There's no locks on the doors. Also doors almost fall off. The water stream is not good and there are a lot of mosquitos there. Also no running water point nearby the campsite only at the ablution. Wouldn't recommend this place. The staff is rather lazy than working hard.

title: I highly recommend.
text:
Very very nice place to end our little journey.
We opted for a rondavel because we needed to empty the bushcamper of all our belongings and repack.
Impeccable room, great swimming pool, restaurant of the same level.
Smiling, attentive staff.
I recommend ++++

title: Overnight pan & meerkat
text:
Planet Boabab is a nice chilled place with friendly staff however I write this review in the hope it will better inform anyone considering this overnight trip as information at the lodge was non existent. 
We opted for the quad bike and enjoyed the experience out on the pan , firstly you go via safari truck then pick up quads after a bumpy 1.30 hr trip to find the habituated meerkats had great interaction and plenty of time with them the guy who stays with them knows quite a bit about the kats but you do have to ask him. Time on the quads was ample for us time on the pan for sunset before continuing to over night stop . Handed warm towels to clean up the dust on arrival and then good BBQ , singing around campfire . It was cool but not cold at this time of year and the sleeping bags were great . Non flush camp toilet as you would expect. 
You sleep close to others so  no privacy , I would recommend long pants for the trip as the engine on the bike is hot on bare legs , I opted for leggings and slept in them , a hoodie for sleeping was cozy and a scarf for the quads to keep some of the dust at bay is recommended. No mozzies to worry about to just toothbrush in the way of toiletries and sun cream. 
Order your evening drinks at the lodge bar prior to leaving extra water would have been good as the allocation isn’t much . We took a very small day pack and that remains on the truck when you’re on the bikes .
I would say the trip is suitable for kids over 10 years there was a family on our trip they opted not to quad but otherwise the experience was the same for them. 
Sleeping under the stars was amazing, so glad we did this . Hope this helps .

placeInfo.name: Planet Baobab
placeInfo.address: This one should be closed, Gweta Botswana

title: Visit the meerkats
text: Planet Baobab was out gateway to visiting the meerkats, accomodation was in the form of a typical Botswana hut, with own bathroom facilities. Fan only, no air conditioning and beds were single with netting to keep you safe from any bugs and bitting things. Note beds are single and small in length so if you are over 5.6ft you may struggle for leg room. Bar and restaurant offer a good selection. Take plenty of sunscreen, insect spray is provided in your room. Staff were very friendly and helpful although the venue seemed to cater more so for German speaking clients rather then English / Australian. The meerkats were wonderful loved my time with them.

title: Brilliant Baobabs, Magnificent Meerkats and Phenomenal Pan!
text:
We stayed in one of the traditional huts this time, (previously we had camped). It was very clean and comfortable. We really enjoyed the "quirkiness" of the hut. 
The grounds are full of huge Baobabs and are kept very neat and tidy. 
The food at the restaurant is very good and the staff helpful and friendly, especially the gentleman who signs. We have met him before and he is just so lovely, always smiling. 

We went on the overnight trip to Ntwetwe pan and it has to be one of the most amazing things we have ever done in our lives. We are in our 60's and have done a lot of things but this was just outstanding.  
We were first taken to visit the habituated Meerkats, my head was used as a lookout by these wonderful little creatures, I was in heaven! 

We didn't take the Quad bikes on offer to the sleepout but our trip companions did. Watching the sunset on the pan was something which will live with me for all my days as will the sunrise. Stunning, just stunning. 

Our guide and the gentleman who cooked the food, set up camp etc were very informative, friendly and a joy to be around.  Our group was 7 people which was nice, not too big so we were able to chat and swap stories.  The beds were super warm and clean. It was like sleeping in a big envelope under the stars.  It was very cold once out of the bed but if you go prepared with warm clothing it's fine.   

We loved our stay at PB and would certainly recommend it. If you want to do something special that will live with you forever then book the overnight excursion.

placeInfo.name: Planet Baobab
placeInfo.address: This one should be closed, Gweta Botswana

title: Leider nicht gut
text:
Bei der Unterkunft waren leider keine Sauberen Handtücher da, die Toilette war sehr klein und alt, ein kleiner alter Spiegel war nur vorhanden. Die Betten hatten kein Lattenrost nur eine Steinunterlage was es sehr Hart machte. 
Der Service von den Angestellten war sehr schlecht, man musste 3 mal seine Bestellung aufgeben und selbst da hat man fehlerhafte Bestellung bekommen. Essen und Trinken hatten auch sehr lang gedauert.
Das einzige Positive war das der Pool sehr groß und gut war.

placeInfo.name: Planet Baobab
placeInfo.address: This one should be closed, Gweta Botswana

title: Great place to stop
text: We showed up without a reservation and the very nice and helpful staff were able to get us a family hut as well as get us in on a half day excursion the next day (that was already fully booked but somehow they were able to figure it out). The hut was cute and cozy. Half board is included and food was good. Meerkat and pan excursion was excellent - we also saw elephants, zebras and jackals. Kids loved the pool. Baobabs around the lodge are very impressive. Super nice staff all around. Great place to stop if driving between maun and nata.

placeInfo.name: Planet Baobab
placeInfo.address: This one should be closed, Gweta Botswana

title: The most budget option for meerkat visit. Lovely huts but crowded campsite
text: We stayed both at a campsite and in the hut. The campsite is quite horrible as the spots are very close to each other and you can hear all the noise at night. The hut was nice and kept cool even in the middle of a very hot day - good for resting! A huge bonus is that this place is the only budget option to visit habituated meerkats. The drive is one hour long and then you can have fun watching these incredible animals. Planet baobab has a quite large fenced property where you can enjoy baobabs and abundant birdlife. It's so good to walk instead of driving which is the only mode of movement in many other places in Botswana.

title: Little interest
text: Not very interesting place...unless you want to see a nesting site and vultures in flight but mid-August little water and pay for that and for 1 hour round trip....not great!!!

title: Gorgeous landscape
text: This is a gorgeous, and very untypical-Botswana, walk. But not everybody can do it: you need to be a bit agile and not be too afraid of hights. Guide is included in the high entry price, and obligatory.

title: Diamond in Gaborone.
text:
I have lunch here at least once a week. The service is always amazing and everyone super friendly. 
The food is fantastic especially there traditional dishes.

placeInfo.name: Botswanacraft

title: Excellent food,  atmosphere and service

placeInfo.name: Botswanacraft

Bonus apart from above and the very chilled atmosphere is my kids had lots of space to run around and my visitors where able to browse the superb curio shop and buy lots of regional craft. So many options !!! A must visit if in Gabs

Nice time

title: Great family breakfast! Value for money!
text: Visited for breakfast with my family. Our waitron Changu was fabulous with her attention to detail and great service! I mentioned to her that I collect plastic bottle caps for charity, and she immediately looked for some, washed them, and gave them to me! Going the extra mile - thank you!!!

placeInfo.name: Botswanacraft
placeInfo.address: Magochanyama Road Plot 20716, off Western Bypass, Block 3, Gaborone Botswana

title: Très décevant
text:
Extrêmement déçu ! J’ai dû prendre un taxi ( 50P ) pour venir jusqu’ici sur conseil de l’hôtel 
Mais grosse déception à l’arrivée ( 14:45 ) car le restaurant ferme à 15:00 donc seul le service des boissons est assuré mais visiblement le personnel est fatigué , peu avenant et peu enclin à faire un effort supplémentaire ( ce que je comprends vu les conditions de travail ) 
Néanmoins j’ai fais l’effort de prendre un taxi pour venir jusqu’ici et mon désappointement est grand.
Même chose à la boutique 
Aucune attention de la vendeuse 
Aucun conseil lorsque je pose des questions sur les objets en vente, aucun effort pour me renseigner ni m’aider. . . 
C’est vraiment dommage et cela donne, une fois encore , une très mauvaise image du pays et en particulier à Gaborone ou l’accueil du client est inexistant partout ou presque ( sauf au Service Philatélique et dans l’hôtel ou je séjourne )

ownerResponse.text: Wow, what a huge let down! I'm so sorry you had such a terrible experience, we usually go out of our way to try and make it a happy place. If you ever come back to Gaborone and Botswanacraft please ask for someone from management who can make a special effort to give you an exceptional experience to make up for lost time. Best wishes for 2026 :)

placeInfo.name: Botswanacraft
placeInfo.address: Magochanyama Road Plot 20716, off Western Bypass, Block 3, Gaborone Botswana

title: Exquisite baskets and great value for money
text:
I was focused on baskets, as basketry in Botswana is one of the finest in the world. At Botswanacraft there are baskets for all purses, and they are honestly priced following  a hierarchy that reflects the degree of intricacy of the design and the finesse of the weave. If you are after gifts that do not cost too much, head for the corner where baskets of small-medium measure are piled. You'll figure out what the numbers 1, 2, 3,...etc...mean in terms of pulas (the local currency). 
If you are after work of arts that cost you over 200 dollars (120 Euro) go to where there are baskets hung on the wall. Some are absolutely amazing. The weave is fine and the development of the design is obtained by simply threading reeds of different natural colour. I was transfixed at some of the basketry work. And, come to think of it later when I was in Cape Town, prices at Botswanacraft are really honest! My major purchase was valued around 80 dollars, but it was worth every single cent. Next time...hopefully (if I manage to go back to that beautiful country) I will get one of the expensive pieces...before gallery people in Paris, Milan, London...discover the beauty of this humble and magnificent art of the Setswana. 
I had the same experience in Laos years ago, when I purchased an incredibly beautiful, hand-woven shawl for 120 AUD, and it is now worth five times what I paid.
Do not be afraid by the price of the baskets if it is over 500 AUD or more. Just think about the quality of the craftmanship and the time spent to make them. And compare with other baskets that you may find elsewhere in Africa and sold in your country. Ad then you'll agree with me...it is worth investing in the pieces that will grace your home with something unique.

text: What a great find this was. Fabulous gifts to buy and take home. Wonderful service. There is a lovely restaurant there too. You wont wanna miss this spot !

placeInfo.name: Botswanacraft
placeInfo.address: Magochanyama Road Plot 20716, off Western Bypass, Block 3, Gaborone Botswana

title: A soul place!
text: Not for the feint hearted! This is hard-core off-road camping at its best. You need to be 100% self-sufficient; take what you need or do without. If you want game and birding around every corner then go somewhere else. That said, for spiritual peace… calm and an island in a busy world, this is a soul rejuvenating experience.

title: Wild road,wild animals and wild nature
text: Incredible nature and animal experience. Camping at wild locations where animals walk through the camp. Getting there is a real challenge and only 4x4 accessible, but it’s worth it.

title: Disappointing experience
text: Central Kalahari Game Reserve is a vast and deserted place, not for beginners! It is the world's second largest national park, about the size of Denmark. We spent three days/2 nights in there and saw only one other vehicle. In some places you can be lucky to see animals, we only saw a couple of elephant and a few antelopes and birds. There should be good chances to see lions, but we saw or heard none. The facilities at the public camp sites (which must be booked in advance - not cheap) are shaded, simple and run down. There is a fireplace, a longdrop toilet and a "shower", where you must bring your own water and put it in a bucket! Considering the exorbitant entrance fees to the park, there should be a little left over to update the park. The tracks in the park are sandy and difficult, the thorny shrubs along the tracks can give heavy scratches to the vehicles at extra cost to the rental company. Signs are worn off and impossible to read, or non existent. It is easy to get lost! Don't go in one vehicle only, go as a group of cars, if you must. We entered at Tsau Gate, near Kuke veterinarian fence, and it is a fairly long drive in loose sand before you reach the gate - and what a gate. The once beautiful traditional thatched entrance buildings are totally dilapidated - the roof gone, the toilets broken - a depressing sight. Instead an expensive tent structure serves as the office. Shame.

title: Small but worth the visit
text: The place is very small, but the interaction with the animals was still worth it, for the price they charge. The staff is very friendly. The barn owl was the most beautiful owl I have ever seen.

placeInfo.name: CARACAL Biodiversity Center
placeInfo.locationString: Kasane, Chobe National Park, North-West District
placeInfo.address: Plot 3102 Airport Road, Kasane, Chobe National Park Botswana

title: Nice stop!
text: We had a great time at the wildlife park. It’s worth a stop. It cost about $7 USD per person for a guided tour, and half the price for unguided. During the tour we got to see and learn about some local wildlife.  Most of the animals had been injured and were there for rehabilitation or for their life as they could not be returned to the wild. The facility is affiliated with Virginia Tech and has a research component as well. One feels good about supporting the operation. The guided tour took about 45 minutes and an hour is more than enough to slowly take your time through the park. It is small with maybe 20 different animals but again worth the stop.

title: Great time
text:
Where do I even begin to describe experiences that we haven’t encountered before, even after so many safaris?

First and foremost, our guide See… we have known See for years. He has often been our tracker, and now he is our guide. It’s a strange but wonderful feeling to realize you can feel quite proud of someone like that. This is exactly why we love Kwando so much; the opportunity for staff to grow—they are given the space and the chance to excel.

We mainly come for the African wild dogs. It is so helpful when a guide knows you well and no explanations are needed… and See knows us very well.
Together with tracker Balak at Four Rivers, he showed us how great results are achieved through teamwork and patience. The search for the dogs… after several game drives, we finally found them. A pack of three… and how difficult they are to follow! But when you are actually allowed to join the hunt, and the men are so incredibly skilled at tracking and keeping up, positioning the vehicle perfectly so you are right at the front during the kill… we have never experienced anything like it before. Wow, wow, wow… "Hold on Sr…" is the only thing I hear on the video, because I couldn't even film due to the speed, haha!

But the general game viewing was also a joy. How special it was to encounter a mother Serval with her cub during the day.

We thoroughly enjoyed Four Rivers. The camp is atmospheric, and the lounge has various seating areas overlooking the water—what a view! 

The bedrooms are also beautiful: spacious, with both indoor and outdoor showers, great beds, and comfortable seating inside and out. The wooden walkways to the rooms are very pleasant (the only thing we found slightly annoying was the bright LED lighting outside).

The staff is so incredibly friendly and familiar; as we say to each other, it feels like family. ❤️ Never change a winning team!

It was quiet in terms of guests during our stay; out of the five nights, we were the only two there for four of them. I have no idea why, because the concession is beautiful, all the wildlife is present, the camp is stunning, and the staff—including the guides and trackers—are amazing. Furthermore, the food (partly thanks to trainee chef Ken) was superb. 

It is truly worth visiting Four Rivers.
We will definitely be back!

placeInfo.name: 4 Rivers Camp - Kwando Safaris
placeInfo.address: Kwara Private Concession Okavango Delta, Maun, Ngamiland East Botswana

title: ⭐ 4 Rivers Camp – Kwando / Linyanti Region Guides: Mwamo & James
text:
4 Rivers was a place full of adventure, emotion and pure excitement.
Mwamo guided us with a beautiful combination of professionalism, passion and storytelling — every moment with him taught us something new about the bush, the animals and the rhythm of nature.
James, our incredible tracker, has a gift that is hard to put into words. He reads the bush like a book, senses movement before it happens, and spots wildlife with unbelievable precision.
Together they created drives that were thrilling, moving and deeply memorable.
Elephants at sunset, lions in the early morning, the magic of Linyanti’s wild energy — each day felt like a gift.
The team made us feel not just like guests, but like part of something bigger.
A truly emotional and powerful safari experience that will stay with us forever.

placeInfo.name: 4 Rivers Camp - Kwando Safaris
placeInfo.address: Kwara Private Concession Okavango Delta, Maun, Ngamiland East Botswana

title: Great location
text: Lovely location on the edge of the lagoon. Manager OP and all staff friendly and welcoming. Food excellent. Guide and tracker, Kenny and Fox, really knew there stuff and where keen to share there knowledge. Driver Hakim also very enthusiastic to share his knowledge.

placeInfo.name: 4 Rivers Camp - Kwando Safaris
placeInfo.address: Kwara Private Concession Okavango Delta, Maun, Ngamiland East Botswana

title: So Good
text:
Four Rivers
 Only praise for this camp. A lovely atmosphere, especially thanks to manager Lizzy and all the other staff members. The room is so comfortable, open, light, we love it over here. The food was good, and to sit after dinner at the fireplace, it’s always special.

With guide Kelvin and tracker JD, we had the most incredible, wild game drives, but also lots of fun, great conversations with Kelvin at dinner table…(such a smart guy), completely our thing. 

We love discovering new places, and it's great when the guide and tracker also enjoy that. It’s beautiful when you go into areas they’ve never been to before…and impressive how they always manage to find their way back…thankfully…hahaha.

We saw a lot general games animals, Wild life was good, we specifically are coming for the wild dogs, and we were so lucky to spent time with them.

Beautiful days ❤️

placeInfo.name: 4 Rivers Camp - Kwando Safaris
placeInfo.address: Kwara Private Concession Okavango Delta, Maun, Ngamiland East Botswana

title: Stunning getaway in the heart of the Delta
text:
We had the privilege of spending 3 days and 2 nights at this lovely lodge.
The setting on the river is magical and the facilities are top class.
The staff were attentive and very friendly, willing to assist at any time. Our game Ranger and Tracker (Kelvin and Mr Fox ) were most knowledgeable on our drives and made the outings fun and exciting. We saw plenty of game and experienced the natural beauty of the Delta in all its glory... something that will be imprinted in our minds forever.. 4 Rivers will not disappoint you .

placeInfo.name: 4 Rivers Camp - Kwando Safaris
placeInfo.address: Kwara Private Concession Okavango Delta, Maun, Ngamiland East Botswana

title: Great guides/trackers at a really comfortable camp
text: Wildlife is down to chance but it is the effort and skill that can make the most amazing sightings happen. My guide and tracker, Kelvin and JD, made it an excellent few days, with a great attitude and welcoming, easy-going manner. They really made it a great safari. I would recommend asking for Kelvin if you are into photography as his understanding helped get me in some great positions for pictures, which is a little different to just following animals around. The night drives are also a real benefit at this camp and I got to see some really interesting behaviour. The tents are simply fantastic, huge with excellent facilities and real comfort. Fully functioning amenities that were of a really good standard. The tents just had the fly net layer down so it is very open but this is common to the best camps I have stayed at. Food was also to a good standard and the serving staff were very good. A little warning that it is a long day with the night drives and dinner was served straight back from the night drive as it was already quite late. There are hours in the middle of the day back at camp to chill out though and grab a snooze or edit some photos. On top of all that, we had some amazing sightings of lions, hyena, leopard, wild dog, elephants, tsessebe, jackals, wattled cranes, ostrich, red lechwe, steenbok and more, including lots of smaller birdlife. Always got to be patient though.

placeInfo.name: 4 Rivers Camp - Kwando Safaris
placeInfo.address: Kwara Private Concession Okavango Delta, Maun, Ngamiland East Botswana

title: Entusiasmante
text:
Campo moderno , con nove tende molto bello e confortevole , con una vista molto bella su un fiume permanente,
La nostra guida Kelvin e il tracker KT davvero Bravi
Animali tanti un leggermente più sospettosi che in altre concessioni , comunque visti tutti gli animali anche i più rari e fotografati da vicino senza problemi

title: Directly on the river - great
text: One of the greatest lodges on our trip through Namibia and Botswana. Pick up at the Airstrip by the GM. Beautiful lodge with fantastic location. (4m from the river) Great guides (Kelvin and Tracker BB) the lodge offers everything you could wish for. Fantastic wildlife watching, only two with the two guides. Very good food and very friendly staff. Highly recommended.

placeInfo.name: 4 Rivers Camp - Kwando Safaris
placeInfo.address: Kwara Private Concession Okavango Delta, Maun, Ngamiland East Botswana

title: Lovely new camp
text:
Really enjoyed this camp. The setting was amazing, the food delicious and the staff fantastic. They made us feel at ease effortlessly. The camp was well managed by Ked along with See. The chef Titus was great, giving the table a new riddle to solve each evening. Mango & Miss T looked after us so well with everyone helping in our quest to learn some Setswana. 
Our guide Ricco had fantastic knowledge and enthusiasm for the nature & wildlife around us. We learnt so much. James the tracker was great aswell. Helping us to learn to differentiate between animal tracks. We were so lucky with our sightings. Lion cubs & sable antelope were 2 highlights. One of my favourite experiences was driving through long grass with carmine bee-eaters circling us to pick up any insects we disturbed.
We had no WiFi here which I find a bonus.

placeInfo.name: 4 Rivers Camp - Kwando Safaris
placeInfo.address: Kwara Private Concession Okavango Delta, Maun, Ngamiland East Botswana

title: Disappointing wildlige sightings but comfortable and welcoming camp
text:
As with other Kwando camps we have stayed at in Botswana we received a lovely, warm welcome. The outlook from the family tent we stayed in was very pretty, looking out over water and countryside. As a very new camp the rooms at 4 Rivers are very comfortable, spacious and well equipped. There was even some wifi, a rare occurrence currently in rural Botswana!
Sadly compared to other Botswana camps we have visited the wildlife sightings were few and far between. We saw far less here during our 3 night stay than at either of the other 2 camps we stayed at. So lots of driving around and relatively little to show for it. This was compounded by the fact that our guide (Patrick) wasn't initially asking us about our interests or involving us in discussing different areas we could go on our drives and what we might hope to see. I did raise this matter with Patrick after a disappointing day of drives and this did improve the situation and mean that we felt able to make the best of what opportunities there were. I don't know the exact reason why the wildlife sightings were disappointing - Obviously 4 Rivers is a new camp and it may be the local wildlife is not yet used to the game drive vehicles. Another factor could have been that our guide, Patrick, was new to this camp, although experienced at moving around within Kwando.  We also had a bit of a hiccup with organising activities, where it looked like it would not be possible for all us guests to do our chosen activities, but the manager did end up finding a solution that worked for all of us.

placeInfo.name: 4 Rivers Camp - Kwando Safaris
placeInfo.address: Kwara Private Concession Okavango Delta, Maun, Ngamiland East Botswana

title: Diverse scenery and strong wildlife viewing
text:
Spending time in Moremi Game Reserve was a highlight of our trip. The combination of woodland, floodplains, and waterways makes it visually diverse and interesting to explore.

Wildlife sightings felt natural and unforced, and each drive brought something new.

A rewarding and scenic reserve to visit.

title: Quite a lot of animals
text:
The Moremi Game Reserve is a nearly 5.00km2 natural park in the eastern area of the Okavango Delta. It is the oldest national park in Botswana and is considered one of the most beautiful and best nature reserves in Africa, as there are large amounts of animals. We found the rides good to very good and also saw quite a lot of animals. However, overall we liked the Khwai Community Concession even better, because there was more water there due to the Okavango and also more animals were to be seen.
Admission costs 270 BWP per person plus 75 BWP per car and you pay either at the North entrance or at the South Gate.

title: End of the Moreni Park due to drought and hunger of elephants
text: Compared to 10 years ago, a lot has changed. The access road from Maun, once a bad gravel road, has now become an almost impassable dirt track. It took you 1.5 hours over the 84 km, now 3.5 hours. The paperwork at the South Gate has been simplified a bit. The beautiful forest near the South Gate has been completely destroyed in a matter of months by a large group of elephants, now barren, barren and without game. The prolonged drought has meant that you have to go far into the park, to an arm of the river where there is still some water. There you will find hippos, elephants and other game. There are no larger herds of zebras left. We did see a number of groups of giraffes. Too bad, but this park seems doomed.

title: Animals, animals, animals.
text: Absolutely wonderful. Animals, animals and more animals. Did a mobile camping safari with a local guide from Botswana who looked after us really well. The photos say everything, can't wait to go again. AND added bonus that Botswana is one of the safest African countries.

title: Most popular destination in the Okavango Delta
text:
It is one of the most popular destinations in the Okavango Delta and is the only (officially) protected area in the Delta, established in 1963, named after the Batawana chief Moremi III, and occupies approximately 5000 square kilometres. So with very abundant fauna. It has well-groomed and well-maintained trails, covers a third of the Okavango Delta and has dry terrain that crops out between marshy terrain. Within the area there are islands, including Chief's Island which can be reached by mokoro, a boat originally carved from an ebony trunk (now in fiberglass but wood-coloured, which doesn't clash...)
They have recently reintroduced rhinos (which were almost extinct here) and now Moremi is home to all the big five: lions, leopards, buffalo, elephants and rhinos. Difficult to see but there are also wild dogs, which are also protected because they are in danger of extinction.

title: Love it.
text: Love the wildlife and you can have sightings all by your self. I did a self drive and all camps are wild.

title: Beautiful moremi
text: One of the best things I have experienced was the safari trip I did to the moremi game reserve I highly recommend everyone visits this place once in their life

title: "Smaller park" with great animal observations
text: The park is about 2/3 the size of Etosha Park, but offers significantly more vegetation and many natural watering holes. We saw significantly more animals, especially herds of elephants and zebras, lions, giraffes, a leopard and much more. The animals were easy to photograph and observe. A real highlight.

title: This reserve comes out of my eyes
text:
Let’s not be afraid of being iconoclastic!
Ok nice things. But rutted tracks and/or deep sand require constant attention: the driver quickly gets tired, to say the least, and sees nothing.
Animals: yes, but not so much.
It’s very nice to say that we made Moreau….

title: My Africa
text:
Those who go in search of the "real" Africa will find their ideal environment here.
Compared to Kenyan, Tanzanian parks, etc., the fundamental difference is: here men are the guests, animals are the masters and we must adapt to their habits.
We faced this adventure by staying for a week in tented camps (a somewhat harsh but absolutely fascinating experience), going every day in search of animals.
“Open” (but absolutely safe) tented camps, which during the night were visited by elephants, hippos and felines in general, making the stay even more authentic.
Sometimes finding them immediately, others after a long search.
In this way we saw extraordinary landscapes in their ordinariness, waiting for the great rains that will completely change the landscapes themselves, but in the end literally finding ourselves face to face with all the possible inhabitants of the area (the rare leopards, lions, elephants, hippos, zebras, giraffes, and an infinite number of birds).
Moremi is the classic case which, at the end of the stay, causes the much famous "Africa sickness".
Absolutely worth visiting.

title: THIS IS THE EXPERIENCE OF A LIFETINE..DO NOT MISS IT
text: Moremi is unique. Nowhere else can you experience nature in its purest state. Not for the inexperienced. You need to plan every minute detail. There are no shops, no fuel, no mobile signal, no roads to speak of. A 4 x 4 vehicle is absolutely essential. Navigation can only be done using coordinates. A good satnav is vital. Wild animals WILL intrude in your campsite, including elephants, lions, hippos and hyenas. Be careful. The first night that I was in camp, a camper in another site was bitten by a hyena. Most of the campsites are remote from one another, secluded and private. Ablution blocks are good and clean. Hot water from solar power is available. The camp staff are unbelievably friendly and helpful Talk to them, they can give you much needed advice. After dark, it is dangerous to walk to the ablutions, if you have to go, drive there. all food and drinking water must be brought with you. Once in the Moremi, there are absolutely no supplies. Expect to average 6 to 10 mph when you travel. The so called roads are dreadful. It is rough and tough out there, but it is absolutely phenomenal, not to be missed. There are several companies offering fully equipped 4 x 4 vehicles for hire. Most of them are equipped with long-range fuel tanks, fresh water tanks, and fridge/freezers as well as being equipped with tents, bedding, pots and pans, crockery and cutlery. There will always be extra equipment that you need to buy, including plastic containers for dry goods, butter, milk and other items. Glass bottles tend to get smashed on the rough roads and dry goods packets tend to burst, leaving a huge mess. PREPARATION is essential, think everything through carefully. Make sure that you have everything that you need for the duration of your stay, you won't get a second chance. Make sure that you have a very powerful torch, to survey the area around your camp at night. If there is anything there, you will see their eyes and you can retreat to safety. Make sure that you have enough batteries. IT IS THE EXPERIENCE OF A LIFETIME, don't miss it. You will need to book a year in advance.

title: Excellent animal viewing no matter what time of year you go!!
text: We were actually at Moremi  in May, at the end of their dry season before the rains come.  We thought it might be easier to see the animals when there were fewer places for water, but our guide told us that regardless of the time of year, there is plenty of animals to see...and boy, was he right!  We were there for three days, so we had a total of 6 drives and on each drive we had an amazing experience.  From early morning lion kills of a zebra to the late afternoon elephant family playing with their kids in the mud, the viewing was some of the best we've ever had.  Definitely will be going back!!

title: Nothing like it!  Go now!
text: The Moremi is one of the special places on earth.  The largest inland delta in the world never disappoints!  The flora and fauna are indescribable!  Enjoy!

title: Excellent combination of both water and land
text: This game reserve is worth the visit - you can see both land animals such as elephants and antelope as well as water animals such as crocs and hippos.

title: un game-drive d'une très grand qualité

title: Moremi Game Reserve, Botswana.
text: Unfortunately we on spent one night at Khwai Camp on our way from Savuti to Maun. Khwai campsite has large treed sites and the ablution facilities are in excellent condition. A representaive from SKL, who manage the campsite came and spoke to us and informed us of the game drives within the area and what we were likely to see. A small herd of Elephant passed close to the camp and we were visited by a Spotted Hyena during the evening.

title: Amazing reserve where elephants play daily in the River
text: We wrapped up our 10 day vacation in Botswana at the Serondela Reserve in Chobe and what a way to wrap up!  The Serondela Reserve put on its best show for us and we loved every moment of it....lions with their young, elephants playing in the river and boatrides past hippos cooling themselves in the shallow waters before turning around and warming themselves on the banks of the river.  All this and amazing sunsets too!  Definitely a highlight of our trip and we can't wait to return.

title: Superbe endroit

Philippe

title: I wish I could Gatekeep this but that would be Unfair to the Team and their Brilliance
text:
I did the overnight Bush camp (two full days of safari) - I booked very last minute, having booked the Mokoro cruise, but when I saw the guys packing up for the camp tour at the office site, they offered me a place for an extremely well price, that I couldn’t resist.
So ten minutes before departure, wearing only the clothes on my back, carrying only sunscreen and insect repellent, I jumped at the chance!
Carrying so little was hardly noticeable since Timmy and his team accommodated me in every way, from organising dental hygiene products, to making sure I was well clothed to stay toasty and warm all night.

The camp was aided by a three-man crew: Tshapeng was our guide, and I’d like to think of him as a friend now. He was so kind, knowledgeable, I didn’t just learn about the animals, but also the plants, the people that have lived on this land since ancient times through to current plights.
I learnt so much about tracking animals, I really felt in the thick of the experience with him.
The chef accommodated all my strange requests, and he did so with passion. Food made in such an environment with so much enthusiasm tastes pure. He was so kind and knowledgeable.
Finally the preparation of the camp: I haven’t camped since 2011, I’ve always hated it and avoided it, but I think that’s because I was in festivals and not camping in the bush.
It was so much fun helping assemble the tents (voluntarily, I wanted to help but if it’s not your thing it’s okay too), the fire beneath the full moon that lit up the entire night sky. The canvas washroom was much better than even the gate toilets.
Finally, the night time Lion roars. It was everything I wanted it to be. Words cannot describe how incredible it is to be in this moment.
If there is one thing you do on the entire continent of Africa - it’s this.
If I wasn’t leaving Maun so soon I would 100 percent book again only with Timmy and his crew. In fact I wish I could stay a bit longer just to have this experience again.

I will be returning with my family/friends, I know these memories are going to be too strong and pull me back to Botswana.

title: Excellent safari tours from this company
text:
We had a boat trip with Temogo and our guide was excellent and very knowledgeable, showing us many interesting things, we were told not to be disappointed if we didn't see many if any animals as obviously they can't guarantee there will be any, but we were in luck first of all we saw quite a few elephants by the river which was a glorious sight and then further down a crocodile we then saw cows and horses by the river, then we saw a Hippo and were able to get some very good pictures before it went into the water. We were also taken to a few other places and shown some bird life. I can thoroughly recommend the boat trip.
The next trip we had with them about 1 week later was a game drive to Moremi, now this was the big one, we were collected at 6am by our guide (Charles) who was really friendly and again we were told not to expect to see Lions and other animals as it depends on where they are at the time. As this was winter time in Botswana it was cold in the morning and our journey there would be very cold in a open vehicle but they provided blankets for us.
Before we even got to Moremi we were greeted by such lovely sights as Giraffes, Elephants and Springbok.
Once we got through the gates at Moremi we were treated to even more of the same animals as well as Zebras, Hippos, Crocodiles, Antelope, amazing bird life and even Botswanas famous sausage tree.
Throughout this our guide was excellent and would stop for the wildlife if we saw it before him although this wasn't very often as he spotted animals way before us and he stopped so as to let the animals cross in front of us such as Elephants and Giraffes which enabled us to get some great pictures.
At lunchtime we were able to get out of the game vehicle and have lunch by a lake that was surrounded by wildlife (on the other side) so were we eating whilst watching Hippos, springbok and Zebras by the water.
Before lunch we had seen 2 female lions and our guide got us very close to them, they had recently killed and had eaten so they were content with us being near them, so content that one of them got up and went to the lake for a drink, our guide followed her and again we got some amazing pics.
All of the guides there from other companies talk to each other and let them know what they've seen and after lunch our guide was talking to another one he found out that a male lion was nearby and resting after having eaten, it turned out it was in the same spot the female Lions were in, just as we saw the male lion the guide reversed and took us near the river, when we enquired why he'd done this he showed us this big Croc that was heading to the river so he parked in front of it and the Croc just lay flat and again we were able to take some amazing pics, after that we headed back to the male Lion to get some excellent pics and at one point the Lion looked at us but because he'd eaten he wasn't bothered by us. Our trip was drawing to an end but even as we were leaving we still saw plenty of Giraffes, Elephants and Springbok.
This is a long trip (ours was nearly 12 hours) and for most of that you are sitting in a game viewer and driving over very bumpy un made roads and you will be a bit sore at the end of the day but the sights you will see are totally worth it and with our guide the day was made totally enjoyable.
